attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS Menu Management Module

attributes.name: modifier_group_id
attributes.description: Reference to the modifier group to which this modifier option belongs (e.g., 'Cheese Options', 'Bun Type', 'Size'). Establishes the parent-child grouping used by Oracle MICROS POS for menu customization display.
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS Menu Management Module

attributes.name: stock_item_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_item. Business justification: Modifiers (extra cheese, bacon add-on, sauce swap) consume specific stock items. This FK enables modifier-level inventory depletion, food cost tracking per modifier, and automatic OLO/POS suppression

attributes.name: allergen_flags
attributes.description: Pipe-delimited list of major food allergens introduced or removed by this modifier, per FDA's 9 major allergens (milk, eggs, fish, shellfish, tree_nuts, peanuts, wheat, soybeans, sesame). Example: 'milk|wheat'. Critical for guest safety, HACCP compliance, and allergen declaration on digital ordering platforms. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: milk|eggs|fish|shellfish|tree_nuts|peanuts|wheat|soybeans|sesame — promote to reference product]
attributes.references: FDA Food Allergen Labeling and Consumer Protection Act (FALCPA); FDA FSMA; ISO 22000 Food Safety Management

attributes.name: approved_by
attributes.description: Name or employee identifier of the menu engineering or culinary team member who approved this modifier for inclusion in the menu catalog. Supports governance, audit trail, and change management for menu updates.
attributes.references: Internal Menu Governance Policy; ISO 22000 Food Safety Management

attributes.name: approved_date
attributes.description: The date on which this modifier was formally approved by the menu engineering team for deployment to production POS and digital ordering systems.
attributes.references: Internal Menu Governance Policy; ISO 22000 Food Safety Management

attributes.name: available_channels
attributes.description: Pipe-delimited list of ordering channels where this modifier is available (e.g., 'dine_in|drive_thru|olo|3pd'). Supports channel-specific menu configuration for DT (Drive-Thru), OLO (Online Ordering), and 3PD (Third-Party Delivery) platforms. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: dine_in|drive_thru|olo|3pd|kiosk|catering — promote to reference product]
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS Menu Management Module; Olo Digital Ordering Platform

attributes.name: available_dayparts
attributes.description: Pipe-delimited list of dayparts during which this modifier is available (e.g., 'breakfast|lunch|dinner|late_night'). Enables daypart-specific menu engineering and ensures modifiers are only offered during operationally appropriate time windows.
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS Menu Management Module; NRA Daypart Standards

attributes.name: calorie_delta
attributes.description: The incremental calorie change (in kilocalories) added to or subtracted from the base menu item's calorie count when this modifier is selected. Positive for additions, negative for removals. Required for FDA menu labeling compliance and nutritional transparency on digital ordering channels.
attributes.references: FDA Menu Labeling Rule (21 CFR Part 101); FDA Food Safety Modernization Act (FSMA)

attributes.name: carbohydrate_delta_g
attributes.description: Incremental change in total carbohydrate content (in grams) when this modifier is applied. Required for FDA nutritional labeling and supports guest dietary needs (e.g., low-carb, diabetic-friendly options).
attributes.references: FDA Menu Labeling Rule (21 CFR Part 101); USDA Nutritional Guidelines

attributes.name: cogs_delta
attributes.business_glossary_term: Modifier Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) Delta
attributes.description: The incremental raw ingredient cost (COGS) associated with applying this modifier, expressed in the local currency. Used for menu engineering profitability analysis, CoGS% (Cost of Goods Sold Percentage) calculations, and P&L (Profit and Loss) reporting at the modifier level.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA CO Module; GAAP/IFRS Cost Accounting; Internal Menu Engineering Policy

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: The timestamp when this modifier record was first created in the menu management system. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and Silver Layer ingestion tracking in the Databricks Lakehouse.
attributes.references: Internal Data Governance Standard; GAAP/IFRS Audit Trail Requirements

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to the price_delta (e.g., 'USD', 'CAD', 'GBP'). Supports multi-currency operations across international franchise markets.
attributes.references: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; GAAP/IFRS Financial Reporting Standards

attributes.name: effective_end_date
attributes.description: The date after which this modifier record is no longer valid in the menu catalog. Null for currently active modifiers. Enables historical menu reconstruction for PMIX analysis, audit, and regulatory reporting.
attributes.references: Internal Menu Engineering Policy; GAAP/IFRS Audit Trail Requirements

attributes.name: effective_start_date
attributes.description: The date from which this modifier record is valid and active in the menu catalog. Supports temporal menu versioning, NRO (New Restaurant Opening) menu rollouts, and historical PMIX (Product Mix) analysis.
attributes.references: Internal Menu Engineering Policy; Oracle MICROS POS Menu Management Module

attributes.name: fat_delta_g
attributes.description: Incremental change in total fat content (in grams) when this modifier is applied to the base menu item. Required for FDA nutritional labeling compliance on menus and digital ordering platforms.
attributes.references: FDA Menu Labeling Rule (21 CFR Part 101); USDA Nutritional Guidelines

attributes.name: image_url
attributes.description: URL pointing to the digital image asset for this modifier, used on kiosk displays, OLO (Online Ordering) platforms, and 3PD (Third-Party Delivery) integrations to visually represent the modifier option to guests.
attributes.references: Olo Digital Ordering Platform; Internal Digital Asset Management Policy

attributes.name: is_allergen_free
attributes.description: Indicates whether this modifier introduces zero major allergens (True) or introduces at least one allergen (False). Used for rapid allergen filtering on digital ordering platforms and guest-facing allergen transparency tools.
attributes.references: FDA Food Allergen Labeling and Consumer Protection Act (FALCPA); ISO 22000

attributes.name: is_available
attributes.description: Real-time availability flag indicating whether this modifier is currently orderable (True) or temporarily unavailable due to ingredient stockout, 86'd item, or operational hold (False). Distinct from status, which reflects the lifecycle state; this flag reflects operational availability at a point in time.
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS Menu Management Module; MarketMan Inventory Management

attributes.name: is_default
attributes.description: Indicates whether this modifier is pre-selected by default when a guest orders the parent menu item (True) or must be explicitly chosen (False). Controls default state in Oracle MICROS POS and Olo Digital Ordering Platform order flows.
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS Menu Management Module; Olo Digital Ordering Platform

attributes.name: is_lto
attributes.business_glossary_term: Modifier Is Limited Time Offer (LTO) Flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this modifier is part of a Limited Time Offer (LTO) promotion (True) or is a permanent menu option (False). LTO modifiers are subject to promotional campaign timelines and require coordinated activation/deactivation across all channels.
attributes.references: Internal Menu Engineering Policy; NRA Industry Standards

attributes.name: is_required
attributes.description: Indicates whether a selection from this modifier's group is mandatory before the order can be completed (True) or optional (False). For example, selecting a size may be required for a beverage item. Enforced at POS and digital ordering checkout.
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS Menu Management Module; Olo Digital Ordering Platform

attributes.name: lto_end_date
attributes.description: The date on which this modifier's LTO availability expires. Null for permanent modifiers. Triggers automatic deactivation in POS and digital ordering systems upon expiry.
attributes.references: Internal Menu Engineering Policy; Marketing Campaign Management

attributes.name: lto_start_date
attributes.description: The date on which this modifier becomes available as part of a Limited Time Offer (LTO) campaign. Null for permanent modifiers. Used to schedule activation in Oracle MICROS POS and Olo Digital Ordering Platform.
attributes.references: Internal Menu Engineering Policy; Marketing Campaign Management

attributes.name: max_quantity
attributes.description: Maximum number of times this modifier can be applied to a single menu item in one order (e.g., a guest can add up to 3 extra cheese slices). Enforced at POS and digital ordering checkout to control portion and cost.
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS Menu Management Module; Internal Operations SOP

attributes.name: menu_modifier_description
attributes.description: Detailed description of the modifier option for internal documentation, menu engineering analysis, and digital channel display (e.g., online ordering, third-party delivery apps).
attributes.references: Olo Digital Ordering Platform; NRA Industry Standards

attributes.name: menu_modifier_name
attributes.description: Human-readable display name of the modifier option as it appears on POS terminals, KDS (Kitchen Display System), and digital ordering channels (e.g., 'Extra Cheese', 'No Onions', 'Gluten-Free Bun', 'Large Size').
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS Menu Management Module; Olo Digital Ordering Platform

attributes.name: menu_modifier_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the modifier option. 'active' indicates available for ordering; 'inactive' is temporarily unavailable; 'discontinued' is permanently removed; 'pending_approval' is awaiting menu engineering sign-off; 'seasonal' is available only during specific periods.
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS Menu Management Module; Internal Menu Governance Policy

attributes.name: modifier_type
attributes.description: Categorical classification of the modifier's operational intent. 'addition' adds an ingredient (e.g., Extra Cheese), 'removal' removes an ingredient (e.g., No Onions), 'substitution' replaces an ingredient (e.g., Gluten-Free Bun), 'size_upsize'/'size_downsize' changes portion size, 'preparation' specifies cooking instruction (e.g., Well Done). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: addition|removal|substitution|size_upsize|size_downsize|preparation — promote to reference product]
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS Menu Management Module; NRA Industry Standards

attributes.name: plu_code
attributes.description: Price Look-Up code assigned to this modifier in Oracle MICROS POS for transaction processing and sales reporting. Used for PMIX (Product Mix) reporting and COGS (Cost of Goods Sold) tracking at the modifier level.
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS Menu Management Module; NRA Industry Standards

attributes.name: pos_button_color
attributes.description: Hexadecimal color code for the modifier's button on Oracle MICROS POS touchscreen terminals. Used to visually differentiate modifier categories (e.g., additions in green, removals in red) to improve FOH (Front of House) order accuracy and speed of service (SOS).
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS Menu Management Module; Internal FOH SOP

attributes.name: prep_instruction
attributes.description: Specific BOH (Back of House) preparation instruction associated with this modifier, transmitted to the KDS (Kitchen Display System) when the modifier is selected (e.g., 'Grill well done', 'Toast bun', 'Serve sauce on side'). Supports SOP (Standard Operating Procedure) compliance in kitchen operations.
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS KDS Integration; Internal BOH SOP

attributes.name: price_delta
attributes.description: The incremental price adjustment applied to the base menu item price when this modifier is selected. Positive values represent upcharges (e.g., +1.50 for Extra Cheese); negative values represent discounts or removals (e.g., -0.50 for No Protein). Zero indicates no price change. Used by Oracle MICROS POS for real-time transaction pricing.
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS Menu Management Module; GAAP/IFRS Revenue Recognition

attributes.name: protein_delta_g
attributes.description: Incremental change in protein content (in grams) when this modifier is applied. Supports FDA nutritional labeling requirements and enables protein-focused menu analytics for health-oriented menu engineering.
attributes.references: FDA Menu Labeling Rule (21 CFR Part 101); USDA Nutritional Guidelines

attributes.name: restaurant_format
attributes.description: Identifies the restaurant format(s) for which this modifier is applicable: QSR (Quick-Service Restaurant), casual dining, fine dining, or all formats. Supports format-specific menu configuration across the franchise and company-owned portfolio.
attributes.references: Internal Menu Engineering Policy; FranConnect Franchise Management

attributes.name: short_name
attributes.description: Abbreviated display name used on KDS (Kitchen Display System) screens and printed receipts where space is constrained (e.g., 'XTR CHSE', 'NO ONI', 'GF BUN').
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS Menu Management Module

attributes.name: sku
attributes.description: SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) code linking this modifier to the inventory and supply chain systems (MarketMan Inventory Management, SAP S/4HANA MM module) for ingredient-level cost tracking and PAR Level management.
attributes.references: MarketMan Inventory Management; SAP S/4HANA MM Module

attributes.name: sodium_delta_mg
attributes.description: Incremental change in sodium content (in milligrams) when this modifier is applied. Critical for FDA menu labeling compliance and guest dietary transparency, particularly for health-conscious consumers and those managing sodium intake.
attributes.references: FDA Menu Labeling Rule (21 CFR Part 101); USDA Dietary Guidelines

attributes.name: sort_order
attributes.description: Numeric sequence controlling the display order of this modifier within its modifier group on POS terminals, KDS screens, and digital ordering interfaces. Lower values appear first. Supports menu engineering decisions on modifier prominence.
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS Menu Management Module; Olo Digital Ordering Platform

attributes.name: source_system
attributes.description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this modifier record originated (e.g., 'micros_pos' for Oracle MICROS POS, 'olo' for Olo Digital Ordering Platform). Supports data lineage and master data management in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer.
attributes.references: Internal Data Governance Standard; Databricks Lakehouse Architecture

attributes.name: tax_category_code
attributes.description: Tax category code assigned to this modifier for POS tax calculation purposes. Certain modifiers (e.g., alcoholic additions, specific food categories) may carry different tax treatment than the base item. Integrates with SAP S/4HANA FI/CO for tax compliance.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI Module; GAAP/IFRS Tax Compliance; Local Tax Regulations

attributes.name: unavailability_reason
attributes.description: Reason code explaining why this modifier is currently unavailable (is_available = False). Supports operational reporting, supply chain issue tracking, and guest communication on digital ordering platforms.
attributes.references: MarketMan Inventory Management; Internal Operations SOP

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: The timestamp when this modifier record was most recently modified in the menu management system. Used for incremental data pipeline processing, change detection, and audit compliance in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer.
attributes.references: Internal Data Governance Standard; GAAP/IFRS Audit Trail Requirements

products.name: pmix_record
products.description: Transactional menu performance record capturing the sales volume, revenue contribution, and margin performance of each menu item at a restaurant for a given reporting period (day/week/daypart). Tracks units sold, gross sales, net sales, discount amount, void count, contribution margin amount, contribution margin percentage, rank within category, daypart breakdown, channel breakdown, and menu engineering classification (star/plow-horse/puzzle/dog). References item_cost for theoretical COGS comparison but does NOT own cost data. Core input for menu engineering reviews, pricing optimization, item lifecycle decisions, and LTO performance evaluation.

attributes.name: pmix_record_id
attributes.description: Unique surrogate identifier for each product mix (PMIX) record in the Silver layer. Serves as the primary key for this transactional reporting entity.

attributes.name: campaign_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Campaign performance reporting: marketing analysts run product-mix reports segmented by campaign to measure campaign-driven sales lift and menu_mix_pct shifts. pmix_record already has promotion_id but

attributes.name: financial_period_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the fiscal period (week, period, or quarter) to which this PMIX record belongs, aligned to the restaurant's internal fiscal calendar. Supports period-over-period financial reporting.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA — CO Fiscal Calendar; GAAP/IFRS

attributes.name: franchisee_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to franchise.franchisee. Business justification: Franchise menu compliance and performance reporting requires pmix data aggregated by franchisee. Franchisors track product mix by franchisee to enforce brand standards, identify menu deviations, and f

attributes.name: menu_item_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the menu item whose sales performance is captured in this PMIX record. Links to the menu item master entity.
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS — Menu Item Master

attributes.name: pos_terminal_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to restaurant.pos_terminal. Business justification: Product mix data originates from POS terminal transactions. Terminal-level sales audit, POS reconciliation, and identifying terminal-specific anomalies (voids, comps) are named operational processes.

attributes.name: promotion_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the active marketing promotion or campaign associated with this menu item during the reporting period, if applicable. Links to the promotion master entity for campaign performance attribution.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM — Campaign Management; Oracle MICROS POS — Promotion Configuration

attributes.name: segment_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to guest.guest_segment. Business justification: Segment-level product mix analysis is a core menu engineering report — operators need to know which items sell to which guest segments to drive targeted promotions and menu optimization. A foodservice

attributes.name: site_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the restaurant location for which this PMIX record was generated. Links to the restaurant master entity.

attributes.name: unit_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the restaurant location for which this PMIX record was generated. Links to the restaurant master entity.

attributes.name: avg_selling_price
attributes.description: Average net selling price per unit of the menu item during the reporting period, calculated as net sales divided by units sold. Reflects actual realized price after discounts and may differ from the menu list price.
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS — Price Analysis; SAP S/4HANA — Revenue Accounting

attributes.name: category_rank
attributes.description: Rank of the menu item within its menu category at the restaurant for the reporting period, ordered by units sold descending. Used to identify top and bottom performers within each category for menu rationalization decisions.
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS — PMIX Category Ranking; NRA Menu Engineering Standards

attributes.name: cogs_amount
attributes.description: Total Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) for the menu item during the reporting period, calculated as units sold multiplied by the item's standard recipe cost. Core input for menu engineering profitability analysis.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA — CO Cost Accounting; MarketMan Inventory Management — Recipe Costing; GAAP/IFRS

attributes.name: cogs_pct
attributes.description: Cost of Goods Sold as a percentage of net sales (CoGS%) for the menu item during the reporting period. A key menu engineering metric used to classify items and assess profitability relative to category benchmarks.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA — CO Profitability Analysis; NRA Menu Engineering Standards; GAAP/IFRS

attributes.name: comp_amount
attributes.description: Total monetary value of complimentary (comped) menu items given during the reporting period. Represents a direct cost to the restaurant and is tracked for P&L accuracy and loss prevention.
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS — Comp Tracking; SAP S/4HANA — Cost Accounting; GAAP/IFRS

attributes.name: comp_count
attributes.description: Number of complimentary (comped) units of this menu item given to guests during the reporting period (e.g., for service recovery, employee meals, or promotional giveaways). Tracked for cost control and loss prevention.
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS — Comp Tracking; NRA Operational Standards

attributes.name: contribution_margin_amount
attributes.description: Contribution margin for the menu item during the reporting period, calculated as net sales minus COGS. Represents the dollar contribution of the item toward covering fixed costs and generating profit. Core metric in menu engineering star/plow-horse/puzzle/dog classification.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA — CO Contribution Margin Analysis; NRA Menu Engineering Standards; GAAP/IFRS

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this PMIX record was first created in the Silver layer data product. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SLA monitoring for data pipeline freshness.
attributes.references: Internal Data Governance Standard; GAAP/IFRS Audit Trail Requirements

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts in this PMIX record (e.g., USD, CAD, GBP). Supports multi-currency reporting for international restaurant operations.
attributes.references: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA — Currency Configuration; GAAP/IFRS

attributes.name: daypart
attributes.description: Daypart segment during which the menu item sales are recorded (e.g., breakfast, lunch, dinner). Daypart is a core foodservice industry segmentation concept used for menu engineering, labor scheduling, and promotional analysis.
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS — Daypart Configuration; NRA Daypart Standards

attributes.name: discount_amount
attributes.description: Total value of discounts, promotional reductions, and coupon redemptions applied to the menu item during the reporting period. Used to reconcile gross to net sales and evaluate promotional effectiveness.
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS — Discount Tracking; SAP S/4HANA — Revenue Deductions; GAAP/IFRS

attributes.name: gross_sales_amount
attributes.description: Total gross sales revenue generated by the menu item during the reporting period, before discounts, voids, and comps. Expressed in local currency. Core component of the MONETARY_TRIPLET for revenue reporting.
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS — Sales Summary; SAP S/4HANA — Revenue Accounting; GAAP/IFRS Revenue Recognition

attributes.name: is_available
attributes.description: Indicates whether the menu item was available for sale during the entire reporting period at the restaurant. A value of False may indicate a stock-out, 86'd item, or temporary removal from the menu, which can suppress PMIX figures.
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS — Item Availability; MarketMan Inventory Management — Stock Status

attributes.name: is_lto
attributes.description: Indicates whether the menu item is a Limited Time Offer (LTO) during the reporting period. LTO items require separate PMIX tracking to evaluate promotional performance and inform future LTO decisions.
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS — Menu Item Attributes; NRA Menu Engineering Standards

attributes.name: menu_category
attributes.description: The menu category to which the item belongs (e.g., Burgers, Beverages, Sides, Desserts, LTO). Used for category-level PMIX analysis and menu engineering decisions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: entree|beverage|side|dessert|appetizer|lto|combo|condiment — promote to reference product]
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS — Menu Category Master; NRA Menu Engineering Standards

attributes.name: menu_engineering_classification
attributes.description: Menu engineering quadrant classification of the item for the reporting period based on the Boston Consulting Group (BCG) matrix adapted for foodservice: Star (high popularity, high margin), Plow Horse (high popularity, low margin), Puzzle (low popularity, high margin), Dog (low popularity, low margin). Core output of menu engineering analysis.
attributes.references: NRA Menu Engineering Standards; Kasavana & Smith Menu Engineering Methodology

attributes.name: menu_list_price
attributes.description: The standard listed menu price for the item at the restaurant during the reporting period. Used to calculate price realization (average selling price vs. list price) and assess discount impact.
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS — Menu Price Configuration; FDA Menu Labeling Requirements

attributes.name: menu_mix_pct
attributes.description: The percentage of total units sold at the restaurant during the reporting period that this menu item represents. Core Product Mix (PMIX) metric used to identify high-velocity and low-velocity items for menu engineering decisions.
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS — PMIX Report; NRA Menu Engineering Standards

attributes.name: net_sales_amount
attributes.description: Net sales revenue for the menu item after deducting discounts, voids, and comps from gross sales. The primary revenue figure used in P&L reporting, PMIX analysis, and AUV calculations.
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS — Net Sales; SAP S/4HANA — Net Revenue; GAAP/IFRS Revenue Recognition

attributes.name: overall_rank
attributes.description: Rank of the menu item across all menu items at the restaurant for the reporting period, ordered by units sold descending. Provides a holistic view of item velocity for menu engineering and operational prioritization.
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS — PMIX Overall Ranking; NRA Menu Engineering Standards

attributes.name: ownership_type
attributes.description: Indicates whether the restaurant is company-owned or franchised. Enables separate PMIX benchmarking and menu engineering analysis across ownership models, which is critical for franchise royalty and P&L reporting.
attributes.references: FranConnect — Franchise Classification; FTC Franchise Disclosure Regulations; IFA Standards

attributes.name: record_status
attributes.description: Lifecycle status of this PMIX record in the Silver layer. 'active' indicates the current valid record; 'revised' indicates the record has been superseded by a correction; 'voided' indicates the record was invalidated due to a data quality issue.
attributes.references: Internal Data Governance Standard; GAAP/IFRS Audit Trail Requirements

attributes.name: refund_amount
attributes.description: Total monetary value of refunds issued for this menu item during the reporting period. Used in net revenue reconciliation and guest satisfaction analysis.
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS — Refund Tracking; SAP S/4HANA — Revenue Adjustments; GAAP/IFRS

attributes.name: refund_count
attributes.description: Number of refunds issued for this menu item during the reporting period. Elevated refund counts may indicate quality issues, order accuracy problems, or guest dissatisfaction.
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS — Refund Tracking; PCI DSS — Refund Transaction Controls

attributes.name: reporting_date
attributes.description: Calendar date for which this PMIX record applies. Used for daily and weekly menu engineering analysis and comparable store sales (Comp Sales / SSS) reporting.
attributes.references: NRA Industry Reporting Standards; GAAP/IFRS Fiscal Period Alignment

attributes.name: reporting_period_type
attributes.description: Granularity of the PMIX record — whether it represents a daily, weekly, or daypart-level aggregation. Determines how units sold and revenue figures should be interpreted.

attributes.name: restaurant_format
attributes.description: The restaurant service format (Quick-Service Restaurant (QSR), casual, or fine-dining) associated with this PMIX record. Supports cross-format menu performance benchmarking.
attributes.references: FranConnect — Restaurant Format Classification; NRA Industry Standards

attributes.name: sales_channel
attributes.description: The ordering channel through which the menu item was sold (e.g., Drive-Thru (DT), Online Ordering (OLO), Third-Party Delivery (3PD), dine-in, kiosk). Enables channel-level PMIX analysis for menu engineering and pricing strategy.
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS; Olo Digital Ordering Platform; NRA Channel Reporting Standards

attributes.name: sales_mix_pct
attributes.description: The percentage of total net sales revenue at the restaurant during the reporting period attributable to this menu item. Distinct from menu mix percentage (unit-based); this is revenue-based mix used for revenue contribution analysis.
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS — Revenue Mix Report; SAP S/4HANA — Revenue Analysis; NRA Standards

attributes.name: sku_code
attributes.description: The Stock Keeping Unit (SKU) code assigned to the menu item in the POS and inventory management systems. Used to reconcile PMIX data with supply chain and inventory records in MarketMan.
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS — Item SKU; MarketMan Inventory Management — SKU Master

attributes.name: source_system
attributes.description: The operational system of record from which this PMIX record was sourced (e.g., Oracle MICROS POS, Olo Digital Ordering Platform, or third-party delivery aggregator). Supports data lineage and reconciliation across channels.
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS; Olo Digital Ordering Platform; Internal Data Governance Standard

attributes.name: unavailability_hours
attributes.description: Total number of hours during the reporting period that the menu item was unavailable for sale (e.g., due to stock-out or being 86'd). Used to adjust PMIX velocity calculations and identify supply chain or inventory issues.
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS — Item 86 Tracking; MarketMan Inventory Management

attributes.name: units_sold
attributes.description: Total number of individual menu item units sold during the reporting period at the restaurant. The primary volume metric in PMIX analysis and menu engineering.
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS — Sales Transaction Data; NRA PMIX Reporting Standards

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this PMIX record was last modified in the Silver layer, such as when a correction or revision was applied. Supports audit trail and change tracking for data governance.
attributes.references: Internal Data Governance Standard; GAAP/IFRS Audit Trail Requirements

attributes.name: void_amount
attributes.description: Total monetary value of voided transactions for this menu item during the reporting period. Used alongside void count for loss prevention analysis and operational quality monitoring.
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS — Void Tracking; SAP S/4HANA — Revenue Adjustments

attributes.name: void_count
attributes.description: Number of voided transactions for this menu item during the reporting period. Voids indicate order errors, customer changes, or operational issues and are tracked separately from discounts for loss prevention and operational quality analysis.
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS — Void Tracking; NRA Operational Standards

products.name: item_cost
products.description: Transactional record capturing the theoretical COGS (Cost of Goods Sold) for each menu item at a point in time, derived from recipe BOM and current ingredient purchase prices. Tracks theoretical food cost amount, theoretical CoGS%, actual CoGS% (from inventory management systems like MarketMan), variance amount, variance percentage, cost calculation date, ingredient price snapshot reference, and cost approval status. Updated when ingredient prices change (weekly/daily frequency) — distinct from pmix_record which captures periodic sales performance. Feeds menu engineering decisions, pricing optimization, P&L reporting, and franchise profitability benchmarking.
products.reference: Theoretical Food Cost / COGS per Item

attributes.name: item_cost_id
attributes.description: Unique surrogate identifier for each item cost record. Primary key for the item_cost data product in the menu domain.

attributes.name: approved_by_employee_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Item cost approval is a financial control process requiring a named employee (e.g., finance controller or menu engineering manager) for SOX compliance and food cost variance reporting in restaurant op

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Cost accounting assigns each menu item's calculated cost to a cost center for internal budgeting and variance analysis; essential for COGS reporting.

attributes.name: financial_period_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.financial_period. Business justification: item_cost has a plain-text 'fiscal_period' column (denormalized). Replacing with a proper FK to financial_period enables period-over-period food cost variance reporting (theoretical vs. actual COGS),

attributes.name: franchisee_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to franchise.franchisee. Business justification: Franchisee COGS benchmarking is a named franchise compliance process — franchisors compare theoretical vs actual item cost by franchisee to identify food cost deviations and trigger corrective action.

attributes.name: gl_account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: COGS posting requires mapping item cost records to specific GL accounts (raw material cost, packaging cost). Foodservice finance teams post theoretical and actual COGS to GL accounts for P&L reporting

attributes.name: menu_item_id
attributes.description: Reference to the menu item for which this theoretical cost record is calculated. Links to the menu.menu_item master product.

attributes.name: profit_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Profit center-level food cost variance reporting is a standard foodservice finance process — each restaurant unit maps to a profit center, and COGS must be attributed to profit centers for unit-level

attributes.name: recipe_id
attributes.description: Reference to the specific recipe version (Bill of Materials) used to derive the theoretical cost. Ensures cost traceability to the exact ingredient composition at time of calculation.

attributes.name: actual_cogs_pct
attributes.business_glossary_term: Actual Cost of Goods Sold Percentage (Actual CoGS%)
attributes.description: Actual realized Cost of Goods Sold percentage sourced from MarketMan Inventory Management, reflecting real purchase costs, waste, and yield losses. Compared against theoretical_cogs_pct to compute variance and identify operational inefficiencies.
attributes.references: MarketMan Inventory Management — Actual COGS Report; GAAP / IFRS

attributes.name: approved_date
attributes.description: The date on which this item cost record was approved by the authorized reviewer. Used for audit trail and to establish the effective start of this cost record in menu engineering and P&L reporting.
attributes.references: GAAP / IFRS — Internal Controls; SOX — Financial Reporting Controls

attributes.name: base_selling_price
attributes.description: The menu item's base selling price at the time of cost calculation, used as the denominator for CoGS% computation. Captured as a point-in-time snapshot to ensure historical cost percentage accuracy even when prices change.
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS — Menu Price Management; GAAP / IFRS

attributes.name: channel
attributes.description: The sales channel for which this cost record applies: dine-in, Drive-Thru (DT), Online Ordering (OLO), Third-Party Delivery (3PD), or all channels. Packaging and portion costs may differ by channel, resulting in channel-specific cost records.
attributes.references: Olo Digital Ordering Platform; Oracle MICROS POS — Channel Management

attributes.name: cogs_pct_variance
attributes.business_glossary_term: Cost of Goods Sold Percentage Variance (CoGS% Variance)
attributes.description: Difference between actual_cogs_pct and theoretical_cogs_pct (actual minus theoretical). Positive variance indicates actual costs exceeded theoretical expectations, signaling waste, theft, or pricing issues. Critical metric for P&L reporting and operational control.
attributes.references: GAAP / IFRS — Variance Analysis; NRA Food Cost Management Standards

attributes.name: cost_calculation_date
attributes.description: The business date on which the theoretical item cost was calculated. Used to track cost trends over time and align with menu engineering review cycles and P&L reporting periods.

attributes.name: cost_calculation_method
attributes.description: Method used to derive the item cost: 'theoretical_bom' = derived from recipe Bill of Materials and price snapshot; 'actual_marktman' = sourced directly from MarketMan actual cost data; 'blended' = weighted combination of theoretical and actual. Ensures analytical transparency.
attributes.references: MarketMan Inventory Management — Cost Calculation; Internal COGS Methodology SOP

attributes.name: cost_per_gram
attributes.description: Theoretical cost per gram of the menu item (theoretical_cost_amount / portion_size_grams). Enables normalized cost comparison across items of different portion sizes and supports portion engineering decisions.
attributes.references: Internal Menu Engineering SOP; NRA Menu Engineering Standards

attributes.name: cost_status
attributes.description: Lifecycle status of this item cost record. 'draft' indicates pending review; 'approved' indicates validated and active for menu engineering; 'superseded' indicates replaced by a newer calculation; 'archived' indicates retired from active use.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: The timestamp when this item cost record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Provides the audit creation trail for the Silver layer record.
attributes.references: Internal Data Governance Standard; GAAP / IFRS — Audit Trail Requirements

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the cost amounts are expressed (e.g., USD, CAD, GBP). Supports multi-currency operations across international franchise markets.

attributes.name: daypart
attributes.description: The daypart for which this cost record applies (breakfast, lunch, dinner, late-night, or all). Ingredient compositions and portion standards may vary by daypart, resulting in daypart-specific cost records for the same menu item.
attributes.references: Internal Menu Engineering SOP; Oracle MICROS POS — Daypart Configuration

attributes.name: effective_end_date
attributes.description: The date on which this item cost record ceases to be active, typically when superseded by a new calculation. Null indicates the record is currently active. Supports bi-temporal cost history and trend analysis.
attributes.references: GAAP / IFRS — Period Cost Recognition; Internal Menu Engineering SOP

attributes.name: effective_start_date
attributes.description: The date from which this item cost record is considered active and applicable for menu engineering, pricing, and P&L reporting. Supports bi-temporal modeling of cost history.
attributes.references: GAAP / IFRS — Period Cost Recognition; Internal Menu Engineering SOP

attributes.name: ingredient_count
attributes.description: Total number of distinct ingredients in the Bill of Materials (BOM) used for this cost calculation. Provides a quick indicator of recipe complexity and the number of price inputs contributing to the theoretical cost.

attributes.name: is_lto
attributes.description: Indicates whether this cost record applies to a Limited Time Offer (LTO) menu item. LTO items often have unique cost structures due to specialty ingredients and require separate cost tracking for promotional P&L analysis.
attributes.references: Internal LTO Management SOP; Oracle MICROS POS — LTO Configuration

attributes.name: marktman_cost_record_code
attributes.description: The native record identifier from MarketMan Inventory Management for the actual cost data used in this calculation. Enables direct traceability back to the source system record for audit and reconciliation purposes.
attributes.references: MarketMan Inventory Management — Cost Record API

attributes.name: menu_engineering_class
attributes.description: Menu engineering quadrant classification based on the item's profitability (CoGS%) and popularity (PMIX). 'star' = high popularity, high margin; 'plow_horse' = high popularity, low margin; 'puzzle' = low popularity, high margin; 'dog' = low popularity, low margin. Drives pricing and promotional strategy.
attributes.references: NRA — Menu Engineering Framework (Kasavana & Smith); Internal Menu Engineering SOP

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-text notes from the menu engineering or finance team explaining the rationale for this cost calculation, any manual adjustments made, or context for significant variances. Supports audit review and knowledge transfer.

attributes.name: packaging_cost
attributes.description: Cost contribution of packaging materials (containers, wrappers, bags, cups) to the total theoretical item cost. Tracked separately from food ingredients to support packaging procurement decisions and sustainability reporting.
attributes.references: Internal COGS Decomposition Standard; Coupa Procurement — Packaging Category

attributes.name: portion_size_grams
attributes.description: Standard portion size in grams for the menu item as defined in the recipe BOM at the time of cost calculation. Used to normalize cost-per-gram comparisons across menu items and to validate portion compliance with HACCP standards.
attributes.references: FDA — Nutrition Labeling and Education Act (NLEA); HACCP — Portion Control CCP

attributes.name: price_basis
attributes.description: The pricing basis applied to ingredient costs in this calculation: 'last_purchase' = most recent purchase price; 'moving_average' = weighted average of recent purchases; 'contract' = contracted supplier price from Coupa; 'standard' = standard cost set by finance. Affects cost comparability.
attributes.references: Coupa Procurement — Contract Pricing; SAP S/4HANA MM — Standard Cost; GAAP / IFRS — Inventory Valuation

attributes.name: price_snapshot_date
attributes.description: The effective date of the ingredient purchase prices used in this cost calculation. Distinct from cost_calculation_date — the snapshot may use prices from a prior period (e.g., last purchase price, rolling average). Critical for cost audit and variance explanation.
attributes.references: MarketMan Inventory Management — Price History; Coupa Procurement — Supplier Pricing

attributes.name: price_snapshot_reference
attributes.description: Reference to the ingredient price snapshot used in this cost calculation. Captures the specific point-in-time purchase prices from MarketMan that were applied to the Bill of Materials to derive theoretical Cost of Goods Sold (COGS).
attributes.references: MarketMan Inventory Management — Purchase Order / Price History

attributes.name: primary_protein_cost
attributes.description: Cost contribution of the primary protein ingredient(s) to the total theoretical item cost. Proteins (beef, chicken, seafood) are typically the highest-cost BOM component and are tracked separately for commodity price sensitivity analysis and USDA-aligned procurement reporting.
attributes.references: USDA — Meat and Poultry Grading; Internal COGS Decomposition Standard

attributes.name: recipe_version
attributes.description: Version identifier of the recipe / Bill of Materials (BOM) used in this cost calculation (e.g., 'v3.2'). Enables traceability of cost changes driven by recipe reformulations versus ingredient price movements.
attributes.references: Internal Menu Engineering SOP; ISO 22000 — Food Safety Management

attributes.name: restaurant_format
attributes.description: The restaurant format for which this cost record applies: Quick-Service Restaurant (QSR), casual dining, fine dining, or all formats. Cost structures differ by format due to labor, portion, and ingredient standards.
attributes.references: Internal Restaurant Operations Standard; NRA — Restaurant Format Classification

attributes.name: source_system
attributes.description: The operational system of record from which this cost record originated: MarketMan (actual cost data), SAP S/4HANA (standard cost), Oracle MICROS POS (price data), or manual entry. Supports data lineage and audit traceability in the Databricks Silver layer.
attributes.references: Internal Data Governance Standard; MarketMan Inventory Management; SAP S/4HANA

attributes.name: target_cogs_pct
attributes.business_glossary_term: Target Cost of Goods Sold Percentage (Target CoGS%)
attributes.description: The budgeted or target CoGS% for this menu item as set by the finance and menu engineering teams. Used as the benchmark against which theoretical_cogs_pct and actual_cogs_pct are evaluated. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.2800 = 28.00%).
attributes.references: Internal Financial Planning SOP; GAAP / IFRS — Budget vs Actual Reporting

attributes.name: target_variance_pct
attributes.description: Acceptable tolerance band for CoGS% variance (theoretical vs actual) before an alert or review is triggered. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0200 = ±2.00%). Supports automated exception reporting in menu engineering workflows.
attributes.references: Internal Menu Engineering SOP; GAAP / IFRS — Variance Tolerance Standards

attributes.name: theoretical_cogs_pct
attributes.business_glossary_term: Theoretical Cost of Goods Sold Percentage (CoGS%)
attributes.description: Theoretical Cost of Goods Sold expressed as a percentage of the menu item's base selling price (theoretical_cost_amount / base_price). Key menu engineering metric used to classify items and set pricing strategy. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.2850 = 28.50%).
attributes.references: NRA Menu Engineering Standards; GAAP / IFRS — Gross Margin Reporting

attributes.name: theoretical_cost_amount
attributes.business_glossary_term: Theoretical Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) Amount
attributes.description: The theoretical food cost amount in the operating currency for one unit of the menu item, derived by multiplying each Bill of Materials (BOM) ingredient quantity by its point-in-time purchase price. Core input to menu engineering and pricing decisions.
attributes.references: GAAP / IFRS — Cost of Goods Sold; NRA Menu Engineering Standards

attributes.name: theoretical_cost_variance_amount
attributes.description: Absolute monetary variance between actual cost and theoretical cost per unit (actual cost amount minus theoretical_cost_amount). Expressed in the operating currency. Used alongside cogs_pct_variance for financial impact quantification in P&L reporting.

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: The timestamp when this item cost record was last modified, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Tracks the most recent update for change management and audit trail purposes in the Databricks Silver layer.
attributes.references: Internal Data Governance Standard; GAAP / IFRS — Audit Trail Requirements

attributes.name: waste_pct
attributes.description: Theoretical food waste percentage factored into the cost calculation, representing expected yield loss during preparation (e.g., trim waste, cooking shrinkage). Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0500 = 5.00%). Sourced from recipe yield standards in MarketMan.
attributes.references: MarketMan Inventory Management — Waste Tracking; FDA — Food Waste Reduction Guidelines

attributes.name: yield_pct
attributes.description: Expected usable yield percentage of the primary ingredient after preparation (e.g., 0.8500 = 85% yield after trimming). Used in yield management calculations to adjust raw ingredient quantities in the BOM to achieve the target portion size.
attributes.references: Internal Recipe Engineering Standard; NRA — Yield Management Best Practices

products.name: combo_meal
products.description: Master record for bundled combo meal offerings — pre-defined combinations of menu items sold together at a bundle price (e.g., Value Meal, Family Pack, Kids Meal). Captures combo name, component items, bundle price, individual item price sum, bundle discount amount, applicable channels, daypart availability, and active status. Distinct from LTOs in that combos are permanent or semi-permanent menu fixtures.
products.reference: Combo Meal / Value Meal / Bundle Offer

attributes.name: combo_meal_id
attributes.description: Unique surrogate identifier for the combo meal record. Primary key for the combo_meal data product in the menu domain.

attributes.name: approved_by_employee_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Combo meal pricing and bundling approval is a menu engineering governance process. A named approving employee is required for audit trails in franchise systems where combo pricing deviations must be a

attributes.name: brand_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to restaurant.brand. Business justification: Combo meals are brand-defined products. Brand-level combo meal reporting, brand standard compliance for combo pricing, and national launch management (combo_meal.is_national_launch) all require direct

attributes.name: campaign_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Required for Combo Promotion: associate combo meals with marketing campaigns that promote them, supporting campaign budgeting, scheduling, and sales attribution.

attributes.name: financial_period_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.financial_period. Business justification: Combo meal performance reporting (pmix_target_pct, food_cost_pct, bundle_discount_amount) must be anchored to fiscal periods for budget vs. actual combo contribution analysis. Foodservice finance team

attributes.name: gl_account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Combo meals are sold as bundled SKUs requiring a dedicated revenue GL account for bundle sales recognition, separate from individual item GL accounts. Foodservice finance teams track combo revenue in

attributes.name: menu_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to menu.menu. Business justification: Combo meals are defined within a specific menu; adding menu_id ties the combo to its parent menu.

attributes.name: promotion_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to marketing.promotion. Business justification: Combo deal promotion tracking: combo meals are routinely tied to specific promotions (e.g., '$5 combo deal') that govern the bundle_discount_amount. Marketing and pricing teams must link each combo to

attributes.name: allergen_flags
attributes.description: Comma-separated list of major allergens present in the combo meal based on its default component items (e.g., 'milk,wheat,soy'). Supports FDA major food allergen labeling requirements and guest advisory communications. Detailed per-allergen declarations are managed in the allergen_declaration product.
attributes.references: FDA Food Allergen Labeling and Consumer Protection Act (FALCPA); ISO 22000

attributes.name: approved_date
attributes.description: Date on which the combo meal was formally approved for publication by the designated menu authority. Supports audit trail and menu governance compliance.

attributes.name: bundle_discount_amount
attributes.description: The monetary discount provided to the guest by purchasing the combo meal versus buying each component item individually (individual_items_price_sum minus bundle_price). Key metric for menu engineering and promotional value analysis.
attributes.references: GAAP Revenue Recognition; FTC Advertising Regulations

attributes.name: bundle_price
attributes.description: The retail selling price of the combo meal as a bundle, charged to the guest at POS or digital ordering channels. This is the price the guest pays for the full combo, distinct from the sum of individual item prices.
attributes.references: GAAP Revenue Recognition; Oracle MICROS POS Pricing

attributes.name: calorie_range_max
attributes.description: Maximum calorie count for the combo meal when customizable components are at their highest-calorie configuration. Required for FDA menu labeling compliance when combos offer substitution options.
attributes.references: FDA Menu Labeling Rule (21 CFR Part 101)

attributes.name: calorie_range_min
attributes.description: Minimum calorie count for the combo meal when customizable components are at their lowest-calorie configuration. Required for FDA menu labeling compliance when combos offer substitution options.
attributes.references: FDA Menu Labeling Rule (21 CFR Part 101)

attributes.name: combo_code
attributes.description: Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for the combo meal, used in Oracle MICROS POS, Olo Digital Ordering Platform, and 3PD integrations for menu lookup and order capture. Serves as the canonical cross-system reference code.

attributes.name: combo_description
attributes.description: Detailed description of the combo meal contents and value proposition, used for digital menu boards, Olo OLO platform, and 3PD (Third-Party Delivery) channel listings. Supports guest decision-making and marketing copy.

attributes.name: combo_name
attributes.description: Human-readable marketing name of the combo meal as displayed on menu boards, POS screens, and digital ordering channels (e.g., 'Classic Value Meal', 'Family Pack', 'Kids Meal'). Used for guest-facing display and reporting.

attributes.name: combo_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the combo meal offering. Controls whether the combo is available for sale across channels. 'test' indicates a market-test combo not yet in full rollout.

attributes.name: combo_type
attributes.description: Classification of the combo meal by bundle format. Drives menu engineering analysis, PMIX reporting, and promotional targeting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: value_meal|family_pack|kids_meal|party_pack|snack_combo|premium_combo — promote to reference product if additional types are needed]

attributes.name: country_code
attributes.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country where this combo meal is offered. Supports global menu management, regulatory compliance, and multi-currency pricing.
attributes.references: ISO 3166-1 Country Codes; GAAP/IFRS International Reporting

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the combo meal record was first created in the data platform. Follows ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports audit trail and data lineage in the Silver layer.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to all monetary fields on this combo meal record (e.g., USD, CAD, GBP). Supports multi-currency operations across global franchise markets.
attributes.references: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; GAAP/IFRS Financial Reporting

attributes.name: daypart
attributes.description: Daypart(s) during which this combo meal is available for sale. Daypart segmentation is a core foodservice operational concept used in PMIX analysis, labor scheduling, and revenue management.

attributes.name: discontinue_date
attributes.description: The date on which the combo meal was or is scheduled to be removed from the menu. Nullable for permanent menu fixtures. Used for menu lifecycle management and supply chain wind-down planning.

attributes.name: effective_end_date
attributes.description: The date on which the current version of the combo meal record ceases to be operationally effective. Nullable for open-ended records. Supports menu versioning and temporal pricing management.
attributes.references: Internal Menu Governance Policy; GAAP Revenue Recognition

attributes.name: effective_start_date
attributes.description: The date from which the current version of the combo meal record (including pricing, components, and availability) is operationally effective. Supports menu versioning and temporal pricing management.
attributes.references: Internal Menu Governance Policy; GAAP Revenue Recognition

attributes.name: food_cost_pct
attributes.description: Cost of Goods Sold as a percentage of the bundle price (item_cost / bundle_price * 100). Core menu engineering KPI used to classify combo meals and drive pricing decisions. Aligns with CoGS% jargon in foodservice P&L management.
attributes.references: GAAP COGS Accounting; NRA Financial Performance Standards

attributes.name: image_url
attributes.description: URL of the primary product image for the combo meal used on digital menu boards, Olo OLO platform, and 3PD channel listings. Must be a publicly accessible HTTPS URL.

attributes.name: individual_items_price_sum
attributes.description: The sum of the base prices of all individual component menu items if purchased separately. Used to calculate the bundle discount amount and communicate value to guests. Sourced from Oracle MICROS POS item pricing.
attributes.references: GAAP Revenue Recognition; FTC Advertising Regulations

attributes.name: is_3pd_available
attributes.description: Indicates whether this combo meal is available through Third-Party Delivery (3PD) partners (e.g., DoorDash, Uber Eats, Grubhub). Controls 3PD channel menu syndication via Olo integration.
attributes.references: Olo Digital Ordering Platform; Internal Channel Management Policy

attributes.name: is_customizable
attributes.description: Indicates whether guests can substitute or modify component items within the combo meal (e.g., swap side item, upsize drink). Drives modifier group display logic in Oracle MICROS POS and Olo.

attributes.name: is_dine_in_available
attributes.description: Indicates whether this combo meal is available for dine-in service. Relevant for casual and fine-dining formats where combo availability may differ by service mode.

attributes.name: is_dt_available
attributes.description: Indicates whether this combo meal is available for sale through the Drive-Thru (DT) channel. Controls channel-specific menu display in Oracle MICROS POS and KDS routing.

attributes.name: is_national_launch
attributes.description: Indicates whether this combo meal is part of a national rollout versus a regional or test-market offering. Drives marketing campaign scope, supply chain planning, and franchise communication.
attributes.references: Internal NRO (New Restaurant Opening) and Launch Policy

attributes.name: is_olo_available
attributes.description: Indicates whether this combo meal is available through the Olo Digital Ordering Platform (OLO) for web and mobile ordering. Controls digital menu availability.
attributes.references: Olo Digital Ordering Platform; Internal Channel Management Policy

attributes.name: is_taxable
attributes.description: Indicates whether the combo meal bundle price is subject to sales tax. Drives tax calculation logic in Oracle MICROS POS and SAP S/4HANA AR module. Tax applicability may vary by jurisdiction.
attributes.references: GAAP/IFRS Tax Compliance; SAP S/4HANA AR Module

attributes.name: item_cost
attributes.description: Total Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) for the combo meal, representing the combined food cost of all component items. Used to calculate COGS% and support P&L and menu engineering decisions. Sourced from MarketMan Inventory Management and SAP S/4HANA CO module.
attributes.references: GAAP COGS Accounting; SAP S/4HANA CO Module

attributes.name: launch_date
attributes.description: The date on which the combo meal became or is scheduled to become available for sale. Used for menu lifecycle management, supply chain lead time planning, and marketing campaign alignment.

attributes.name: menu_engineering_class
attributes.description: Menu engineering quadrant classification for the combo meal based on profitability and popularity: 'star' (high profit, high popularity), 'plow_horse' (low profit, high popularity), 'puzzle' (high profit, low popularity), 'dog' (low profit, low popularity). Core menu engineering decision-support field.
attributes.references: NRA Menu Engineering Standards; Kasavana & Smith Menu Engineering Model

attributes.name: olo_item_code
attributes.description: Olo Digital Ordering Platform external identifier for this combo meal. Used for menu synchronization between the internal menu master and the Olo platform for web, mobile, and 3PD channel ordering.

attributes.name: ownership_model
attributes.description: Indicates whether this combo meal is available at franchise-operated locations, company-owned locations, or both. Supports franchise compliance tracking via FranConnect and royalty reporting.
attributes.references: FranConnect Franchise Management; IFA Franchise Best Practices

attributes.name: pmix_target_pct
attributes.description: Target Product Mix (PMIX) percentage representing the expected share of total transactions this combo meal should represent within its category. Used for menu performance benchmarking and sales forecasting.
attributes.references: NRA Menu Engineering Standards; Internal PMIX Reporting

attributes.name: pos_item_code
attributes.description: Oracle MICROS POS-specific item code used to ring up the combo meal at the point of sale. This is the PLU/button code used by FOH (Front of House) staff and KDS (Kitchen Display System) routing.

attributes.name: region_code
attributes.description: Geographic region code where this combo meal is available (e.g., 'NORTHEAST', 'WEST', 'SOUTH'). Supports regional menu variation, pricing strategy, and PMIX analysis across the restaurant network.

attributes.name: restaurant_format
attributes.description: Restaurant format(s) for which this combo meal is applicable. QSR (Quick-Service Restaurant) combos differ structurally from casual or fine-dining bundles. Drives menu engineering and franchise compliance.

attributes.name: sort_order
attributes.description: Display sequence order for the combo meal on menu boards, POS screens, and digital ordering interfaces. Lower values appear first. Controls guest-facing menu presentation.

attributes.name: source_system
attributes.description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this combo meal record originated (e.g., Oracle MICROS POS, Olo Digital Ordering Platform). Supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver layer.
attributes.references: Internal Data Governance and Lineage Policy

attributes.name: tax_category_code
attributes.description: Tax category code assigned to the combo meal for sales tax calculation in Oracle MICROS POS and SAP S/4HANA. Determines applicable tax rates by jurisdiction and product type.
attributes.references: GAAP/IFRS Tax Compliance; SAP S/4HANA Tax Configuration

attributes.name: total_calories
attributes.description: Total calorie count for the combo meal as configured with default component items. Required for FDA menu labeling compliance on menus with 20+ locations. Displayed on menu boards and digital ordering platforms.
attributes.references: FDA Menu Labeling Rule (21 CFR Part 101); NRA ServSafe

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the combo meal record was last modified in the data platform. Follows ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports change tracking, audit trail, and incremental data processing in the Silver layer.

products.name: item_86_event
products.description: Transactional record capturing an '86' event — when a menu item is temporarily removed from service at a specific restaurant due to stockout, quality issue, or equipment failure. Captures the item, restaurant, channel affected, start timestamp, end timestamp, reason code (out-of-stock/equipment/quality/recall), reported-by, and resolution action. Critical for operational compliance and guest experience tracking.
products.reference: 86 Event / Item Outage / Item Unavailability Event

attributes.name: item_86_event_id
attributes.description: Unique surrogate identifier for each 86 event record — when a menu item is temporarily pulled from service at a restaurant. Primary key of this table.

attributes.name: channel_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to order.channel. Business justification: When an item is 86'd, the specific channel affected (OLO, drive-thru, dine-in) must be recorded for channel suppression actions and impact reporting. channel_affected is a denormalized plain column; r

attributes.name: financial_period_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.financial_period. Business justification: item_86_event tracks estimated_lost_covers (lost revenue from stockouts). Linking to financial_period enables period-level lost sales reporting, a key foodservice operations finance metric for invento

attributes.name: ingredient_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supply.ingredient. Business justification: 86-event root cause analysis: kitchen managers and supply chain teams must identify which specific ingredient caused an item outage for reorder triggering and root cause reporting. The existing reason

attributes.name: inspection_violation_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to foodsafety.inspection_violation. Business justification: When a health inspector orders removal of a menu item due to a violation, the 86 event must be traceable to the triggering inspection_violation for compliance documentation and corrective action track

attributes.name: maintenance_work_order_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to realestate.maintenance_work_order. Business justification: Equipment failure causing item unavailability (86 event) must be correlated with the maintenance work order raised to resolve it. QSR operations teams use this link for root cause analysis, SLA compli

attributes.name: menu_lto_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to menu.menu_lto. Business justification: An 86 event (item temporarily removed from service) is operationally critical when it affects a Limited Time Offer item. The item_86_event already has is_lto_item BOOLEAN flag indicating the item is a

attributes.name: primary_item_equipment_asset_id
attributes.description: Reference to the specific kitchen equipment unit that failed and caused the 86 event, when reason_code = 'equipment_failure'. Supports Repairs and Maintenance (R&M) tracking and equipment reliability analysis.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA — Fixed Assets (FA); Oracle MICROS POS — Kitchen Equipment

attributes.name: primary_item_reported_by_employee_id
attributes.description: Reference to the employee (Back of House (BOH) or Front of House (FOH) staff member) who first reported or logged the 86 event. Links to workforce employee master.
attributes.references: Workday HCM — Workforce Management; OSHA Incident Reporting Standards

attributes.name: primary_menu_item_id
attributes.description: Reference to the menu item that was 86'd (removed from service). Links to the menu.menu_item master record.

attributes.name: recall_event_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supply.recall_event. Business justification: FDA/USDA recall traceability: when is_recall_related=true on an 86 event, regulators and operations teams require a direct link to the specific recall_event for compliance reporting, affected-location

attributes.name: shift_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to workforce.shift. Business justification: Item 86 events occur during a specific shift. Linking to the active shift enables operational reporting on which shifts experience the most stockouts, supports labor-menu correlation analysis, and is

attributes.name: site_id
attributes.description: Reference to the restaurant location where the 86 event occurred. Enables operational and geographic analysis of item unavailability.

attributes.name: stock_item_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_item. Business justification: An 86 event is fundamentally triggered by stock depletion of a specific stock_item. This FK enables inventory-driven 86 management, post-event root cause analysis, and automated reorder triggers. The

attributes.name: unit_id
attributes.description: Reference to the restaurant location where the 86 event occurred. Enables operational and geographic analysis of item unavailability.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when this 86 event record was first created in the data platform. Audit trail field for data lineage and Silver layer ingestion tracking.
attributes.references: GAAP/IFRS Audit Trail Standards; PCI DSS Record Integrity

attributes.name: daypart_affected
attributes.description: The daypart during which the 86 event was active or first occurred. Enables PMIX (Product Mix) and daypart-level impact analysis. Aligns with menu.daypart definitions.
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS — Daypart Configuration; NRA Daypart Standards

attributes.name: duration_minutes
attributes.description: Total elapsed time in minutes from start_timestamp to end_timestamp. Populated upon resolution. Used for Speed of Service (SOS) impact analysis and operational KPI reporting.

attributes.name: end_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the menu item was restored to service and the 86 event was closed. Null if the event is still open. Used to calculate total downtime duration.

attributes.name: escalated_to_team
attributes.description: The internal team to which the 86 event was escalated, if applicable. Supports accountability tracking and cross-functional response coordination.
attributes.references: Zenput — Operational Compliance; FranConnect — Franchise Compliance

attributes.name: escalation_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the 86 event was escalated to operations management, supply chain, or franchise support teams. Null if the event was resolved without escalation. Supports SLA and response time analysis.
attributes.references: Zenput — Operational Compliance; FranConnect — Franchise Compliance

attributes.name: estimated_lost_covers
attributes.description: Estimated number of guest covers (transactions) that could not be fulfilled due to the 86 event during the affected period. Derived operationally at the restaurant level for guest experience impact reporting. Not a calculated KPI — captured as a manager estimate at time of resolution.
attributes.references: NRA — Guest Experience Standards; Oracle MICROS POS — Transaction Reporting

attributes.name: event_number
attributes.description: Human-readable, externally referenceable identifier for the 86 event (e.g., 86-2024-07-15-A3F9K2). Used in operational communications, Zenput audit tasks, and guest experience escalations.
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS — Menu Management; Zenput — Operational Compliance

attributes.name: event_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle state of the 86 event. 'open' = item currently unavailable; 'resolved' = item restored to service; 'escalated' = issue elevated to operations or supply chain team; 'cancelled' = event logged in error.
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS — Menu Management; NRA SOP Standards

attributes.name: guest_notification_sent
attributes.description: Indicates whether a guest-facing notification was sent (e.g., via OLO or 3PD platform) to inform customers that the item is temporarily unavailable. True = notification dispatched. Supports Customer Satisfaction (CSAT) and Net Promoter Score (NPS) impact tracking.
attributes.references: Olo Digital Ordering Platform — Guest Notifications; NRA Guest Experience Standards

attributes.name: haccp_ccp_reference
attributes.business_glossary_term: Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points (HACCP) Critical Control Point (CCP) Reference
attributes.description: Reference code to the specific HACCP Critical Control Point (CCP) that was breached or triggered this 86 event, if applicable. Used for food safety audit trails and regulatory compliance reporting.
attributes.references: HACCP — Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points; FDA Food Safety Modernization Act; ISO 22000

attributes.name: is_food_safety_related
attributes.description: Indicates whether the 86 event was triggered by a food safety concern (e.g., HACCP Critical Control Point (CCP) failure, quality issue, or regulatory recall). True = food safety event requiring Zenput audit task and potential regulatory notification.
attributes.references: HACCP — Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points; FDA Food Safety Modernization Act; ISO 22000 Food Safety Management

attributes.name: is_lto_item
attributes.description: Indicates whether the 86'd item is a Limited Time Offer (LTO). True = LTO item. 86 events on LTO items carry heightened business impact due to marketing commitments and guest expectations, and may require immediate escalation.
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS — Menu Management; NRA LTO Best Practices

attributes.name: is_recall_related
attributes.description: Indicates whether the 86 event was triggered by a formal supplier or regulatory product recall. True = item must not be served and recall protocol must be followed per FDA/USDA guidelines.
attributes.references: FDA Food Safety Modernization Act; USDA Meat and Poultry Inspection; ISO 22000

attributes.name: kds_alert_sent
attributes.business_glossary_term: Kitchen Display System (KDS) Alert Sent Flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether a Kitchen Display System (KDS) alert was sent to BOH staff notifying them of the 86 event. True = KDS alert dispatched. Supports BOH operational awareness and throughput management.
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS — Kitchen Display System (KDS)

attributes.name: olo_item_code
attributes.description: The item identifier in the Olo Digital Ordering Platform at the time of the 86 event. Used to confirm whether the item was also suppressed on the OLO channel during the event.
attributes.references: Olo Digital Ordering Platform — Menu Management

attributes.name: olo_suppressed
attributes.description: Indicates whether the item was suppressed on the Olo Digital Ordering Platform during the 86 event. True = item hidden from online ordering menu. Prevents guest orders for unavailable items on digital channels.
attributes.references: Olo Digital Ordering Platform — Menu Management

attributes.name: ownership_model
attributes.description: Indicates whether the restaurant where the 86 event occurred is a franchise location or a company-owned location. Drives escalation routing and compliance accountability.
attributes.references: FranConnect — Franchise Compliance; IFA Franchise Best Practices

attributes.name: pos_item_code
attributes.description: The Point of Sale (POS) item code as configured in Oracle MICROS POS at the time of the 86 event. Captured at event time to preserve the POS state even if the menu item code changes later.

attributes.name: pos_suppressed
attributes.business_glossary_term: Point of Sale (POS) Item Suppressed Flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the item was actively suppressed (hidden/disabled) on the Oracle MICROS POS system during the 86 event. True = item removed from POS ordering screens. Ensures operational accuracy and prevents staff from ringing up unavailable items.

attributes.name: reason_code
attributes.description: Standardized reason code explaining why the menu item was removed from service. 'out_of_stock' = inventory depleted; 'equipment_failure' = kitchen equipment down; 'quality_issue' = item failed quality/HACCP check; 'recall' = supplier or regulatory recall; 'supplier_delay' = ingredient not delivered; 'prep_time_exceeded' = throughput constraint. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: out_of_stock|equipment_failure|quality_issue|recall|supplier_delay|prep_time_exceeded — promote to reference product]
attributes.references: HACCP — Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points; FDA Food Safety Modernization Act; NRA SOP Standards

attributes.name: reason_detail
attributes.description: Free-text narrative providing additional context beyond the reason_code. For example, specifying which piece of equipment failed, which ingredient was out of stock, or the nature of the quality issue. Supports root cause analysis.
attributes.references: Zenput — Operational Compliance; NRA SOP Standards

attributes.name: reported_by_role
attributes.description: Job role of the employee who reported the 86 event (e.g., manager, Back of House (BOH) staff, Front of House (FOH) staff, shift lead, franchisee). Supports accountability and training gap analysis.
attributes.references: Workday HCM — Job Role Classification; NRA ServSafe Certification

attributes.name: resolution_action
attributes.description: The corrective action taken to resolve the 86 event and restore the item to service. 'restocked' = inventory replenished; 'equipment_repaired' = kitchen equipment fixed; 'item_substituted' = alternative item offered; 'recalled_item_removed' = item permanently pulled per recall; 'supplier_order_placed' = emergency procurement initiated; 'no_action' = event closed without resolution (e.g., end of daypart). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: restocked|equipment_repaired|item_substituted|recalled_item_removed|supplier_order_placed|no_action — promote to reference product]
attributes.references: MarketMan Inventory Management; Zenput — Operational Compliance; FDA Food Safety Modernization Act

attributes.name: restaurant_format
