attributes.name: discount_amount
attributes.description: The total monetary discount applied to this payment tender, including promotional discounts, coupon redemptions, and employee meal discounts. Part of the MONETARY_TRIPLET adjustment component for net revenue calculation.
attributes.references: GAAP/IFRS Revenue Recognition ASC 606; Oracle MICROS POS — Discount Tracking

attributes.name: gift_card_number_masked
attributes.description: Masked identifier for the restaurant-issued gift card used in this tender transaction. Retains only partial digits for identification while protecting the full card value. Applicable when tender_type is gift_card.
attributes.references: PCI DSS v4.0 Req 3.3; NRA Gift Card Best Practices

attributes.name: interchange_fee_amount
attributes.description: The fee charged by the card-issuing bank for processing this card payment, expressed in the transaction currency. A key component of payment processing cost analysis and COGS% (Cost of Goods Sold Percentage) calculations.
attributes.references: Visa/Mastercard Interchange Fee Schedules; GAAP/IFRS Cost Recognition

attributes.name: is_split_tender
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether this payment record is part of a split-tender transaction where multiple payment instruments were used to satisfy a single order. True when the order has more than one associated payment tender record.
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS — Split Tender Processing

attributes.name: is_voided
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether this payment tender record has been voided prior to settlement. A voided transaction was authorized but cancelled before batch close, distinguishing it from a post-settlement refund.
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS — Void Processing; PCI DSS v4.0

attributes.name: loyalty_points_redeemed
attributes.description: The number of loyalty program points redeemed as part of this payment tender. Applicable when tender_type is loyalty_redemption. Used for loyalty liability accounting and program performance analytics.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM — Loyalty Redemption; GAAP/IFRS Deferred Revenue

attributes.name: masked_card_number
attributes.description: PCI DSS-compliant masked representation of the payment card number, retaining only the first 4 and last 4 digits (e.g., 4111********1111). Full PANs (Primary Account Numbers) are never stored in the lakehouse per PCI DSS Requirement 3.3.
attributes.references: PCI DSS v4.0 Req 3.3 — Protect Stored Account Data

attributes.name: offline_authorization_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this payment was authorized in offline mode due to network connectivity loss at the restaurant. Offline authorizations carry elevated fraud and chargeback risk and require special reconciliation handling.
attributes.references: PCI DSS v4.0 Req 12.3; Oracle MICROS POS — Offline Authorization

attributes.name: payment_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle state of the payment tender record. Tracks the payment workflow from authorization through settlement or failure. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: authorized|captured|settled|declined|voided|refunded|pending — promote to reference product if additional states are needed]
attributes.references: PCI DSS v4.0; Oracle MICROS POS — Payment Lifecycle

attributes.name: pos_transaction_number
attributes.description: The externally-known transaction number assigned by the Oracle MICROS POS system at the time of payment capture. Used for end-of-day reconciliation, chargeback research, and audit trails.
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS — Transaction Reference; PCI DSS v4.0 Req 10

attributes.name: processor_name
attributes.description: The name of the payment processing service provider that handled authorization and settlement for this tender (e.g., Worldpay, Heartland, Stripe, Square). Used for processor-level performance and fee analysis.
attributes.references: PCI DSS v4.0 — Third-Party Service Provider Management

attributes.name: processor_reference_code
attributes.description: The unique transaction identifier assigned by the external payment processor (e.g., Stripe, Worldpay, Heartland) for this tender. Used for cross-system reconciliation between POS and payment gateway records.
attributes.references: PCI DSS v4.0 Req 10; Payment Processor Integration Standards

attributes.name: promo_code_applied
attributes.description: The promotional or discount code entered by the guest at the time of payment, if applicable. Used to link payment records to marketing campaign performance and LTO (Limited Time Offer) redemption analytics.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM — Marketing Campaign Tracking; Oracle MICROS POS — Discount Configuration

attributes.name: refund_reason
attributes.description: Categorical reason code explaining why a refund or void was issued for this payment. Used for operational quality analysis, CSAT (Customer Satisfaction) root cause investigation, and fraud monitoring.
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS — Void/Refund Reason Codes; NRA Operational Standards

attributes.name: response_code
attributes.description: The ISO 8583 or processor-specific response code returned by the payment network for this authorization attempt (e.g., 00 = Approved, 05 = Do Not Honor, 51 = Insufficient Funds). Used for decline analysis and payment success rate monitoring.
attributes.references: ISO 8583 — Financial Transaction Message Standard; PCI DSS v4.0

attributes.name: settlement_batch_code
attributes.description: The batch identifier assigned by the payment processor during end-of-day settlement processing. Groups all captured transactions submitted together for bank funding. Used for daily cash reconciliation and SAP S/4HANA AR posting.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA — AR Settlement; PCI DSS v4.0 Req 10

attributes.name: settlement_date
attributes.description: The date on which the payment processor settled funds to the restaurant's bank account. Distinct from captured_timestamp. Used for cash flow forecasting, bank reconciliation, and financial close processes in SAP S/4HANA.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA — GL/AR Settlement; GAAP/IFRS Cash Basis Reporting

attributes.name: source_system
attributes.description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this payment record originated. Supports data lineage tracking and multi-channel reconciliation across Oracle MICROS POS, Olo Digital Ordering Platform, self-service kiosks, and 3PD (Third-Party Delivery) API feeds.
attributes.references: Enterprise Data Governance — Data Lineage Standards

attributes.name: split_tender_sequence
attributes.description: Sequential line number identifying the order of tender application within a split-tender transaction. For example, a guest paying part cash and part credit card would have sequence 1 (cash) and sequence 2 (credit card). Enables correct reconstruction of multi-tender payment scenarios.
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS — Split Tender Configuration

attributes.name: tax_amount
attributes.description: The total sales tax or VAT amount included in this payment tender, as calculated by the POS system based on applicable jurisdiction tax rules. Required for tax remittance reporting and SAP S/4HANA tax posting.
attributes.references: FDA/USDA Tax Compliance; SAP S/4HANA — Tax Module; GAAP/IFRS

attributes.name: tender_type
attributes.description: Classification of the payment instrument used for this tender. Supports split-tender scenarios. Key dimension for payment mix analysis and financial settlement routing. 3PD (Third-Party Delivery) settlement represents aggregated remittance from delivery partners. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: cash|credit_card|debit_card|gift_card|loyalty_redemption|3pd_settlement|mobile_wallet|voucher — promote to reference product if additional tender types are needed]
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS — Tender Type Configuration; PCI DSS v4.0

attributes.name: tendered_amount
attributes.description: The gross amount presented by the guest for this specific tender line. In cash scenarios, this may exceed the order total, resulting in change due. In card scenarios, this equals the charged amount. Core component of the MONETARY_TRIPLET for payment-level financial analysis.
attributes.references: GAAP/IFRS Revenue Recognition; PCI DSS v4.0

attributes.name: third_party_delivery_partner
attributes.description: Identifies the 3PD (Third-Party Delivery) aggregator platform responsible for payment settlement when tender_type is 3pd_settlement. Used for partner-level remittance reconciliation and commission tracking.
attributes.references: Olo Digital Ordering Platform — 3PD Integration; FTC Franchise Disclosure

attributes.name: third_party_order_reference
attributes.description: The order identifier assigned by the 3PD (Third-Party Delivery) partner platform (e.g., DoorDash order ID, Uber Eats order ID). Used to cross-reference restaurant POS records with delivery partner settlement reports.
attributes.references: Olo Digital Ordering Platform — 3PD Order Mapping

attributes.name: tip_amount
attributes.description: Gratuity amount added by the guest to this payment tender. Tracked separately from the order total for labor distribution, tax reporting, and server tip-out calculations. Applicable in casual and fine-dining service models.
attributes.references: IRS Publication 531 — Reporting Tip Income; GAAP/IFRS

attributes.name: token
attributes.description: A network-issued or processor-issued token that replaces the actual card PAN (Primary Account Number) for secure storage and recurring payment processing. Enables PCI DSS scope reduction through tokenization.
attributes.references: PCI DSS v4.0 Req 3.5 — Tokenization; EMV Payment Tokenization Specification

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: Audit timestamp recording when this payment record was last modified in the data platform, such as when a status transitions from authorized to settled or a refund is processed.
attributes.references: GAAP/IFRS Audit Trail Requirements; PCI DSS v4.0 Req 10

products.name: status_event
products.description: Immutable event log tracking every lifecycle state transition of a guest order from placement through fulfillment or cancellation. States include: received, acknowledged, in_preparation, ready, dispatched, delivered, cancelled, voided. Captures the state, prior state, transition timestamp, triggering actor (POS, KDS, OLO, 3PD webhook), and any exception reason. Enables SOS (Speed of Service) and ticket time analytics.
products.reference: Oracle MICROS KDS Status / Olo Order Webhook Event

attributes.name: status_event_id
attributes.description: Unique surrogate identifier for each immutable order lifecycle state transition event record in the Silver layer. Primary key of this append-only event log.

attributes.name: channel_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to order.channel. Business justification: status_event.service_channel is a denormalized STRING storing the order channel for each lifecycle event. Normalizing to channel_id FK referencing order.channel.channel_id enforces referential integri

attributes.name: employee_id
attributes.description: Reference to the employee (FOH cashier, BOH cook, or manager) who manually triggered this state transition. Null when the transition was system- or device-initiated. Supports labor accountability and SOS coaching.
attributes.references: Workday HCM — Employee Master; Oracle MICROS POS — Employee Clock-In

attributes.name: guest_order_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent guest order for which this status transition event was recorded. Links all lifecycle events back to the originating order.
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS — Order Management Module

attributes.name: pos_terminal_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the Oracle MICROS POS terminal or kiosk that captured or processed this state transition event. Used for terminal-level performance diagnostics and audit trails.

attributes.name: site_id
attributes.description: Reference to the restaurant location where the order was placed and fulfilled. Used to segment Speed of Service (SOS) and ticket time analytics by location.

attributes.name: source_event_status_event_id
attributes.description: The native event or transaction identifier assigned by the originating source system (e.g., MICROS transaction ID, OLO event UUID, KDS bump ID). Enables exact-match deduplication and cross-system reconciliation during Silver layer ingestion.
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS; Olo Digital Ordering Platform; Internal Data Lineage Standard

attributes.name: unit_id
attributes.description: Reference to the restaurant location where the order was placed and fulfilled. Used to segment Speed of Service (SOS) and ticket time analytics by location.

attributes.name: business_date
attributes.description: The operational business date to which this event is attributed for financial and operational reporting purposes. May differ from event_date for late-night orders that cross midnight but belong to the prior business day's P&L period.
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS — Business Day Configuration; GAAP/IFRS Reporting Period Standards

attributes.name: cumulative_elapsed_seconds
attributes.description: Total number of seconds elapsed from the initial 'received' event to this event, representing the running ticket time for the order up to this point. Used to measure end-to-end Speed of Service (SOS) at any point in the lifecycle.
attributes.references: NRA Speed of Service (SOS) Standards; Oracle MICROS POS — Ticket Time Reporting

attributes.name: current_state
attributes.description: The order lifecycle state that this event transitions the order INTO. Drives Speed of Service (SOS) analytics, KDS routing, and fulfillment tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: received|acknowledged|in_preparation|ready|dispatched|delivered|cancelled|voided — promote to reference product]
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS; KDS (Kitchen Display System) Workflow; Olo Digital Ordering Platform

attributes.name: data_quality_flag
attributes.description: Silver layer data quality classification for this event record. Values: valid (passed all checks), suspect (failed one or more quality rules), duplicate (identified as a duplicate of another event), late_arriving (received significantly after event_timestamp), corrected (overrides a prior suspect record).
attributes.references: Internal Data Quality Framework; Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer Standards

attributes.name: daypart
attributes.description: The operational daypart segment during which this event occurred (e.g., breakfast, lunch, dinner, late_night). Used to segment Speed of Service (SOS) and throughput analytics by time-of-day business period.
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS — Daypart Configuration; NRA Daypart Standards

attributes.name: delivery_zone
attributes.description: Geographic delivery zone or radius tier assigned to this order for third-party delivery (3PD) or direct delivery fulfillment. Used for delivery time SOS benchmarking and zone-level fulfillment analytics.
attributes.references: Olo Digital Ordering Platform — Delivery Zone Configuration

attributes.name: drive_thru_lane
attributes.description: Identifies the specific drive-thru (DT) lane associated with this order event for multi-lane drive-thru configurations. Null or 'none' for non-drive-thru channels. Enables per-lane SOS and throughput analytics.
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS — Drive-Thru Configuration; NRA Drive-Thru Standards

attributes.name: elapsed_seconds_in_prior_state
attributes.description: Number of seconds the order spent in the prior_state before transitioning to the current_state. Computed at ingestion time from consecutive event timestamps. Core input for Speed of Service (SOS) and ticket time analytics.
attributes.references: NRA Speed of Service (SOS) Standards; Oracle MICROS POS — Ticket Time Reporting

attributes.name: event_date
attributes.description: Calendar date (yyyy-MM-dd) on which this event occurred, derived from event_timestamp. Stored as a discrete DATE column to support efficient date-based partitioning, daily SOS reporting, and Same-Store Sales (SSS) period alignment.
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS; GAAP/IFRS Reporting Period Standards

attributes.name: event_sequence
attributes.description: Monotonically increasing integer representing the ordinal position of this event within the full lifecycle of a single order. Enables reconstruction of the complete state machine path (e.g., 1=received, 2=acknowledged, 3=in_preparation).
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS — Order Event Stream

attributes.name: event_timestamp
attributes.description: Precise date and time (ISO 8601 with timezone offset) at which the order transitioned into the current state. The principal real-world event time used for Speed of Service (SOS) and ticket time calculations.
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS; Olo Digital Ordering Platform; ISO 8601

attributes.name: exception_reason_code
attributes.description: Structured code indicating why an exceptional transition occurred (e.g., ITEM_OUT_OF_STOCK, GUEST_CANCEL, PAYMENT_FAILED, EQUIPMENT_DOWN, DUPLICATE_ORDER). Null for normal lifecycle transitions. Drives exception reporting and root-cause analytics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: promote to reference product for full code list]
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS — Exception Codes; Zenput Operational Compliance

attributes.name: exception_reason_description
attributes.description: Free-text narrative describing the exception or anomaly that caused this state transition, as entered by staff or returned by the triggering system. Supplements the structured exception_reason_code for operational context.
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS — Exception Notes; Zenput Operational Compliance

attributes.name: fiscal_period
attributes.description: The fiscal reporting period (e.g., '2024-P04-W02') to which this event belongs, aligned to the company's internal fiscal calendar. Enables period-over-period SOS and throughput comparisons in financial reporting.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA — Fiscal Calendar; GAAP/IFRS Reporting Standards

attributes.name: fulfillment_mode
attributes.description: Indicates whether the order was placed for immediate fulfillment, scheduled for a future time (advance order), or ASAP (as-soon-as-possible with a requested window). Affects SOS target calculation and kitchen scheduling.
attributes.references: Olo Digital Ordering Platform — Order Scheduling; Oracle MICROS POS

attributes.name: ingestion_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time this event record was first written into the Silver layer of the Databricks Lakehouse. Distinct from event_timestamp (real-world event time). Used for data latency monitoring, pipeline SLA tracking, and audit trail.
attributes.references: Internal Data Architecture Standard — Silver Layer Audit; Databricks Lakehouse Best Practices

attributes.name: is_cancellation_event
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether this event represents an order cancellation (current_state = 'cancelled'). Enables rapid filtering for cancellation rate analytics, refund processing triggers, and guest recovery workflows.
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS; Olo Digital Ordering Platform — Cancellation Policy

attributes.name: is_sos_breach
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether the cumulative elapsed time at this event exceeded the configured SOS target for the channel and daypart. True = SOS breach detected. Drives SOS compliance dashboards and operational alerts.

attributes.name: is_terminal_state
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether the current_state is a terminal (end-of-lifecycle) state for the order (i.e., delivered, cancelled, or voided). Simplifies filtering for completed order analytics without requiring state enumeration logic.
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS — Order Lifecycle Definition

attributes.name: is_void_event
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether this event represents an order void (current_state = 'voided'). Voids differ from cancellations in that they are typically manager-initiated post-tender corrections. Supports POS audit and loss prevention reporting.
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS — Void Management; PCI DSS — Transaction Integrity

attributes.name: kds_station_code
attributes.description: Identifier of the KDS (Kitchen Display System) station that acknowledged or bumped this order event. Populated only when the triggering actor is KDS. Enables BOH (Back of House) station-level ticket time analysis.
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS — KDS Integration; KDS (Kitchen Display System) Workflow

attributes.name: manager_override
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether this state transition required a manager authorization override (e.g., void, post-close cancellation, or exception state). Supports loss prevention, audit compliance, and operational accountability reporting.
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS — Manager Override Log; OSHA SOP Compliance

attributes.name: olo_order_reference
attributes.description: External order reference number assigned by the Olo Digital Ordering Platform for online and mobile orders. Populated only for OLO-originated events. Enables cross-system reconciliation between the lakehouse and the OLO platform.
attributes.references: Olo Digital Ordering Platform — Order API

attributes.name: order_type
attributes.description: Classification of the order by business purpose. Values: standard (regular guest order), lto (Limited Time Offer promotional order), catering, group, loyalty_redemption, comp (complimentary). Enables type-specific SOS benchmarking and PMIX analysis.
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS — Order Type Configuration; NRA Order Classification

attributes.name: partition_date
attributes.description: The date value used as the physical partition key for this table in the Databricks Lakehouse Delta Lake storage layer. Typically aligned to business_date to optimize time-range query performance for SOS and throughput analytics.
attributes.references: Databricks Lakehouse Delta Lake Partitioning Best Practices; Internal Data Architecture Standard

attributes.name: prior_state
attributes.description: The order lifecycle state immediately before this transition event. Null for the first event (initial placement). Enables detection of invalid state transitions and rollback analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: received|acknowledged|in_preparation|ready|dispatched|delivered|cancelled|voided — promote to reference product]
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS; KDS (Kitchen Display System) Workflow

attributes.name: promise_time_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time communicated to the guest as the expected ready or delivery time at the moment of order placement. Used to measure promise-time accuracy and guest satisfaction (CSAT) correlation.
attributes.references: Olo Digital Ordering Platform — Promise Time API; NRA Guest Experience Standards

attributes.name: record_updated_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time this event record was last modified in the Silver layer (e.g., due to late-arriving corrections or enrichment). For a truly immutable event log this should rarely change; tracked for data quality auditing.
attributes.references: Internal Data Architecture Standard — Silver Layer Audit

attributes.name: scheduled_fulfillment_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time the guest requested or the system scheduled for order fulfillment. Populated only for scheduled/advance orders. Used to calculate on-time fulfillment rate and schedule adherence.
attributes.references: Olo Digital Ordering Platform — Order Scheduling

attributes.name: sos_target_seconds
attributes.description: The configured Speed of Service (SOS) target in seconds for this service channel and daypart combination at the time of the event. Enables real-time and historical SOS compliance measurement against operational benchmarks.
attributes.references: NRA Speed of Service (SOS) Standards; Oracle MICROS POS — SOS Configuration

attributes.name: source_system
attributes.description: The operational system of record that emitted this event record. Identifies the authoritative source for lineage, reconciliation, and data quality monitoring. Values: MICROS_POS, OLO, KDS, 3PD_WEBHOOK, CATERING_SYSTEM, KIOSK, MANUAL. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MICROS_POS|OLO|KDS|3PD_WEBHOOK|CATERING_SYSTEM|KIOSK|MANUAL — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS; Olo Digital Ordering Platform; Internal Data Lineage Standard

attributes.name: third_party_delivery_provider
attributes.description: Name of the third-party delivery (3PD) aggregator that submitted the webhook triggering this event (e.g., doordash, uber_eats, grubhub). Null or 'none' for non-3PD channels. Enables 3PD-specific SOS and fulfillment analytics.
attributes.references: Olo Digital Ordering Platform — 3PD Integration; FTC Advertising Regulations

attributes.name: third_party_event_reference
attributes.description: External event or webhook identifier provided by the third-party delivery (3PD) provider for this specific state transition. Used for cross-system reconciliation and dispute resolution with 3PD partners.
attributes.references: Olo Digital Ordering Platform — 3PD Webhook Specification

attributes.name: triggering_actor
attributes.description: The system or human actor that initiated this state transition. Values include POS (Oracle MICROS Point of Sale), KDS (Kitchen Display System), OLO (Online Ordering platform), 3PD (Third-Party Delivery webhook), CATERING, STAFF (manual override), SYSTEM (automated rule), or GUEST (self-service cancellation). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: POS|KDS|OLO|3PD|CATERING|STAFF|SYSTEM|GUEST — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS; Olo Digital Ordering Platform; 3PD Integration Specification

products.name: channel
products.description: Reference master defining all service channels through which guest orders are placed: POS (dine-in, counter), drive-thru (DT), online ordering (OLO), third-party delivery (3PD — DoorDash, Uber Eats, Grubhub), catering, kiosk, and mobile app. Captures channel code, channel category, integration platform, fulfillment mode (dine-in, takeout, delivery, curbside), and active status. Serves as the FK target for guest_order channel attribution, channel-mix reporting, and ACV benchmarking.

attributes.name: channel_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the order channel. Primary key for the order_channel reference master.

attributes.name: gl_account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Revenue by channel (dine-in, drive-thru, delivery, catering) is posted to distinct GL revenue accounts for channel-level P&L reporting. Restaurant controllers map each order channel to a GL account to

attributes.name: activation_date
attributes.description: Date when this order channel was first activated and made available for order placement across the restaurant network.

attributes.name: average_ticket_time_seconds
attributes.description: Benchmark average ticket time (order placement to fulfillment) in seconds for this channel, used for Speed of Service (SOS) performance tracking. Null if not applicable.

attributes.name: channel_category
attributes.description: High-level classification of the channel by service model: dine_in (FOH counter, table service), takeout (DT, counter pickup), delivery (3PD, own delivery), catering (bulk orders), hybrid (multi-mode channels like kiosk).

attributes.name: channel_code
attributes.description: Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the order channel (e.g., POS, DT, OLO, 3PD, KIOSK, MOBILE, CATERING). Used as the business identifier for integration and reporting.

attributes.name: channel_description
attributes.description: Detailed description of the order channel, including its purpose, typical use cases, and any special operational considerations.

attributes.name: channel_name
attributes.description: Full descriptive name of the order channel (e.g., Point of Sale, Drive-Thru, Online Ordering, Third-Party Delivery, Kiosk, Mobile App, Catering).

attributes.name: channel_type
attributes.description: Ownership classification of the channel: owned (company-operated channels like POS, DT, proprietary OLO), third_party (external platforms like 3PD aggregators), hybrid (co-branded or white-label solutions).

attributes.name: commission_rate_percent
attributes.description: Commission or transaction fee percentage charged by the channel provider (applicable to 3PD channels). Null for owned channels. Used for COGS and P&L analysis.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this order channel record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: deactivation_date
attributes.description: Date when this order channel was deactivated or retired. Null if the channel is still active.

attributes.name: default_daypart
attributes.description: Default daypart (e.g., breakfast, lunch, dinner, late_night) typically associated with this channel. Null if the channel operates across all dayparts equally.

attributes.name: display_order
attributes.description: Numeric sort order for displaying this channel in reporting dashboards and user interfaces. Lower numbers appear first.

attributes.name: fulfillment_mode
attributes.description: Primary method by which orders placed through this channel are fulfilled: dine_in (guest consumes on-premise), takeout (guest picks up), delivery (delivered to guest location), curbside (pickup at designated area), catering (scheduled bulk delivery).

attributes.name: integration_platform
attributes.description: Name of the technology platform or system that powers this channel (e.g., Oracle MICROS, Olo, DoorDash, Uber Eats, Grubhub, proprietary mobile app). Null for native POS channels.

attributes.name: is_active
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether this channel is currently active and available for order placement. False indicates the channel has been retired or temporarily disabled.

attributes.name: is_digital
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether this is a digital channel (true for OLO, mobile app, kiosk, 3PD; false for traditional POS, DT counter service).

attributes.name: kds_routing_enabled
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether orders from this channel are routed to the Kitchen Display System (KDS) for BOH preparation tracking. True for most channels; may be false for catering orders prepared off-site.

attributes.name: requires_restaurant_assignment
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether orders from this channel must be assigned to a specific restaurant location at order time (true for delivery, catering; false for walk-in POS).

attributes.name: supports_guest_data_capture
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether this channel captures guest identification and contact information (true for OLO, mobile, 3PD; false for anonymous POS transactions).

attributes.name: supports_loyalty_integration
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether this channel supports integration with the customer loyalty program for points accrual and redemption.
attributes.references: CRM and Loyalty Program Integration Standards

attributes.name: supports_payment_at_order
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether payment is collected at the time of order placement (true for OLO, 3PD, mobile; false for traditional counter service where payment occurs at pickup).

attributes.name: supports_scheduled_orders
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether this channel allows customers to schedule orders for future fulfillment (true for catering, some OLO platforms; false for immediate-service channels like DT).

attributes.name: target_sos_seconds
attributes.business_glossary_term: Target Speed of Service (SOS) in Seconds
attributes.description: Target Speed of Service (SOS) threshold in seconds for this channel, representing the operational goal for order fulfillment time. Used for performance benchmarking and SOP compliance.

attributes.name: third_party_provider
attributes.description: Name of the third-party delivery service provider if this channel is a 3PD channel (e.g., DoorDash, Uber Eats, Grubhub, Postmates). Null for non-3PD channels.

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this order channel record was last modified.

products.name: delivery_order
products.description: Manages the delivery fulfillment record for orders dispatched via third-party delivery (3PD) platforms (DoorDash, Uber Eats, Grubhub) or first-party delivery. Captures 3PD platform reference ID, courier assignment, estimated and actual delivery times, delivery distance, delivery fee, platform commission rate, delivery status, and any delivery exception (late, missing item, cancelled). Bridges the Olo digital ordering platform data with internal order records.

attributes.name: delivery_order_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the delivery order record. Primary key.

attributes.name: delivery_platform_id
attributes.description: Reference to the third-party delivery (3PD) platform or first-party delivery service provider (DoorDash, Uber Eats, Grubhub, proprietary fleet).

attributes.name: employee_id
attributes.description: Reference to the courier or driver assigned to deliver the order. May be null if not yet assigned.

attributes.name: facility_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to realestate.facility. Business justification: Delivery orders are fulfilled from a specific facility (ghost kitchen, satellite prep station, or multi-facility site). Facility health inspection scores, operational status, and kitchen capacity dire

attributes.name: franchisee_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to franchise.franchisee. Business justification: Delivery orders generate royalty-bearing revenue attributed to the franchisee operating the fulfilling unit. Franchise royalty reporting, platform commission reconciliation, and franchisee performance

attributes.name: guest_order_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent order being fulfilled via delivery. Links to the core order transaction.

attributes.name: member_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to loyalty.member. Business justification: Delivery-channel loyalty analytics: delivery orders by loyalty members need direct member linkage for delivery-channel points accrual, loyalty-driven free-delivery benefit tracking, and member deliver

attributes.name: shift_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to workforce.shift. Business justification: Delivery driver productivity reporting (deliveries per shift, labor cost per delivery, on-time rate by shift) requires direct shift attribution on delivery orders. Foodservice operations analysts use

attributes.name: site_id
attributes.description: Reference to the restaurant location fulfilling the delivery order.

attributes.name: unit_id
attributes.description: Reference to the restaurant location fulfilling the delivery order.

attributes.name: actual_delivery_time_minutes
attributes.description: Actual time in minutes taken by the courier to deliver the order from pickup to guest, calculated from picked up timestamp to actual delivery timestamp.

attributes.name: actual_delivery_timestamp
attributes.description: Actual date and time when the order was delivered to the guest, as confirmed by the courier or delivery platform.

attributes.name: actual_prep_time_minutes
attributes.description: Actual time in minutes taken by the restaurant to prepare the order, calculated from order confirmation to ready for pickup.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when this delivery order record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO currency code for all monetary amounts in this delivery order (e.g., USD, CAD, MXN).

attributes.name: customer_feedback
attributes.description: Free-text feedback provided by the guest about the delivery experience. Used for quality improvement and service recovery.

attributes.name: customer_rating
attributes.description: Guest rating for the delivery experience on a scale of 1 to 5. Used for Net Promoter Score (NPS) and Customer Satisfaction (CSAT) tracking.

attributes.name: delivery_address_line1
attributes.description: Primary street address line for the delivery destination. Contains personally identifiable information (PII).

attributes.name: delivery_address_line2
attributes.description: Secondary address line for apartment, suite, or unit number. Contains personally identifiable information (PII).

attributes.name: delivery_city
attributes.description: City name for the delivery destination. Contains personally identifiable information (PII).

attributes.name: delivery_country_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO country code for the delivery destination (e.g., USA, CAN, MEX).

attributes.name: delivery_distance_km
attributes.description: Calculated distance in kilometers from the restaurant to the delivery destination. Used for delivery fee calculation and logistics analysis.

attributes.name: delivery_exception_type
attributes.description: Type of exception or issue encountered during delivery fulfillment. Used for quality tracking and Customer Satisfaction (CSAT) analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: none|late_delivery|missing_items|wrong_address|customer_unavailable|order_damaged|cancelled_by_customer|cancelled_by_restaurant — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: delivery_fee_amount
attributes.description: Delivery fee charged to the guest for the delivery service. Excludes taxes and tips.

attributes.name: delivery_instructions
attributes.description: Special instructions provided by the guest for delivery (e.g., gate code, building entrance, leave at door). May contain personally identifiable information (PII).

attributes.name: delivery_latitude
attributes.description: Geographic latitude coordinate of the delivery destination. Used for routing optimization and distance calculation. Contains location PII.

attributes.name: delivery_longitude
attributes.description: Geographic longitude coordinate of the delivery destination. Used for routing optimization and distance calculation. Contains location PII.

attributes.name: delivery_postal_code
attributes.description: Postal or ZIP code for the delivery destination. Contains personally identifiable information (PII).

attributes.name: delivery_state_province
attributes.description: State or province code for the delivery destination. Contains personally identifiable information (PII).

attributes.name: delivery_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the delivery order. Tracks progression from order placement through final delivery or cancellation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|confirmed|preparing|ready_for_pickup|picked_up|in_transit|delivered|cancelled|failed — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: estimated_delivery_time_minutes
attributes.description: Estimated time in minutes for the courier to deliver the order from pickup to guest, as provided by the delivery platform routing algorithm.

attributes.name: estimated_delivery_timestamp
attributes.description: Estimated date and time for delivery to the guest, as communicated by the delivery platform or internal routing system.

attributes.name: estimated_prep_time_minutes
attributes.description: Estimated time in minutes for the restaurant to prepare the order, as communicated to the delivery platform.

attributes.name: exception_notes
attributes.description: Free-text notes describing the delivery exception or issue. Captured by courier, restaurant staff, or customer service.

attributes.name: is_contactless_delivery
attributes.description: Indicates whether the delivery was requested as contactless (leave at door). True if contactless, False otherwise.

attributes.name: order_confirmed_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the restaurant confirmed acceptance of the delivery order.

attributes.name: order_placed_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the delivery order was placed by the guest via the online ordering (OLO) or third-party delivery (3PD) platform.

attributes.name: picked_up_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the courier picked up the order from the restaurant location.

attributes.name: platform_commission_amount
attributes.description: Total commission amount paid to the third-party delivery platform for this order. Business-confidential financial information.

attributes.name: platform_commission_rate
attributes.description: Commission rate (as a decimal) charged by the third-party delivery platform on the order subtotal. Business-confidential pricing information.

attributes.name: platform_order_reference
attributes.description: External order identifier provided by the third-party delivery platform. Used for reconciliation and support inquiries with 3PD providers.

attributes.name: proof_of_delivery_url
attributes.description: URL to the proof of delivery image or document (e.g., photo of order at doorstep). Used for dispute resolution.

attributes.name: ready_for_pickup_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the order was marked as ready for courier pickup in the Kitchen Display System (KDS) or Point of Sale (POS).

attributes.name: tip_amount
attributes.description: Tip amount provided by the guest to the courier. May be pre-tip or post-delivery adjustment.

attributes.name: total_ticket_time_minutes
attributes.description: Total elapsed time in minutes from order placement to actual delivery. Key Speed of Service (SOS) metric for delivery orders.

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when this delivery order record was last modified.

products.name: catering_order
products.description: Manages large-format catering orders placed by corporate, event, or group guests. Captures catering event date, headcount, delivery or pickup mode, setup requirements, special dietary accommodations, catering package selected, quoted price, deposit amount, balance due, and fulfillment status. Distinct from standard guest orders due to advance booking lead time, custom BOM requirements, and dedicated fulfillment workflow.

attributes.name: catering_order_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the catering order. Primary key.

attributes.name: campaign_id

attributes.name: catering_gl_account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Catering revenue is posted to a distinct GL account (catering/banquet revenue) separate from regular food sales for P&L segmentation. Role-prefix 'catering_' used because catering revenue GL differs f

attributes.name: financial_period_id

attributes.name: member_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to loyalty.member. Business justification: Catering loyalty accrual: catering orders placed by loyalty members require direct member linkage for points accrual on catering spend, tier qualification, and member-level catering history reporting.

attributes.name: menu_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to menu.menu. Business justification: Catering orders are fulfilled from a specific catering menu version. Linking catering_order directly to menu enables catering menu compliance validation, pricing accuracy checks, and catering sales mi

attributes.description: Identifier of the guest or corporate account placing the catering order.

attributes.name: promotion_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to marketing.promotion. Business justification: Catering promotion tracking: catering orders frequently involve promotional pricing (group discounts, event promotions). When catering_order.guest_order_id is null, the promotion path via guest_order

attributes.description: Identifier of the sales representative or catering coordinator who managed this order.

attributes.name: site_id

attributes.name: gratuity_amount

attributes.name: lead_time_days

products.name: discount
products.description: Records all discounts, promotions, coupons, and comp adjustments applied to a guest order or individual order items. Captures discount type (coupon, loyalty redemption, employee meal, manager comp, promotional LTO, bundle/combo deal), discount amount or percentage, promotion reference, authorization employee, and pre-approved vs. exception-based flag. Supports COGS% impact analysis, promotional ROI tracking, and combo savings attribution.

attributes.name: discount_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the order discount record. Primary key.

attributes.name: authorization_employee_id
attributes.description: Reference to the employee who authorized or applied the discount, particularly for manager comps and exception-based discounts.

attributes.name: channel_id

attributes.name: financial_period_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.financial_period. Business justification: Promotional discount expense is tracked by financial period for marketing budget variance reporting and P&L accuracy. Restaurant finance teams must reconcile discount liability to the period in which

attributes.name: gl_account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Tracks discount expense against a designated GL account, supporting discount expense reporting.

attributes.name: guest_order_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent guest order to which this discount was applied.

attributes.name: menu_item_id

attributes.name: menu_lto_id

attributes.name: pos_terminal_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the POS terminal or workstation where the discount was applied.

attributes.name: profile_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to guest.profile. Business justification: Needed for discount usage analytics per guest, supporting marketing ROI analysis and compliance with promotional eligibility rules.

attributes.name: promotion_id

attributes.name: shift_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to workforce.shift. Business justification: Loss prevention and shrinkage reporting require shift-level discount audits — tracking which shift had abnormal discount volumes or unauthorized comps. Restaurant loss prevention teams run shift-level

attributes.name: site_id
attributes.description: Reference to the restaurant location where this discount was applied.

attributes.name: unit_id
attributes.description: Reference to the restaurant location where this discount was applied.

attributes.name: amount
attributes.description: Absolute monetary value of the discount applied, in the transaction currency. Used for Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) impact analysis.

attributes.name: applied_at

attributes.name: authorization_required

attributes.name: created_at

attributes.name: currency_code
