attributes.description: Free-text explanation or reason code for the discount, particularly for manager comps and exception-based adjustments.

attributes.name: revenue_impact_amount
attributes.description: The calculated impact of this discount on gross revenue for Profit and Loss (P&L) reporting and Same-Store Sales (SSS) analysis.

attributes.name: tax_treatment
attributes.description: Indicates whether the discount is applied before tax calculation, after tax calculation, or results in a tax-exempt transaction.

attributes.name: updated_at
attributes.description: The date and time when this discount record was last modified in the system.

attributes.name: valid_from_date
attributes.description: The start date from which this discount or promotion is valid and can be applied.

attributes.name: valid_to_date
attributes.description: The end date after which this discount or promotion is no longer valid.

attributes.name: void_reason
attributes.description: Explanation or reason code for why the discount was voided. Null if discount was not voided.

attributes.name: voided_at
attributes.description: The date and time when the discount was voided or removed from the order. Null if discount was not voided.

products.name: refund
products.description: Captures refund and void transactions issued against a completed or in-progress guest order. Records refund reason (wrong item, quality complaint, missing item, guest dissatisfaction), refund amount, refund method (original tender, gift card, loyalty points), authorizing manager, refund timestamp, and whether the refund was full or partial. Supports CSAT root-cause analysis and exception management.

attributes.name: refund_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the refund transaction. Primary key for the order_refund product.

attributes.name: financial_period_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.financial_period. Business justification: Refunds must be posted to the correct fiscal period for accurate P&L reporting and period-close. Restaurant controllers track refund volumes by period for loss analysis and to ensure refunds are not m

attributes.name: gl_account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Posts refund amounts to a specific GL account to reflect refund expense in financials.

attributes.name: guest_order_id
attributes.description: Reference to the original guest order against which this refund was issued. Links to the parent order transaction.

attributes.name: illness_report_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to foodsafety.illness_report. Business justification: Refunds issued as compensation for foodborne illness complaints must be linked to the illness report for financial impact analysis of food safety incidents. Finance and QA teams use this link to quant

attributes.name: order_item_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to order.order_item. Business justification: Refunds in a restaurant context are frequently item-level (e.g., wrong item, quality complaint on a specific menu item). Adding order_item_id to refund enables partial refund tracking at the line-item

attributes.name: pos_terminal_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the Point of Sale (POS) terminal or workstation where the refund was processed.

attributes.name: redemption_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to loyalty.redemption. Business justification: Refund-triggered redemption reversal: when a refund is issued on an order with a loyalty redemption, the refund must reference the redemption to trigger points restoration and redemption reversal. Thi

attributes.name: refund_employee_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the manager or supervisor who approved and authorized the refund transaction.

attributes.name: refund_guest_profile_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the guest who received the refund, if known and captured in the Customer Relationship Management (CRM) system.

attributes.name: refund_processing_employee_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the Front of House (FOH) employee who processed the refund transaction at the Point of Sale (POS).

attributes.name: refund_profile_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the guest who received the refund, if known and captured in the Customer Relationship Management (CRM) system.

attributes.name: refund_voided_by_employee_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the employee who voided or reversed the refund transaction.

attributes.name: shift_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to workforce.shift. Business justification: Shift-level refund exception reporting is a standard restaurant loss prevention control — managers review refunds processed per shift to detect fraud or policy violations. Direct shift attribution on

attributes.name: site_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the restaurant location where the refund was processed.

attributes.name: unit_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the restaurant location where the refund was processed.

attributes.name: amount
attributes.description: Total monetary value refunded to the guest, including tax adjustments.

attributes.name: approved_at
attributes.description: Date and time when the refund was approved by the authorizing manager.

attributes.name: batch_code
attributes.description: Identifier for the financial batch in which this refund was grouped for accounting reconciliation and settlement.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA Financial Accounting (FI) Batch Processing

attributes.name: channel
attributes.description: Channel through which the refund was initiated and processed, indicating the customer touchpoint.

attributes.name: created_at
attributes.description: Timestamp when the refund record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: csat_impact_flag
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether this refund is associated with a Customer Satisfaction (CSAT) incident requiring follow-up or root-cause analysis.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code indicating the currency in which the refund was issued.

attributes.name: daypart
attributes.description: Time-of-day segment during which the original order and refund occurred, used for operational and sales analysis.

attributes.name: fraud_review_notes
attributes.description: Internal notes from fraud review or investigation related to this refund transaction.

attributes.name: gl_posting_date
attributes.description: Date on which the refund transaction was posted to the General Ledger (GL) for financial reporting.

attributes.name: guest_contact_method
attributes.description: Method by which the guest communicated the refund request or complaint to the restaurant.

attributes.name: is_fraudulent
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether this refund was flagged as potentially fraudulent during review or audit.

attributes.name: is_voided
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether this refund transaction was subsequently voided or reversed.

attributes.name: loyalty_points_refunded
attributes.description: Number of loyalty program points returned to the guest as part of the refund, if applicable.

attributes.name: nps_survey_sent
attributes.business_glossary_term: Net Promoter Score (NPS) Survey Sent Flag
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether a Net Promoter Score (NPS) survey was sent to the guest following the refund.

attributes.name: order_channel
attributes.description: Service channel through which the original order was placed, retained for refund analysis by channel.

attributes.name: original_payment_method
attributes.description: Payment method used in the original order transaction, retained for reconciliation and audit purposes.

attributes.name: payment_processor_ref
attributes.description: External reference number from the payment processor for the refund transaction, used for reconciliation with payment gateway.

attributes.name: reason_code
attributes.description: Standardized code categorizing the primary reason for issuing the refund. Used for root-cause analysis and Customer Satisfaction (CSAT) improvement initiatives. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: wrong_item|quality_complaint|missing_item|guest_dissatisfaction|order_error|temperature_issue|allergy_concern|long_wait_time|incorrect_price|duplicate_charge|other — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: reason_description
attributes.description: Free-text detailed explanation of the refund reason provided by the manager or customer service representative.

attributes.name: refund_method
attributes.description: Method by which the refund value was returned to the guest, indicating the tender type used for reimbursement.

attributes.name: refund_number
attributes.description: Business-facing unique refund transaction number displayed on receipts and used for customer service tracking.

attributes.name: refund_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the refund transaction indicating processing state.

attributes.name: refund_type
attributes.description: Classification indicating whether the refund covers the entire order or only specific items.

attributes.name: refunded_at
attributes.description: Date and time when the refund transaction was completed and funds were returned to the guest.

attributes.name: requested_at
attributes.description: Date and time when the refund was initially requested by the guest or identified by staff.

attributes.name: subtotal
attributes.description: Pre-tax amount of the refund, representing the base value of items or services refunded.

attributes.name: tax_amount
attributes.description: Tax portion of the refund amount, calculated based on applicable sales tax rates.
attributes.references: IRS Publication 334 - Tax Guide for Small Business

attributes.name: third_party_delivery_provider
attributes.description: Name of the third-party delivery service provider if the original order was fulfilled through Third-Party Delivery (3PD).

attributes.name: updated_at
attributes.description: Timestamp when the refund record was last modified in the system.

attributes.name: void_reason
attributes.description: Explanation for why the refund transaction was voided or reversed.

attributes.name: voided_at
attributes.description: Date and time when the refund was voided or reversed, if applicable.

products.name: tax
products.description: Records tax line details applied to a guest order, supporting multi-jurisdiction tax compliance. Captures tax authority (federal, state, county, city), tax type (sales tax, VAT, beverage tax, bag fee), tax rate, taxable amount, tax amount, and whether the tax was included in the menu price or added at checkout. Supports financial reconciliation and regulatory tax reporting across all restaurant locations.
products.reference: Oracle MICROS Tax Detail / GAAP Tax Reporting

attributes.name: tax_id
attributes.description: Unique surrogate identifier for each tax line record applied to a guest order. Primary key for the order_tax data product in the Databricks Silver Layer.

attributes.name: financial_period_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.financial_period. Business justification: Sales tax remittance to tax authorities is period-based. Restaurant tax compliance requires aggregating tax collected by fiscal period for state/local tax filings. Period-level tax reporting is a regu

attributes.name: gl_account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Ensures tax liabilities from each order are posted to the correct tax GL account for compliance and reporting.

attributes.name: guest_order_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent guest order to which this tax line belongs. Establishes the header-to-line relationship between guest_order and order_tax.

attributes.name: order_item_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to order.order_item. Business justification: Tax records in multi-jurisdiction environments are applied at the line-item level (e.g., food vs. beverage tax rates differ by item). Adding order_item_id to tax enables item-level tax attribution, su

attributes.name: site_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the restaurant location where the order was placed. Used to resolve the applicable tax jurisdiction and for location-level financial reconciliation.

attributes.name: unit_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the restaurant location where the order was placed. Used to resolve the applicable tax jurisdiction and for location-level financial reconciliation.

attributes.name: adjusted_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX) when a post-transaction tax adjustment was applied to this line. Populated only when tax_status = 'adjusted'. Supports audit trail for tax corrections.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-TX Tax Adjustment; GAAP ASC 740

attributes.name: adjustment_reason
attributes.description: The reason code for a post-transaction tax adjustment when tax_status = 'adjusted' (e.g., rate_correction, jurisdiction_change, exemption_applied, refund, audit_correction, other). Required for audit trail and regulatory compliance.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-TX; IRS Audit Requirements; GAAP ASC 740

attributes.name: amount
attributes.description: The computed tax amount for this tax line in the transaction currency (taxable_amount × tax_rate, or as overridden by the tax engine). Core field for financial reconciliation, P&L reporting, and tax remittance.
attributes.references: GAAP ASC 606; IFRS 15; IRS Publication 334; SAP S/4HANA FI-TX

attributes.name: applied_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX) when this tax line was computed and applied to the order by the tax engine or POS system. Represents the principal business event time for this tax line record.
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS Transaction Log; SAP S/4HANA FI-TX

attributes.name: authority_code
attributes.description: Standardized code identifying the tax authority or jurisdiction as defined by the tax engine or ERP (SAP S/4HANA Tax Condition). Used for automated tax remittance and reconciliation.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA Tax Configuration; Vertex Tax Engine

attributes.name: authority_level
attributes.description: The governmental jurisdiction level that imposes this tax line. Supports multi-jurisdiction tax compliance across all restaurant locations. Values: federal, state, county, city, district, special.
attributes.references: IRS Publication 15; State Revenue Codes; Local Tax Ordinances

attributes.name: authority_name
attributes.description: The full name of the tax authority or jurisdiction imposing this tax (e.g., 'California State Board of Equalization', 'Cook County Revenue Department', 'City of Chicago'). Used for regulatory reporting and remittance.
attributes.references: State and Local Tax Authority Registries; IRS

attributes.name: cost_center_code
attributes.description: The SAP S/4HANA cost center code associated with the restaurant location for this tax line. Used for internal cost allocation, P&L reporting by location, and management accounting.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA CO-CCA Cost Center Accounting; GAAP/IFRS Management Reporting

attributes.name: country_code
attributes.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the tax jurisdiction (e.g., 'USA', 'CAN', 'GBR'). Required for international operations, VAT compliance, and cross-border tax reporting.
attributes.references: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; IFRS IAS 12; VAT Directive 2006/112/EC

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX) when this tax line record was first created in the data platform. Audit trail field for data lineage and Silver Layer ingestion tracking.
attributes.references: Internal Data Governance Standards; GAAP Audit Trail Requirements

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this tax line (e.g., 'USD', 'CAD', 'GBP'). Required for multi-currency operations and financial reporting under GAAP/IFRS.

attributes.name: daypart
attributes.description: The meal period or daypart during which the order was placed (breakfast, lunch, dinner, late_night, snack). Used for daypart-level tax and revenue analysis in foodservice operations.
attributes.references: NRA Daypart Reporting Standards; Oracle MICROS POS Daypart Configuration

attributes.name: engine_source
attributes.description: Identifies the system or engine that calculated and applied this tax line (e.g., pos = Oracle MICROS POS built-in tax, vertex = Vertex O Series, avalara = Avalara AvaTax, sap_tax = SAP S/4HANA tax engine, manual = manually entered). Used for reconciliation and audit.
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS Tax Configuration; SAP S/4HANA FI-TX; Vertex O Series

attributes.name: exemption_certificate_ref
attributes.description: Reference number or identifier of the tax exemption certificate presented by the guest or organization. Required for audit documentation when is_exempt is True. Null when no exemption applies.
attributes.references: IRS Publication 557; State Revenue Authority Exemption Certificate Requirements

attributes.name: exemption_reason
attributes.description: The reason code for a tax exemption when is_exempt is True (e.g., government, non_profit, resale, diplomatic, employee_meal, other). Required for audit trail and regulatory compliance. Null when is_exempt is False.
attributes.references: IRS Tax Exemption Guidelines; State Revenue Codes; FTC Franchise Disclosure Rules

attributes.name: is_exempt
attributes.description: Indicates whether this tax line was flagged as exempt (True) due to a guest exemption certificate, government order, or qualifying non-profit status. When True, tax_amount should be zero and exemption_reason should be populated.
attributes.references: IRS Tax Exemption Guidelines; State Revenue Codes; USDA Exemption Rules

attributes.name: is_refunded
attributes.description: Indicates whether the tax amount on this line has been refunded to the guest (True) as part of an order refund or cancellation. Used for refund reconciliation and tax reversal tracking.
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS Refund Processing; SAP S/4HANA FI-TX Reversal; GAAP ASC 606

attributes.name: is_tax_inclusive
attributes.description: Indicates whether this tax was already included in the menu price (True) or added at checkout on top of the menu price (False). Critical for VAT/GST jurisdictions and for accurate revenue recognition and tax remittance calculations.

attributes.name: line_sequence
attributes.description: Sequential number ordering multiple tax lines within the same order (e.g., 1 = state sales tax, 2 = county tax, 3 = city beverage tax). Ensures deterministic ordering for receipt display and financial reporting.
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS Tax Line Sequencing; NRA Receipt Standards

attributes.name: order_channel
attributes.description: The service channel through which the order was placed (pos = in-store Point of Sale, drive_thru = Drive-Thru (DT), olo = Online Ordering (OLO), third_party_delivery = Third-Party Delivery (3PD), kiosk = self-service kiosk, catering). Used for channel-level tax analysis and compliance.
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS; Olo Digital Ordering Platform; NRA Channel Reporting Standards

attributes.name: original_tax_amount
attributes.description: The tax amount as originally computed at the time of order placement, before any post-transaction adjustments. Populated when tax_status = 'adjusted' to preserve the audit trail of the original calculation.
attributes.references: GAAP ASC 740; IFRS IAS 12; IRS Audit Requirements

attributes.name: period_date
attributes.description: The business date (yyyy-MM-dd) to which this tax line is attributed for tax remittance and financial period reporting. May differ from the transaction timestamp for end-of-day batch processing or period adjustments.
attributes.references: GAAP ASC 740; IFRS IAS 12; IRS Tax Period Rules

attributes.name: pos_tax_line_ref
attributes.business_glossary_term: Point of Sale (POS) Tax Line Reference
attributes.description: The native tax line identifier or sequence number assigned by Oracle MICROS POS for this tax entry. Used for cross-system reconciliation between the POS transaction log and the lakehouse Silver Layer.
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS Tax Transaction Log

attributes.name: rate
attributes.description: The applicable tax rate expressed as a decimal fraction (e.g., 0.0875 for 8.75%). Sourced from the tax engine or POS tax configuration at the time of the transaction. Used for tax amount validation and audit.
attributes.references: State and Local Tax Authority Rate Tables; SAP S/4HANA Tax Condition Records

attributes.name: refund_amount
attributes.description: The tax amount refunded to the guest when is_refunded is True. May be a partial refund (less than tax_amount) for partial order refunds. Used for financial reconciliation and tax reversal reporting.
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS Refund Processing; SAP S/4HANA FI-TX; GAAP ASC 606

attributes.name: remittance_period
attributes.description: The fiscal period (e.g., '2024-Q1', '2024-03') in which this tax amount is scheduled for remittance to the tax authority. Used for tax filing calendar management and period-end close.
attributes.references: IRS Tax Filing Calendar; State Revenue Authority Filing Schedules; GAAP ASC 740

attributes.name: remittance_status
attributes.description: Indicates whether this tax amount has been remitted to the applicable tax authority (pending = not yet remitted, remitted = successfully paid to authority, not_applicable = exempt or zero-rate, failed = remittance attempt failed). Supports tax compliance tracking.
attributes.references: IRS Tax Remittance Requirements; State Revenue Authority Filing Rules; SAP S/4HANA FI-TX

attributes.name: sap_tax_document_ref
attributes.description: The SAP S/4HANA FI-TX tax document number associated with this tax line for GL posting and tax remittance. Enables end-to-end traceability from POS transaction to ERP financial posting.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-TX Tax Document; GAAP/IFRS Financial Reporting Standards

attributes.name: source_system
attributes.description: The operational system of record from which this tax line record originated (micros_pos = Oracle MICROS POS, sap_s4hana = SAP S/4HANA FI-TX, olo = Olo Digital Ordering Platform, manual = manually entered). Used for data lineage and reconciliation.
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS; SAP S/4HANA; Olo Digital Ordering Platform; Internal Data Lineage Standards

attributes.name: state_code
attributes.description: Two-letter US state code (or equivalent provincial code) of the tax jurisdiction where the restaurant is located (e.g., 'IL', 'CA', 'TX'). Used for state-level tax rate lookup, remittance routing, and regulatory reporting.
attributes.references: ANSI INCITS 38; IRS State Tax Authority Codes; State Revenue Departments

attributes.name: tax_code
attributes.description: The internal or POS-assigned tax code identifying the specific tax rule applied (e.g., 'TX_CA_SALES', 'TX_CHI_BEV'). Maps to the tax condition record in SAP S/4HANA and Oracle MICROS POS tax configuration.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA Tax Condition Records; Oracle MICROS POS Tax Configuration

attributes.name: tax_name
attributes.description: Human-readable name of the tax as displayed on the guest receipt or financial report (e.g., 'Illinois Sales Tax', 'Chicago Beverage Tax', 'CA Bag Fee'). Required for receipt compliance and guest transparency.
attributes.references: NRA Receipt Compliance Guidelines; State Consumer Protection Laws

attributes.name: tax_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of this tax line record. 'applied' = tax successfully computed and applied; 'voided' = tax line cancelled due to order void; 'adjusted' = tax amount corrected post-transaction; 'pending' = awaiting tax engine resolution; 'reversed' = tax reversed for refund.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-TX Tax Document Status; Oracle MICROS POS Void Processing

attributes.name: tax_type
attributes.description: The category of tax applied to this line (e.g., sales_tax, vat, beverage_tax, bag_fee, excise_tax, service_tax). Supports differentiated tax reporting and compliance across jurisdictions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sales_tax|vat|beverage_tax|bag_fee|excise_tax|service_tax|surcharge|environmental_fee — promote to reference product if additional types are needed]
attributes.references: FDA Food Labeling Regulations; State Revenue Codes; IRS Publication 334

attributes.name: taxable_amount
attributes.description: The portion of the order subtotal that is subject to this specific tax line, expressed in the transaction currency. Excludes non-taxable items, exempt amounts, and tax-inclusive components. Used for tax calculation validation.
attributes.references: GAAP ASC 606; IRS Publication 334; State Revenue Codes

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX) when this tax line record was last modified in the data platform. Used for change data capture (CDC), incremental loads, and audit trail.
attributes.references: Internal Data Governance Standards; Databricks Delta Lake CDC

attributes.name: voided_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX) when this tax line was voided, if applicable. Populated only when tax_status = 'voided'. Used for void audit trail and financial reversal processing.
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS Void Processing; SAP S/4HANA FI-TX Reversal

products.name: delivery_platform
products.description: Master reference table for delivery_platform. Referenced by delivery_platform_id.

attributes.name: delivery_platform_id
attributes.description: Primary key for delivery_platform

attributes.name: commission_gl_account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Delivery platform commissions (e.g., DoorDash, Uber Eats fees) are accrued to a specific commission expense GL account. Restaurant finance teams post platform commission accruals by GL account for exp

attributes.name: parent_delivery_platform_id
attributes.description: Self-referencing FK on delivery_platform (parent_delivery_platform_id)

attributes.name: average_delivery_time_minutes
attributes.description: Typical time in minutes from order acceptance to delivery completion for this platform.

attributes.name: contract_end_date
attributes.description: Date when the contractual agreement with the platform expires or is terminated (nullable for open‑ended contracts).

attributes.name: contract_start_date
attributes.description: Date when the contractual agreement with the platform became effective.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the delivery platform record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: delivery_platform_code
attributes.description: Short alphanumeric code used to reference the platform in internal systems.

attributes.name: delivery_platform_name
attributes.description: Official name of the delivery platform (e.g., DoorDash, UberEats).

attributes.name: delivery_platform_status
attributes.description: Current operational status of the delivery platform.

attributes.name: fee_percentage
attributes.description: Standard commission fee charged by the platform as a percentage of order value.

attributes.name: integration_method
attributes.description: Technical method used to exchange data with the platform.

attributes.name: last_updated_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp of the most recent update to the delivery platform record.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free‑form field for any supplemental information or remarks about the platform.

attributes.name: onboarding_date
attributes.description: Date the platform was first integrated and made available for order routing.

attributes.name: platform_type
attributes.description: Classification of the platform based on ownership and integration model.

attributes.name: support_contact_email
attributes.description: Primary email address for technical or operational support contacts at the platform.

attributes.name: support_phone_number
attributes.description: Primary phone number for technical or operational support contacts at the platform.

domains.name: supply
domains.description: Manages end-to-end food and non-food supply chain including supplier master data, vendor management, sourcing, purchase orders, inbound logistics, distribution center operations, and ingredient traceability. Tracks COGS, supplier performance, contract compliance, and spend analytics via Coupa Procurement.
domains.references: ISO 22000, USDA Grading Standards, GMP, Coupa Procurement

products.name: supplier
products.description: Master record for every supplier and vendor in the foodservice supply chain, including food and non-food suppliers, distributors, co-manufacturers, and their key contacts. Captures supplier identity, classification (broadline, specialty, local), approval status, diversity certification, payment terms, lead times, regulatory compliance status (FDA, USDA, HACCP), and primary/secondary contact information. SSOT for supplier identity and contact details across supply chain operations. Sourced from Coupa Procurement supplier master and SAP S/4HANA vendor master (MM).
products.reference: SAP Vendor Master (LFA1), Coupa Supplier Record

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the supply_supplier data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).

attributes.name: default_gl_account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Vendor master records carry a default GL account for automated AP posting — standard in foodservice ERP (SAP, Oracle). Role-prefixed 'default_' because a supplier may have multiple GL contexts; this i

attributes.name: legal_entity_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Suppliers are associated with a legal entity in the vendor master for tax reporting (1099, VAT), payment routing, and intercompany transaction identification. Standard in foodservice ERP vendor master

products.name: ingredient
products.description: Master catalog of all food ingredients, raw materials, beverages, and packaging SKUs procured across the foodservice supply chain. Captures SKU code, ingredient name, commodity category, unit of measure, allergen flags (Big 9), USDA grade, country of origin, shelf life days, storage temperature requirements (ambient/refrigerated/frozen), and HACCP critical control classification. Serves as the supply-side item master linking to menu domain BOM for recipe costing. SSOT for ingredient identity across supply, inventory, and menu domains. Sourced from SAP MM material master and MarketMan Inventory Management.

attributes.name: ingredient_id
attributes.description: System-generated surrogate key uniquely identifying each ingredient record.

attributes.name: gl_account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Ingredients are mapped to GL accounts for inventory valuation and COGS posting (food cost GL vs. packaging GL). Foodservice cost accountants expect each ingredient to carry a GL account for automated

attributes.name: haccp_plan_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to foodsafety.haccp_plan. Business justification: Required for HACCP compliance: each ingredient must be linked to its HACCP plan for safety documentation and audit reporting.

attributes.name: allergen_flags
attributes.description: Pipe‑separated list of allergens present in the ingredient.

attributes.name: carbohydrate_content_percent
attributes.description: Percentage of carbohydrates by weight in the ingredient.

attributes.name: country_of_origin
attributes.description: Three‑letter country code indicating where the ingredient was produced or sourced.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Date‑time when the ingredient record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three‑letter ISO currency code for cost_per_unit (e.g., USD, EUR).

attributes.name: effective_from
attributes.description: Date when the ingredient becomes available for ordering.

attributes.name: effective_until
attributes.description: Date after which the ingredient is no longer available (null if indefinite).

attributes.name: fat_content_percent
attributes.description: Percentage of total fat by weight in the ingredient.

attributes.name: haccp_classification
attributes.description: Risk level of the ingredient according to HACCP guidelines.

attributes.name: halal_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the ingredient meets halal certification requirements.

attributes.name: ingredient_category
attributes.description: Broad classification of the ingredient for sourcing, menu engineering, and cost analysis.

attributes.name: ingredient_code
attributes.description: Business code or SKU assigned to the ingredient for ordering and inventory tracking.

attributes.name: ingredient_name
attributes.description: Descriptive name of the ingredient as used in menus, procurement, and reporting.
attributes.references: Ingredient master data source: SAP MM Material Master

attributes.name: ingredient_status
attributes.description: Current operational status of the ingredient in the catalog.

attributes.name: inspection_status
attributes.description: Result of the most recent inspection.

attributes.name: kosher_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the ingredient meets kosher certification requirements.

attributes.name: last_inspection_date
attributes.description: Date of the most recent food safety inspection for this ingredient.

attributes.name: lead_time_days
attributes.description: Typical number of days from order placement to receipt of the ingredient.

attributes.name: non_gmo_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the ingredient is verified as non‑genetically modified.

attributes.name: nutritional_calories_per_unit
attributes.description: Energy content per unit of the ingredient, expressed in kilocalories.

attributes.name: organic_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the ingredient is certified organic.

attributes.name: packaging_type
attributes.description: Standard packaging format for the ingredient.

attributes.name: par_level
attributes.description: Periodic Automatic Replenishment Level – minimum inventory quantity to trigger re‑order.

attributes.name: protein_content_percent
attributes.description: Percentage of protein by weight in the ingredient.

attributes.name: shelf_life_days

products.name: purchase_order
products.description: Core transactional record for every purchase order issued to suppliers for food ingredients, beverages, packaging, and non-food supplies. Captures PO number, supplier reference, order date, requested delivery date, ship-to distribution center or restaurant, total PO value, currency, payment terms, approval status, and sourcing event linkage. Represents the contractual commitment to buy. Sourced from Coupa Procurement PO module and SAP S/4HANA MM purchasing.

attributes.description: Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Procurement audit trail and PO approval workflows require tracking which employee created/approved each purchase order. Foodservice procurement teams assign designated buyers per supplier or category.

attributes.name: financial_period_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.financial_period. Business justification: POs are accrued into financial periods for food cost reporting and period-end close. Foodservice operators require period-level PO accrual reporting to manage COGS and inventory cut-off accurately.

attributes.name: franchisee_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to franchise.franchisee. Business justification: Purchase orders are issued by individual franchisees; linking enables franchise‑level cost allocation and procurement performance reports.

attributes.name: gl_account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: POs are GL-coded at creation for food cost accrual and three-way match in foodservice ERP systems. A domain expert expects every PO to carry a GL account for automated journal posting at goods receipt

attributes.name: ingredient_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supply.ingredient. Business justification: Each purchase order line orders a specific ingredient or SKU. purchase_order_line must reference ingredient to identify what is being procured. This links transactional procurement data to the ingredi

attributes.name: legal_entity_id

attributes.name: nro_project_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to realestate.nro_project. Business justification: NRO first-fill purchase orders: new restaurant openings require dedicated opening-inventory POs coordinated with the NRO project schedule and capex budget. Linking POs to the NRO project enables openi

attributes.name: site_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to realestate.site. Business justification: Internal purchase‑order entity must be associated with the restaurant site it serves for accurate inventory and financial reconciliation.

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supply.supply_supplier. Business justification: A purchase order is issued to a specific supplier. supply_purchase_order must reference supply_supplier to identify which vendor the PO was sent to. This is a foundational procurement relationship — w

attributes.name: unit_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to restaurant.unit. Business justification: PURCHASE_ORDER creation is done per restaurant location; linking PO to unit enables inventory budgeting and location‑specific spend reporting.

products.name: goods_receipt
products.description: Transactional record of inbound goods received at a distribution center or restaurant, including both header-level receipt information and line-level detail per SKU. Header captures receipt date/time, receiving location, PO reference, receiving condition, and inspector ID. Lines capture specific ingredient/SKU received, quantity accepted/rejected, lot number, expiration date, temperature at receipt (cold chain compliance), storage location assigned, and variance from PO quantity. Critical for three-way match (PO-receipt-invoice), HACCP traceability, and ingredient-level lot tracking. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA MM (MIGO) and MarketMan receiving module.

Feeds SAP S/4HANA AP (FI-AP) for payment processing and COGS accrual. Sourced from Coupa invoice management and SAP S/4HANA FI.

products.name: supplier_contract
products.description: Master record for negotiated supply agreements and their associated price schedules. Captures contract number, effective and expiration dates, volume commitments, rebate agreements, exclusivity terms, renewal type, and compliance status. Includes contracted unit prices per ingredient/SKU with validity periods, volume tiers, and price types (fixed, indexed, market-based). Used by supply chain for price validation during goods receipt and invoice matching, and for COGS variance analysis. Sourced from Coupa contract management module.

products.name: contract_price
products.description: Contracted unit price records tied to a supplier contract for specific ingredients or SKUs over a defined validity period. Captures ingredient/SKU reference, contracted unit price, currency, price validity start and end dates, price tier (volume break), and price type (fixed, indexed, market-based). Enables COGS% variance analysis against actual invoice prices and supports menu costing in the menu domain.
products.reference: SAP Info Record (EINE), Coupa Contract Price Line

attributes.name: contract_price_id
attributes.description: System-generated unique identifier for the contract price record.

attributes.name: financial_period_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.financial_period. Business justification: Contract prices are effective within financial periods for budget vs. actual food cost variance analysis. Foodservice finance teams require period-level contract price tracking to reconcile contracted

attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supply.ingredient. Business justification: A contract price record defines the negotiated unit price for a specific ingredient or SKU. contract_price must reference ingredient to identify which item the price applies to. This enables price com

attributes.name: stock_item_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the ingredient or stock keeping unit covered by this price.

attributes.name: supplier_contract_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supply.supplier_contract. Business justification: contract_price is the line-level price schedule record under a supplier_contract header. This is the canonical header-line relationship for contract pricing. contract_price must reference supplier_con

attributes.name: contract_price_code
attributes.description: Business identifier or code assigned to the contract price (e.g., CP-2024-001).

attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the price record.

attributes.description: Date‑time when the price record was initially created in the source system.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the price (e.g., USD, EUR).

attributes.name: default_price
attributes.description: Baseline price per unit (SKU) when no volume tier applies. [Moved from supplier_contract: default_price on supplier_contract represents a baseline price per SKU — but in a contract covering multiple ingredients, each ingredient has its own contracted price. This attribute is more accurately represented as contracted_unit_price on the contract_price association, where it is scoped to a specific contract-ingredient combination. Keeping it on supplier_contract implies a single price for the entire contract, which misrepresents multi-SKU contracts.]

attributes.name: effective_from
attributes.description: Date when the contracted price becomes binding.

attributes.name: effective_until
attributes.description: Date when the contracted price expires (null if open‑ended).

attributes.name: is_current
attributes.description: Indicates whether this price record is the currently active price for the SKU.

attributes.name: price_amount
attributes.description: Numeric value of the contracted unit price.

attributes.name: price_change_reason
attributes.description: Free‑text explanation for why the price was changed or created.

attributes.name: price_index_reference

attributes.name: price_tier_max_qty

attributes.name: price_tier_min_qty
attributes.description: Lower bound quantity for this price tier (volume break).

attributes.name: price_type
attributes.description: Classification of the price calculation method.

attributes.name: price_uom
attributes.description: Unit of measure for the default price (e.g., per unit, per case). [Moved from supplier_contract: price_uom on supplier_contract is the UOM for the default_price, which is ingredient-specific. Since each ingredient in a contract may have a different UOM (e.g., beef by lb, buns by case), this attribute belongs on the contract_price association as unit_of_measure, scoped to the specific contract-ingredient price line.]

products.name: distribution_center
products.description: Master record for each distribution center (DC), warehouse, and logistics node in the foodservice supply network including company-owned DCs, third-party logistics (3PL) facilities, cross-dock locations, and commissary kitchens. Captures DC code, name, full address, geographic region/market, facility type (ambient, refrigerated, frozen, mixed-temp), total storage capacity (pallet positions), operating hours, supported restaurant count, primary and backup DC assignments, and facility manager. SSOT for DC identity used across inbound logistics, inventory allocation, and restaurant replenishment. Sourced from SAP EWM and network design tools.

Captures recall reference number, recall class (Class I, II, III), affected ingredient/SKU, affected lot numbers, recall reason (contamination, allergen mislabeling, foreign material), recall initiation date, affected DC and restaurant locations, quantity recalled, disposal method, and regulatory notification status. Enables rapid traceability response using ingredient_lot linkage.

products.name: approved_vendor_item
products.description: This association product represents the sourcing contract between a supplier and an ingredient in the foodservice supply chain. It captures the Approved Vendor List (AVL) entry that procurement teams actively manage — recording which suppliers are approved to provide which ingredients, at what price, under what terms, and for what time period. Each record links one supply_supplier to one ingredient with attributes that exist only in the context of this supplier-ingredient sourcing relationship, enabling multi-sourcing strategy, price benchmarking, and supply continuity planning.. Existence Justification: In foodservice procurement, a supplier provides many ingredients (e.g., a broadline distributor like Sysco supplies beef, lettuce, cooking oil, and packaging), and a single ingredient (e.g., ground beef) is sourced from multiple approved suppliers at different prices, lead times, and minimum order quantities. This relationship is actively managed by procurement teams as an Approved Vendor List (AVL) or Supplier Item Catalog — a recognized operational business concept with its own lifecycle (approval, pricing updates, deactivation). The relationship carries substantial data (unit_price, lead_time_days, min_order_quantity, preferred_supplier_flag, effective_from, effective_until) that belongs to neither the supplier master nor the ingredient master alone.

attributes.name: approved_vendor_item_id
attributes.business_glossary_term: Approved Vendor Item - Approved Vendor Item Id
attributes.description: Primary key for the approved_vendor_item association

attributes.name: ingredient_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to the ingredient or SKU being sourced from this supplier.

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.business_glossary_term: Approved Vendor Item - Supply Supplier Id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to the approved supplier in the supply_supplier master record.

attributes.name: approval_status
attributes.description: Current approval status of this supplier for this specific ingredient on the Approved Vendor List. Managed by procurement and food safety teams.

attributes.name: cost_per_unit
attributes.description: Standard purchase cost for one unit of the ingredient in the specified currency. [Moved from ingredient: Purchase cost per unit is negotiated per supplier-ingredient combination. Storing it on the ingredient master implies a single price, which is incorrect when multiple suppliers offer the same ingredient at different prices. It belongs on the approved_vendor_item association as unit_price.]

attributes.name: effective_from
attributes.description: Date on which this supplier-ingredient sourcing agreement becomes active and the supplier is approved to fulfill orders for this ingredient.

attributes.name: effective_until
attributes.description: Date after which this supplier-ingredient sourcing agreement expires and the supplier is no longer approved for this ingredient. Null if the agreement is open-ended.

attributes.name: lead_time_days
attributes.description: Number of days from order placement to receipt of this ingredient from this specific supplier. Supplier-specific and belongs to the sourcing relationship, not the ingredient master.

attributes.name: min_order_quantity
attributes.description: Minimum number of units that must be ordered per purchase order for this supplier-ingredient combination. A contractual term that belongs to the sourcing relationship.

attributes.name: preferred_supplier_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this supplier is the designated primary (preferred) source for this ingredient. Used in multi-sourcing strategy to determine default sourcing decisions.

attributes.name: unit_price
attributes.description: Negotiated purchase price per unit of the ingredient from this specific supplier. Varies by supplier-ingredient combination and cannot reside on either master record.

domains.name: inventory
domains.description: Manages BOH stock levels, SKU tracking, PAR levels (Periodic Automatic Replenishment), waste tracking (Waste%), yield management, receiving, transfers, physical counts, and replenishment orders via MarketMan. Supports COGS% optimization and food cost control across all restaurant units.
domains.references: HACCP, ISO 22000, MarketMan Inventory Management SOP

products.name: stock_item
products.description: Master record for every SKU tracked in restaurant inventory — food, beverage, packaging, and non-food supplies. Captures SKU code, item name, unit of measure (UOM), item category (protein, produce, dry goods, beverage, paper goods, cleaning), storage class (ambient, refrigerated, frozen), reorder point, reorder quantity, standard cost, vendor item code, shelf life days, HACCP temperature range, allergen flags, daypart applicability, seasonal adjustment flags, and active status. Also owns PAR-level configuration per unit-location: PAR quantity, minimum quantity (reorder point), maximum quantity (shelf capacity), day-of-week overrides, seasonal adjustment flags, and effective date range. This is the SSOT for all stockable items and their replenishment parameters managed through MarketMan.

attributes.name: stock_item_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the stock item. Primary key for the stock_item product.

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Allows allocation of inventory holding costs to specific cost centers for budgeting and variance analysis.

attributes.name: gl_account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Required for financial reporting of inventory valuation and COGS per item, enabling accurate GL posting of stock costs.

attributes.name: haccp_plan_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to foodsafety.haccp_plan. Business justification: Each stock item (ingredient) is governed by a specific HACCP plan defining its safe handling, temperature limits, and critical controls. stock_item already stores haccp_max_temp_f/haccp_min_temp_f. Li

attributes.name: ingredient_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supply.ingredient. Business justification: REQUIRED: Ingredient Inventory Valuation Report needs each stock item linked to its ingredient master for nutrition, allergen, and compliance tracking.

attributes.name: uom_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to inventory.uom. Business justification: stock_item.unit_of_measure is a STRING column that denotes the storage/base unit of measure for the SKU. The uom table is the authoritative UOM reference master in this domain. Adding uom_id FK normal

attributes.description: Primary classification of the stock item by food service category. Used for inventory organization, COGS analysis, and procurement planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: protein|produce|dairy|dry_goods|beverage|paper_goods|cleaning|packaging — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.description: Detailed description of the stock item including brand, size, packaging, and other distinguishing characteristics.

attributes.description: Human-readable name of the stock item as it appears in inventory management and ordering systems.

attributes.description: Date and time when the stock item record was last updated. Used for change tracking and audit trails.

attributes.description: Target inventory quantity that should be maintained at all times to support operational needs without overstocking. Used for automated reorder triggers.

attributes.description: Inventory threshold quantity that triggers a replenishment order. Set below PAR level to account for lead time.

attributes.name: reorder_quantity
attributes.description: Standard quantity to order when inventory falls below reorder point. Calculated to restore inventory to PAR level.

attributes.description: Number of days the item remains usable after receipt. Critical for waste management, FIFO rotation, and food safety compliance.

attributes.name: yield_percentage
attributes.description: Percentage of usable product after preparation and trimming. Critical for recipe costing and waste tracking.

products.name: stock_location

Captures location code, location name, location type (refrigerated, frozen, ambient, bar), temperature zone, capacity (cubic feet or shelf count), restaurant unit reference, and active status. Enables granular stock-on-hand tracking by storage zone.

attributes.name: location_name

attributes.name: location_type
attributes.description: Classification of the storage location based on its primary function and environmental control (refrigerated, frozen, ambient, bar, prep_area, service_station).

attributes.name: next_scheduled_cycle_count_date

attributes.references: MarketMan Inventory Management - Cycle Count Schedule

attributes.name: next_scheduled_maintenance_date

attributes.name: storage_area_type

attributes.description: Indicates whether the location is in Back of House (BOH) or Front of House (FOH) area of the restaurant.
