products.name: on_hand_balance
products.description: Current stock-on-hand snapshot for each SKU at each storage location within a restaurant unit. Captures quantity on hand, quantity reserved (committed to prep), quantity available, last physical count date, last adjustment date, last received date, unit cost, extended value, and variance from PAR level. Updated by receiving, transfers, waste events, and physical counts. The authoritative real-time inventory position record used for replenishment decisions and food cost reporting.

attributes.name: on_hand_balance_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the on-hand balance record. Primary key for the inventory position snapshot.

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Links on‑hand quantities to cost centers to reflect inventory value in financial statements per cost center.

attributes.name: facility_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to realestate.facility. Business justification: On‑hand snapshots need facility context for real‑estate cost allocation, insurance valuation, and property‑level inventory audits.

attributes.name: financial_period_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.financial_period. Business justification: On-hand balance snapshots are taken at period end for inventory valuation on the balance sheet. Linking to financial_period enables period-over-period inventory value comparison and supports COGS calc

attributes.name: franchisee_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to franchise.franchisee. Business justification: FRANCHISEE‑LEVEL INVENTORY DASHBOARD aggregates on‑hand balances per franchisee for performance and royalty calculations.

attributes.name: primary_on_restaurant_unit_id
attributes.description: Reference to the restaurant unit where this inventory is held. Links to the restaurant operations master.

attributes.name: primary_on_sku_stock_item_id
attributes.description: Reference to the SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) for which this balance is recorded. Links to the inventory item master.

attributes.name: stock_location_id
attributes.description: Reference to the specific storage location within the restaurant (walk-in cooler, dry storage, freezer, prep area). Links to storage location master.

attributes.name: uom_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to inventory.uom. Business justification: on_hand_balance.unit_of_measure is a STRING column denoting the UOM for the on-hand quantity snapshot. The uom table is the authoritative UOM reference master. Adding uom_id FK normalizes this referen

attributes.name: abc_classification
attributes.description: The ABC inventory classification based on value and usage velocity. A items are high-value/high-velocity requiring tight control, C items are low-value/low-velocity.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which unit_cost and extended_value are denominated.

attributes.name: cycle_count_frequency
attributes.description: The frequency at which this SKU should be physically counted as part of the cycle count program. Typically aligned with ABC classification.

attributes.name: days_until_expiration
attributes.description: The number of days remaining until the expiration date. Used to prioritize usage and identify items at risk of waste.

attributes.name: expiration_date
attributes.description: The date by which this inventory must be used or discarded. Critical for food safety, waste management, and FIFO (First In First Out) rotation.

attributes.name: extended_value
attributes.description: The total dollar value of the on-hand inventory, calculated as quantity_on_hand multiplied by unit_cost. Used for financial reporting and COGS% analysis.

attributes.name: inventory_status
attributes.description: The current lifecycle status of this inventory position. Determines whether the inventory is available for use, held for quality review, or flagged for disposal.

attributes.name: is_perishable
attributes.description: Indicates whether this SKU is a perishable item requiring temperature control and expiration tracking. True for fresh produce, dairy, meat; false for dry goods.

attributes.name: last_adjustment_date
attributes.description: The date when the last inventory adjustment (waste, transfer, or correction) was recorded for this SKU at this location.

attributes.name: last_movement_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time of the last inventory transaction (receipt, transfer, issue, or adjustment) that affected this balance. Used to identify stagnant inventory.

attributes.name: last_physical_count_date
attributes.description: The date when the last physical inventory count was performed for this SKU at this location. Used to track audit compliance and inventory accuracy.

attributes.name: last_received_date
attributes.description: The date when this SKU was last received into inventory at this location. Used to track inventory freshness and identify slow-moving items.

attributes.name: lot_number
attributes.description: The supplier or manufacturer lot number for traceability and recall management. Critical for food safety compliance under HACCP (Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points).

attributes.name: par_level
attributes.description: The target inventory level for this SKU at this location, used for automatic replenishment decisions. Represents the optimal stock level to maintain service without excess waste.

attributes.name: quantity_available
attributes.description: The net quantity available for use or sale, calculated as quantity_on_hand minus quantity_reserved. Represents the true available-to-promise inventory.

attributes.name: quantity_on_hand
attributes.description: The total physical quantity of the SKU currently in stock at this location. Represents the gross inventory position before reservations.

attributes.name: quantity_reserved
attributes.description: The quantity of the SKU that is committed to prep, production, or pending orders and not available for new allocation. Used in BOH (Back of House) prep planning.

attributes.name: record_created_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this on-hand balance record was first created in the data platform. Audit trail for data lineage.

attributes.name: record_updated_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this on-hand balance record was last updated in the data platform. Audit trail for change tracking.

attributes.name: reorder_point
attributes.description: The inventory level at which a replenishment order should be triggered. When quantity_available falls below this threshold, the system generates a purchase order.

attributes.name: safety_stock
attributes.description: The minimum buffer stock maintained to protect against stockouts due to demand variability or supply delays. Part of the PAR level calculation.

attributes.name: sku_code
attributes.description: The business identifier code for the SKU. Denormalized from SKU master for reporting convenience.

attributes.name: sku_description
attributes.description: Human-readable description of the SKU. Denormalized from SKU master for reporting convenience.

attributes.name: snapshot_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this on-hand balance snapshot was captured. Represents the point-in-time inventory position for reporting and reconciliation.

attributes.name: source_system
attributes.description: The operational system that provided this inventory balance record (MarketMan Inventory Management, SAP Materials Management, manual physical count, or POS integration).

attributes.name: temperature_zone
attributes.description: The required storage temperature zone for this SKU (ambient/dry, refrigerated, or frozen). Critical for food safety compliance and storage location assignment.

attributes.name: unit_cost
attributes.description: The cost per unit of measure for this SKU at this location. Used to calculate extended inventory value and COGS (Cost of Goods Sold).

attributes.name: valuation_method
attributes.description: The accounting method used to value this inventory (FIFO - First In First Out, LIFO - Last In First Out, weighted average, or standard cost). Determines COGS calculation.

attributes.name: variance_from_par
attributes.business_glossary_term: Variance from Periodic Automatic Replenishment (PAR) Level
attributes.description: The difference between quantity_on_hand and par_level. Positive values indicate overstocking, negative values indicate understocking and trigger replenishment.

products.name: receiving_order
products.description: Records the receipt of goods delivered to a restaurant unit from a supplier or distribution center, capturing both delivery-level header information and line-level SKU detail in a single consolidated entity. Header attributes: purchase order reference, delivery date/time, supplier reference, driver name, invoice number, total received value, receiving status (pending/partial/complete/rejected), temperature check result, seal integrity check, and receiving manager reference. Line attributes (one per SKU received): stock item reference, ordered vs received vs rejected quantities, unit of measure, unit cost, extended cost, lot number, expiration date, temperature at receipt (°F/°C), condition code (acceptable/damaged/short-dated/rejected), and variance reason code. Links to MarketMan purchase order and SAP S/4HANA goods receipt. Drives on-hand balance updates, COGS% tracking, and supplier performance measurement.

attributes.name: receiving_order_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the receiving order record. Primary key for inbound inventory transactions at restaurant unit level.

attributes.name: ap_invoice_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.ap_invoice. Business justification: 3-way match (PO + goods receipt + AP invoice) is a standard AP control in restaurant foodservice. Linking receiving_order to ap_invoice enables automated invoice matching, dispute resolution, and supp

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Receiving transactions are posted to a cost center for food cost allocation and budget variance reporting. Restaurant operators track receiving costs by cost center to manage food cost percent targets

attributes.name: facility_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to realestate.facility. Business justification: Receiving dock assignment per facility supports labor‑cost tracking and health‑inspection compliance at each restaurant location.

attributes.name: financial_period_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.financial_period. Business justification: Received goods must be posted to the correct fiscal period for COGS accrual and inventory valuation. Period-end close in restaurant finance requires all receiving transactions to be period-stamped. No

attributes.name: franchisee_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to franchise.franchisee. Business justification: Franchisee receiving reports track supplier deliveries per franchisee to reconcile costs against royalty fees.

attributes.name: goods_receipt_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supply.goods_receipt. Business justification: Goods receipt to inventory posting reconciliation is a core foodservice receiving control. The receiving_order (inventory posting) must reference the supply-side goods_receipt (physical inspection rec

attributes.name: haccp_plan_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to foodsafety.haccp_plan. Business justification: Receiving is a critical HACCP control point. Each receiving order must be processed per the applicable HACCP plan governing temperature checks, seal integrity, and quality inspection. receiving_order

attributes.name: inbound_shipment_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supply.inbound_shipment. Business justification: Delivery performance tracking requires linking the inventory receiving event to the logistics shipment record. Foodservice operators compare scheduled vs actual arrival (inbound_shipment) against what

attributes.name: purchase_order_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supply.supply_purchase_order. Business justification: Three-way match (PO → receipt → invoice) is a fundamental AP control in foodservice procurement. Linking receiving_order to supply_purchase_order enables PO fulfillment reporting and quantity variance

attributes.name: receiving_employee_id
attributes.description: Employee identifier of the restaurant manager or BOH staff member who inspected and accepted the delivery. Links to Workday HCM employee master.

attributes.name: receiving_manager_employee_id
attributes.description: Employee identifier of the restaurant manager or BOH staff member who inspected and accepted the delivery. Links to Workday HCM employee master.

attributes.name: receiving_restaurant_unit_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the restaurant unit receiving the goods. Links to restaurant operations master data.

attributes.name: receiving_unit_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the restaurant unit receiving the goods. Links to restaurant operations master data.

attributes.name: replenishment_order_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to inventory.replenishment_order. Business justification: A receiving_order is the physical fulfillment event for a replenishment_order. Linking receiving_order.replenishment_order_id → replenishment_order.replenishment_order_id closes the procurement loop:

attributes.name: shift_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to workforce.shift. Business justification: Receiving orders occur during specific shifts; shift-level receiving reports (delivery accuracy, temperature compliance by shift) are standard foodservice operational controls. receiving_shift is a pl

attributes.name: stock_location_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_location. Business justification: receiving_order.receiving_location is a free-text STRING denoting where goods were received within the restaurant unit. Replacing it with a FK to stock_location.stock_location_id normalizes this refer

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: System timestamp when the receiving order record was first created in MarketMan or source system. Audit trail for data lineage.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the received value (e.g., USD, CAD, EUR). Supports multi-currency operations for international franchise units.

attributes.name: days_variance
attributes.description: Calculated difference in days between expected and actual delivery date. Positive values indicate late delivery; negative values indicate early delivery. Used for supplier scorecard.

attributes.name: delivery_date
attributes.description: Calendar date when the goods were delivered to the restaurant unit. Used for inventory aging and FIFO tracking.

attributes.name: delivery_note_number
attributes.description: Packing slip or delivery note number provided by the supplier. Cross-reference document for reconciliation and dispute resolution.

attributes.name: delivery_time
attributes.description: Precise timestamp when the delivery was received at the restaurant Back of House (BOH). Critical for temperature control compliance and HACCP documentation.

attributes.name: delivery_timeliness
attributes.description: Classification of delivery punctuality relative to expected delivery date. On Time: within agreed window; Early: before window; Late: after window. Key supplier performance indicator.

attributes.name: driver_name
attributes.description: Name of the driver who delivered the goods. Captured for traceability and food safety audit trail per HACCP requirements.

attributes.name: expected_delivery_date
attributes.description: Scheduled delivery date from the original purchase order. Used to calculate delivery timeliness and supplier on-time performance metrics.

attributes.name: modified_timestamp
attributes.description: System timestamp when the receiving order record was last updated. Tracks changes to status, variance resolution, or quality inspection updates.

attributes.name: posted_to_inventory_flag
attributes.description: Boolean indicator that received goods have been posted to restaurant unit inventory in MarketMan and SAP S/4HANA. True when goods receipt is complete and stock levels updated.

attributes.name: posted_to_inventory_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the receiving transaction was posted to inventory system. Used for inventory valuation date and FIFO/LIFO costing.

attributes.name: quality_inspection_result
attributes.description: Overall quality assessment result for the received goods. Approved: meets standards; Rejected: fails quality criteria; Conditional: accepted with notes; Not Inspected: visual check only. Aligns with Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) standards.

attributes.name: quality_notes
attributes.description: Free-text notes captured by receiving manager regarding product quality, packaging condition, or other observations. Used for supplier feedback and continuous improvement.

attributes.name: receiving_number
attributes.description: Business-facing unique receiving document number generated by MarketMan or restaurant POS system. Format: RCV-YYYYMMDD-NNNN.

attributes.name: receiving_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the receiving transaction. Pending: awaiting inspection; Partial: some items received; Complete: fully received and accepted; Rejected: delivery refused; Disputed: discrepancy under review.

attributes.name: rejection_reason
attributes.description: Detailed explanation if receiving status is rejected. Captures specific food safety, quality, or compliance failure that led to refusal of delivery. Required for FDA and HACCP audit trail.
attributes.references: HACCP Corrective Action, FDA Food Code

attributes.name: seal_integrity_check
attributes.description: Verification that tamper-evident seals on delivery containers were intact upon arrival. Intact: seal unbroken; Broken: potential contamination risk; Not Applicable: no seal required. Part of food safety protocol.

attributes.name: source_system
attributes.description: Identifier of the operational system that originated this receiving record. MarketMan: primary inventory system; SAP MM: ERP goods receipt; Manual Entry: paper-based receiving logged retrospectively.

attributes.name: supplier_name
attributes.description: Legal or trade name of the supplier or distributor. Denormalized for reporting convenience; authoritative source is supplier master.

attributes.name: temperature_check_result
attributes.description: Result of temperature verification for refrigerated and frozen goods upon delivery. Pass: within safe range; Fail: out of range, reject or quarantine; Not Applicable: ambient goods. Critical for HACCP and FDA compliance.
attributes.references: FDA Food Code, HACCP Critical Control Point

attributes.name: temperature_recorded
attributes.description: Actual temperature reading in Fahrenheit recorded during delivery inspection for refrigerated or frozen items. Null for ambient goods. Used for food safety audit trail.

attributes.name: total_items_ordered
attributes.description: Total count of distinct Stock Keeping Units (SKUs) listed on the originating purchase order. Used for completeness verification.

attributes.name: total_items_received
attributes.description: Total count of distinct SKUs actually received and accepted in this transaction. Variance from total_items_ordered triggers partial or disputed status.

attributes.name: total_received_value
attributes.description: Total monetary value of all goods received in this transaction, in local currency. Used for Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) calculation and variance analysis against PO.

attributes.name: variance_flag
attributes.description: Boolean indicator that quantity or value variance exists between PO and received goods. True triggers exception workflow for manager review and supplier follow-up.

attributes.name: variance_reason
attributes.description: Categorized reason for any discrepancy between ordered and received goods. Used for supplier performance tracking and root cause analysis.

products.name: physical_count
products.description: Records scheduled or ad-hoc physical inventory count events at a restaurant unit, capturing both count-level header metadata and line-level SKU counts in a single consolidated entity. Header attributes: count date, count type (full/spot-check/cycle-count/pre-close/post-close), count status, count period, initiated-by/approved-by employees, total variance value and percentage. Line attributes (one per SKU-location counted): stock item reference, storage location, system quantity (book inventory), counted quantity, variance quantity and value ($), unit of measure, unit cost, count method (manual/scan), counted-by employee reference, and recount flag. Variance lines trigger investigation workflows and feed period-end food cost reconciliation and COGS% reporting.

attributes.name: physical_count_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the physical inventory count event. Primary key.

attributes.name: financial_period_id
attributes.description: Reference to the fiscal period (month/quarter) for which this count is being performed, used for period-end food cost reconciliation and P&L (Profit and Loss) reporting.

attributes.name: franchisee_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to franchise.franchisee. Business justification: Franchisee audit of physical inventory counts is required for compliance and variance analysis.

attributes.name: gl_journal_entry_id
attributes.description: Reference to the General Ledger journal entry created to record the inventory variance from this physical count.

attributes.name: primary_physical_initiated_by_employee_id
attributes.description: Reference to the employee who initiated or started the physical count event.

attributes.name: primary_physical_restaurant_unit_id
attributes.description: Reference to the restaurant unit where the physical count was performed.

attributes.name: recount_of_count_id
attributes.description: Reference to the original physical count ID if this count is a recount or correction of a previous count event.

attributes.name: stock_location_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_location. Business justification: A physical count event can be scoped to a specific storage location within a restaurant unit (e.g., counting only the walk-in cooler or dry storage). Adding stock_location_id FK to physical_count link

attributes.name: actual_end_timestamp
attributes.description: The actual date and time when the physical count was completed and submitted.

attributes.name: actual_start_timestamp
attributes.description: The actual date and time when the physical count was started by the counting team.

attributes.name: approval_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when the physical count was approved by the manager or supervisor.

attributes.name: cancellation_reason
attributes.description: Free-text explanation for why the physical count was cancelled or voided.

attributes.name: cancellation_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when the physical count was cancelled or voided, if applicable.

attributes.name: count_date
attributes.description: The calendar date on which the physical inventory count was conducted.

attributes.name: count_method
attributes.description: The method used to perform the physical count: manual (paper-based or manual entry), barcode-scan (handheld scanner), rfid (radio-frequency identification), or hybrid (combination of methods).

attributes.name: count_number
attributes.description: Business identifier for the physical count event, formatted as PC-YYYYMMDD-NNNN for external reference and audit trail.

attributes.name: count_period
attributes.description: The daypart or shift during which the count was performed, aligned with restaurant operating periods.

attributes.name: count_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the count event: scheduled (planned but not started), in-progress (counting underway), submitted (completed awaiting approval), approved (validated by manager), posted (applied to inventory system), or cancelled (voided).

attributes.name: count_type
attributes.description: Classification of the count event: full (complete inventory), spot-check (random sample), cycle-count (scheduled rotation), pre-close (before period end), post-close (after period end), or opening (new restaurant opening).

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this physical count record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: is_period_end_count
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether this count is a mandatory period-end count for financial close and COGS% (Cost of Goods Sold Percentage) calculation. True if period-end count, False otherwise.

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this physical count record was last updated or modified.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-text notes or comments about the count event, including explanations for significant variances, operational issues during count, or special circumstances.

attributes.name: physical_inventory_value
attributes.description: The total dollar value of inventory based on the physical count results, calculated by multiplying counted quantities by unit costs.

attributes.name: posted_to_gl_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when the count variance was posted to the General Ledger (GL) for financial reporting, typically after approval.

attributes.name: recount_required_flag
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether a recount is required due to excessive variance or data quality issues. True if recount needed, False otherwise.

attributes.name: scheduled_start_timestamp
attributes.description: The planned date and time when the physical count was scheduled to begin.

attributes.name: source_system
attributes.description: The system or application from which the physical count data originated: MarketMan (primary inventory system), SAP (ERP integration), Manual (paper-based), or Mobile-App (handheld device).

attributes.name: submission_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when the physical count was submitted for approval after completion.

attributes.name: system_inventory_value
attributes.description: The total dollar value of inventory according to the system (MarketMan) at the time of count, used as the baseline for variance calculation.

attributes.name: total_sku_counted
attributes.description: The total number of unique SKUs (Stock Keeping Units) that were physically counted during this count event.

attributes.name: total_sku_with_variance
attributes.business_glossary_term: Total SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) With Variance
attributes.description: The number of SKUs that showed a variance (difference) between system inventory and physical count.

attributes.name: total_variance_amount
attributes.description: The total dollar value variance between system inventory and physical count results, calculated as (physical count value - system inventory value). Positive indicates overage, negative indicates shortage.

attributes.name: total_variance_percentage
attributes.description: The total variance expressed as a percentage of system inventory value, calculated as (variance amount / system inventory value) * 100. Key metric for COGS% (Cost of Goods Sold Percentage) analysis and food cost control.

attributes.name: variance_reason_code
attributes.description: Primary reason code for inventory variance: theft (shrinkage), spoilage (expired/damaged), waste (prep waste), receiving-error (incorrect receipt), transfer-error (incorrect transfer), system-error (data entry mistake), or unknown. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: theft|spoilage|waste|receiving-error|transfer-error|system-error|unknown — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

products.name: waste_log
products.description: Records every food waste event at a restaurant unit — spoilage, overproduction, prep waste, expiration, and quality rejection. Captures waste date, waste time, stock item reference, waste quantity, unit of measure, waste cost ($), waste category (spoilage, overproduction, prep loss, expiration, quality-reject, theft/unknown), waste reason description, responsible station (BOH/FOH), recorded-by employee reference, and manager approval flag. Drives Waste% KPI calculation and supports yield management and COGS% optimization.

attributes.name: waste_log_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the waste log entry. Primary key.

attributes.name: campaign_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Marketing ROI analysis needs to attribute waste spikes to specific campaigns; this FK supports that report.

attributes.name: corrective_action_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to foodsafety.foodsafety_corrective_action. Business justification: When a food safety corrective action mandates disposal of non-compliant stock (temperature exceedance, contamination), the resulting waste event must be traceable to that corrective action for HACCP c

attributes.name: critical_control_point_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to foodsafety.critical_control_point. Business justification: waste_log.haccp_violation flag indicates a CCP breach caused the waste event. Linking to the specific critical_control_point enables HACCP deviation tracking, regulatory reporting, and CCP performance

attributes.name: equipment_asset_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to restaurant.equipment_asset. Business justification: HACCP equipment-linked waste tracking: waste events caused by equipment failure (refrigeration breakdown, fryer malfunction) must reference the responsible equipment asset for food safety audits, insu

attributes.name: facility_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to realestate.facility. Business justification: Waste tracking per facility enables waste‑cost attribution to each property for sustainability and expense reporting.

attributes.name: financial_period_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.financial_period. Business justification: Waste is reported by fiscal period for P&L variance analysis and food cost percent calculation. Restaurant operators and franchisors track waste cost against period budgets. No existing FK from waste_

attributes.name: franchisee_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to franchise.franchisee. Business justification: Franchisee waste reporting feeds sustainability metrics and waste‑related royalty adjustments.

attributes.name: health_inspection_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to foodsafety.health_inspection. Business justification: Health inspectors can mandate immediate disposal of food items during an inspection. Linking waste_log to health_inspection provides regulatory traceability — health departments and operators need to

attributes.name: illness_report_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to foodsafety.illness_report. Business justification: When a foodborne illness is reported, suspected food items must be immediately disposed of and the disposal recorded. Linking waste_log to illness_report provides regulatory traceability for health de

attributes.name: maintenance_work_order_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to realestate.maintenance_work_order. Business justification: Refrigeration or equipment failures cause waste events directly attributable to a maintenance work order. Linking waste_log to maintenance_work_order supports warranty/insurance claims, HACCP violatio

attributes.name: order_item_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to order.order_item. Business justification: Void-to-waste reconciliation: when an order item is voided, comped, or returned, the physical food becomes waste. Linking waste_log to the triggering order_item enables loss prevention reporting, food

attributes.name: primary_waste_recorded_by_employee_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the employee who recorded the waste event in the system.

attributes.name: primary_waste_restaurant_unit_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the restaurant unit where the waste event occurred.

attributes.name: recall_event_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supply.recall_event. Business justification: FDA/USDA recall compliance requires linking disposal and waste records to the triggering recall event. Foodservice operators must demonstrate that recalled product was properly disposed of; waste_log

attributes.name: recipe_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the recipe or Bill of Materials (BOM) associated with the wasted prepared item, if applicable.

attributes.name: shift_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the work shift during which the waste event occurred, used for labor and operational analysis.

attributes.name: stock_item_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the Stock Keeping Unit (SKU) that was wasted.

attributes.name: stock_location_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_location. Business justification: Waste is recorded per storage location; replacing the denormalized storage_location field with a FK enables location‑level waste analytics.

attributes.name: waste_gl_account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Posts waste expense to a designated GL account for waste cost reporting and profitability analysis.

attributes.name: approval_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when the waste log entry was approved by a manager.

attributes.name: batch_number
attributes.description: The production or receiving batch identifier for the wasted item, used for traceability and food safety compliance.

attributes.name: corrective_action_taken
attributes.description: Description of any corrective action implemented to prevent recurrence of this type of waste.

attributes.name: daypart
attributes.description: The operational time segment during which the waste event occurred (breakfast, lunch, dinner, late-night).

attributes.name: disposal_method
attributes.description: The method used to dispose of the wasted item: trash (landfill), compost, donation (food rescue), rendering (animal feed/byproduct), or other.

attributes.name: expiration_date
attributes.description: The use-by or best-before date of the wasted item, critical for expiration-related waste tracking.

attributes.name: haccp_violation
attributes.business_glossary_term: HACCP (Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points) Violation Flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the waste event was associated with a HACCP critical control point violation.

attributes.name: manager_approved
attributes.description: Indicates whether a manager has reviewed and approved this waste log entry.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Additional free-form notes or comments about the waste event for operational context.

attributes.name: on_hand_quantity_before_waste
attributes.description: The inventory quantity on hand immediately before the waste event was recorded.

attributes.name: par_level_at_waste
attributes.business_glossary_term: PAR (Periodic Automatic Replenishment) Level at Waste
attributes.description: The PAR level setting for this item at the time of waste, used to analyze whether overstocking contributed to waste.

attributes.name: record_created_timestamp
attributes.description: The system timestamp when this waste log record was first created in the database.

attributes.name: record_updated_timestamp
attributes.description: The system timestamp when this waste log record was last modified.

attributes.name: responsible_station
attributes.description: The operational area where the waste occurred: BOH (Back of House - kitchen/prep areas) or FOH (Front of House - service/dining areas).

attributes.name: temperature_at_waste
attributes.description: The temperature in Fahrenheit of the item or storage area at the time of waste, relevant for food safety and spoilage analysis.

attributes.name: unit_of_measure
attributes.description: The unit in which the waste quantity is measured (e.g., kilograms, pounds, each, gallons). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: kg|lb|oz|g|ea|case|box|gal|ltr|ml — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: waste_category
attributes.description: The classification of the waste event: spoilage (deterioration), overproduction (excess prepared food), prep-loss (trimming/cooking loss), expiration (past use-by date), quality-reject (failed quality standards), theft-unknown (unexplained loss).

attributes.name: waste_cost
attributes.description: The monetary cost of the wasted item in the restaurant's operating currency, calculated as quantity times unit cost.

attributes.name: waste_date
attributes.description: The calendar date on which the waste event occurred.

attributes.name: waste_prevention_opportunity
attributes.description: Notes on potential process improvements or training opportunities identified from this waste event.

attributes.name: waste_quantity
attributes.description: The numeric quantity of the item wasted, measured in the unit of measure specified.

attributes.name: waste_reason
attributes.description: Free-text description providing additional context and specific reason for the waste event.

attributes.name: waste_timestamp
attributes.description: The precise date and time when the waste event was recorded.

products.name: stock_transfer
products.description: Records the movement of inventory between restaurant units, between storage locations within a unit, or from a unit back to a distribution center — capturing both transfer header and line-level SKU detail in a single consolidated entity. Header attributes: transfer date, transfer type (inter-unit/intra-unit/return-to-vendor/return-to-DC), origin and destination units/locations, transfer status, requested-by/approved-by employees, and total transfer value. Line attributes (one per SKU transferred): stock item reference, transferred quantity, unit of measure, unit cost, extended cost, lot number, expiration date, and condition code. Maintains inventory accuracy across the restaurant network and supports inter-unit food cost allocation.

attributes.name: stock_transfer_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the stock transfer transaction. Primary key for the stock transfer record.

attributes.name: catering_order_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to order.catering_order. Business justification: Catering-driven inter-unit stock transfers: high-volume catering events at one unit may require stock transferred from a nearby unit. Linking stock_transfer to the catering_order enables event-driven

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Captures internal transfer cost allocation to cost centers for inter‑unit inventory movement accounting.

attributes.name: destination_restaurant_unit_id
attributes.description: Reference to the restaurant unit or distribution center receiving the transferred inventory. For intra-unit transfers, this is the same as origin unit.

attributes.name: destination_stock_location_id
attributes.description: Reference to the specific storage location (walk-in cooler, dry storage, freezer) within the destination unit where items will be received. Critical for BOH (Back of House) inventory accuracy.

attributes.name: facility_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to realestate.facility. Business justification: Inter‑facility stock transfers require facility context to allocate transfer costs and maintain accurate on‑hand balances per property.

attributes.name: financial_period_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.financial_period. Business justification: Inter-unit stock transfers affect inventory valuation at both origin and destination units and must be posted to the correct fiscal period. stock_transfer has fiscal_period as a plain text attr — a pr

attributes.name: origin_restaurant_unit_id
attributes.description: Reference to the restaurant unit or distribution center from which inventory is being transferred. For intra-unit transfers, this is the same as destination unit.

attributes.name: origin_stock_location_id
attributes.description: Reference to the specific storage location (walk-in cooler, dry storage, freezer) within the origin unit from which items are being transferred. Critical for BOH (Back of House) inventory accuracy.

attributes.name: primary_stock_requested_by_employee_id
attributes.description: Reference to the employee (typically kitchen manager or shift supervisor) who initiated the stock transfer request. Used for accountability and audit trail.

attributes.name: tertiary_stock_received_by_employee_id
attributes.description: Reference to the employee at the destination unit who physically received and confirmed the transferred inventory. Null until transfer is received.

attributes.name: cancellation_date
attributes.description: Date when the stock transfer was cancelled. Null if transfer was not cancelled.

attributes.name: cancellation_reason
attributes.description: Free-text explanation for why the transfer was cancelled. Used for operational analysis and process improvement.

attributes.name: carrier_name
attributes.description: Name of the transportation carrier or delivery service used for inter-unit transfers. Null for intra-unit transfers or self-pickup.
attributes.references: Supply Chain Management - Carrier Master Data

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this stock transfer record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.
attributes.references: Enterprise Data Management - Audit Trail Standards

attributes.name: expected_delivery_date
attributes.description: Planned or estimated date when the transferred inventory is expected to arrive at the destination. Used for receiving planning and PAR (Periodic Automatic Replenishment) level management.
attributes.references: Supply Chain Management - Delivery Planning Standards

attributes.name: external_transfer_reference
attributes.description: Unique identifier from the source operational system (MarketMan, SAP MM). Used for data lineage, reconciliation, and cross-system traceability.
attributes.references: Enterprise Data Integration - Source System Key Mapping

attributes.name: fiscal_period
attributes.description: Fiscal period (year and period number) to which this transfer is assigned for financial reporting. Format: YYYY-PNN (e.g., 2024-P03 for period 3 of fiscal year 2024).
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI - Fiscal Calendar Standards

attributes.name: gl_posting_date
attributes.description: Date when the inventory transfer transaction was posted to the general ledger for financial reporting. May differ from physical transfer date due to period-end cutoffs.
attributes.references: GAAP - Revenue Recognition and Inventory Accounting Standards

attributes.name: haccp_monitoring_required_flag
attributes.business_glossary_term: HACCP (Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points) Monitoring Required Flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this transfer requires continuous temperature monitoring and documentation per HACCP critical control point protocols. True for high-risk perishable items.
attributes.references: FDA HACCP Principles - Critical Control Points

attributes.name: inspection_notes
attributes.description: Free-text notes from quality inspection documenting any issues, observations, or conditions. Used for quality tracking and supplier performance evaluation.

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this stock transfer record was most recently updated. Used for change tracking and data synchronization.
attributes.references: Enterprise Data Management - Audit Trail Standards

attributes.name: priority_level
attributes.description: Urgency classification for the transfer: routine (standard replenishment), high (needed within 24 hours), urgent (needed same day), emergency (critical stockout situation requiring immediate action).

attributes.name: quality_inspection_required_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether items in this transfer require formal quality inspection upon receipt per GMP (Good Manufacturing Practice) or food safety protocols. True for high-value or high-risk items.
attributes.references: FDA GMP Regulations - Quality Control Requirements

attributes.name: quality_inspection_status
attributes.description: Result of quality inspection at receiving: not-required (no inspection needed), pending (awaiting inspection), passed (accepted), failed (rejected), conditional-accept (accepted with notes or partial rejection).

attributes.name: shipping_method
attributes.description: Method used to transport inventory between locations: internal-delivery (company fleet), courier (contracted delivery service), third-party (3PD logistics provider), self-pickup (destination unit retrieves), direct-transfer (hand-carried for intra-unit).

attributes.name: source_system_code
attributes.description: Code identifying the operational system that originated this stock transfer record: MARKETMAN (MarketMan Inventory Management), SAP-MM (SAP Materials Management), MANUAL (manually entered), LEGACY (migrated from prior system).

attributes.name: temperature_controlled_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this transfer requires temperature-controlled transportation to maintain food safety and quality. True for perishable items requiring refrigeration or freezing per HACCP (Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points) standards.
attributes.references: FDA Food Safety Modernization Act - Cold Chain Requirements

attributes.name: temperature_zone_required
attributes.description: Required temperature zone for transport: ambient (room temperature), refrigerated (33-40°F), frozen (0°F or below), multi-temp (mixed temperature requirements). Critical for HACCP compliance and food safety.
attributes.references: FDA Food Code - Temperature Control Standards

attributes.name: total_item_count
attributes.description: Total number of distinct SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) line items included in this transfer. Used for transfer complexity assessment and receiving workload planning.

attributes.name: total_quantity_transferred
attributes.description: Aggregate quantity of all items transferred, summed across all line items. Unit of measure varies by item (cases, pounds, gallons, each). Used for high-level transfer volume tracking.

attributes.name: total_transfer_value_usd
attributes.description: Total dollar value of all items included in this stock transfer, calculated at standard cost. Critical for COGS% (Cost of Goods Sold Percentage) tracking and inventory valuation across units.

attributes.name: tracking_number
attributes.description: Shipment tracking number provided by carrier for monitoring in-transit status. Null for internal transfers without formal tracking.
attributes.references: Supply Chain Management - Shipment Tracking Standards

attributes.name: transfer_approval_date
attributes.description: Date when the stock transfer was approved by authorized manager. Null if not yet approved or if auto-approved (same as request date).

attributes.name: transfer_number
attributes.description: Business-facing unique transfer document number used for tracking and reference in operational systems and communications. Format: STR-YYYYMMDD-NNNN.
attributes.references: MarketMan Inventory Management - Transfer Document Standards

attributes.name: transfer_reason_code
attributes.description: Business reason for initiating the stock transfer: par-replenishment (restore PAR levels), excess-stock (reduce overstock), expiring-soon (move near-expiry items), quality-issue (return defective goods), menu-change (redistribute due to menu update), seasonal-adjustment (rebalance for seasonal demand).

attributes.name: transfer_reason_notes
attributes.description: Free-text explanation providing additional context for the transfer. Used for operational communication and audit documentation.

attributes.name: transfer_received_date
attributes.description: Date when the inventory was physically received and confirmed at the destination location. Null until transfer is fully or partially received.

attributes.name: transfer_request_date
attributes.description: Date when the stock transfer was initially requested. Used for tracking lead times and transfer cycle analytics.

attributes.name: transfer_ship_date
attributes.description: Date when the inventory physically left the origin location. Used for in-transit tracking and speed of service metrics.

attributes.name: transfer_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the stock transfer: requested (initiated but pending approval), approved (authorized for execution), rejected (denied by approver), in-transit (shipped but not yet received), received (fully received and confirmed), cancelled (voided before completion), partially-received (some items received, others pending). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: requested|approved|rejected|in-transit|received|cancelled|partially-received — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
attributes.references: MarketMan Inventory Management - Transfer Workflow States

attributes.name: transfer_type
attributes.description: Classification of the transfer based on origin and destination: inter-unit (between restaurant units), intra-unit (between storage locations within same unit), return-to-dc (from unit back to distribution center), return-to-vendor (from unit back to supplier), emergency (urgent replenishment), rebalance (inventory optimization transfer).
attributes.references: Inventory Management Best Practices - Transfer Classification

attributes.name: variance_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether a discrepancy was identified between shipped quantities and received quantities. True if any line item shows variance requiring investigation.

attributes.name: variance_reason
attributes.description: Root cause classification for any quantity variance: damage-in-transit (items damaged during shipment), short-shipment (fewer items sent than documented), overage (more items received than expected), quality-rejection (items rejected at receiving), counting-error (human error in count), none (no variance).
attributes.references: Inventory Management - Variance Root Cause Analysis

products.name: replenishment_order
products.description: Automated or manually triggered replenishment order generated when stock falls below PAR level or reorder point, capturing both order header and line-level SKU detail in a single consolidated entity. Header attributes: order date, order type (auto-PAR/manual/emergency), restaurant unit reference, supplier reference, requested delivery date, order status, total order value, order source (MarketMan auto-replenishment/manager-initiated), and approval status. Line attributes (one per SKU ordered): stock item reference, ordered quantity, unit of measure, unit cost, extended cost, PAR quantity at time of order, on-hand quantity at time of order, variance-to-PAR quantity, and confirmed delivery quantity. Bridges inventory management and procurement; links to purchase orders in Coupa and SAP MM. Enables food cost forecasting and supplier fill-rate tracking.

attributes.name: replenishment_order_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the replenishment order. Primary key for the replenishment order entity.

attributes.description: Reference to the user (typically BOH manager or receiving clerk) who received and verified the replenishment order delivery at the restaurant unit.

attributes.name: facility_id

attributes.name: financial_period_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.financial_period. Business justification: Replenishment orders are accrued to fiscal periods for AP liability recognition and inventory cost reporting. Period-end close requires all open POs to be assigned to the correct financial period. No

attributes.description: Reference to the user (manager or approver) who approved the replenishment order. Null if approval not required or still pending.

attributes.name: promotion_id

This link closes the demand-to-supply loop.

attributes.description: Reference to the user (manager or approver) who approved the replenishment order. Null if approval not required or still pending.

attributes.name: order_type
attributes.description: Classification of the replenishment order trigger mechanism. Auto-PAR: triggered automatically when stock falls below PAR level; Manual: manager-initiated; Emergency: urgent out-of-stock order; Scheduled: routine periodic order; Spot: one-time special order.

attributes.name: payment_terms
attributes.description: Payment terms agreed with supplier for this replenishment order. Examples: Net 30, Net 60, COD, 2/10 Net 30. Inherited from supplier master agreement or negotiated per order.

attributes.name: priority_level
attributes.description: Business priority classification for the replenishment order. Urgent priority for emergency out-of-stock situations; high for items approaching stockout; normal for routine PAR replenishment.

attributes.name: received_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the replenishment order was physically received and logged into inventory at the restaurant unit. Triggers inventory update and AP invoice matching.

attributes.description: Delivery or freight charge for the replenishment order in USD. May be waived based on minimum order value or supplier agreement.

attributes.description: Delivery method for the replenishment order. Determines delivery speed and cost. Will-call indicates restaurant will pick up from supplier.

attributes.description: Date and time when the replenishment order was submitted to the supplier. Marks transition from draft to active order.

attributes.description: Shipment tracking number provided by carrier for the replenishment order. Enables real-time delivery status monitoring.

attributes.name: variance_flag

attributes.name: variance_notes
attributes.description: Detailed notes describing any discrepancies found during receiving. Includes short shipments, damaged goods, wrong items, or quality issues. Used for supplier dispute resolution.

products.name: adjustment
products.description: Records manual adjustments to on-hand inventory balances outside of standard receiving, waste, or count workflows. Captures adjustment date, adjustment type (positive adjustment, negative adjustment, write-off, theft, breakage, system correction), stock item reference, storage location, adjusted quantity, adjustment value ($), reason code, supporting document reference, adjusted-by employee, and manager approval. Provides a complete audit trail for all non-standard inventory movements and supports shrinkage analysis.

attributes.description: Identifier of the user who closed and finalized this food cost period. Typically a restaurant manager or accounting staff member.

attributes.name: profit_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Each restaurant unit is a profit center; food cost reporting is the primary P&L metric tracked at profit center level. food_cost_period has cost_center_id but not profit_center_id — profit center is t

attributes.name: restaurant_unit_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the restaurant unit for which this food cost period is calculated. Links to the specific restaurant location.

attributes.name: sales_report_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to franchise.sales_report. Business justification: Food cost percentage is calculated using sales revenue from the franchisee's submitted sales report. Linking food_cost_period to the specific sales_report used as the revenue denominator enables audit

attributes.name: tertiary_food_employee_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the user who closed and finalized this food cost period. Typically a restaurant manager or accounting staff member.

attributes.name: unit_performance_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to restaurant.unit_performance. Business justification: Period-end food cost reconciliation: food_cost_period records (actual_food_cost, cogs_percent_actual, waste_percent) are directly reconciled against the unit_performance record for the same fiscal per

attributes.name: actual_food_cost
attributes.description: The actual Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) for the period, calculated as: Opening Inventory + Purchases + Transfers In - Transfers Out - Closing Inventory. The real food cost incurred.
attributes.references: GAAP Cost of Goods Sold Standards

attributes.name: adjustment_amount
attributes.description: Any manual adjustments applied to the food cost calculation for the period, such as corrections for inventory errors, vendor credits, or accounting reclassifications. Positive or negative value.

attributes.name: adjustment_reason
attributes.description: Explanation for any manual adjustment applied to the food cost calculation. Provides audit trail and justification for period adjustments.

attributes.name: approved_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this food cost period was reviewed and approved by management. Indicates final sign-off on the period results.

attributes.name: beverage_sales_revenue
attributes.description: The total beverage sales revenue for the period. Tracked separately from food sales for category-specific cost analysis.

attributes.name: closed_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this food cost period was officially closed and calculations were finalized. Marks the completion of the period-close process.

attributes.name: closing_inventory_value
attributes.description: The total dollar value of food inventory on hand at the end of the period, determined by physical count or perpetual inventory system. Represents the ending stock value for COGS calculation.

attributes.name: cogs_percent_actual
attributes.business_glossary_term: Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) Percentage Actual
attributes.description: The actual food cost as a percentage of food sales revenue for the period. Key performance metric for restaurant profitability and cost control. Calculated as (Actual Food Cost / Food Sales Revenue) * 100.

attributes.name: cogs_percent_theoretical
attributes.business_glossary_term: Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) Percentage Theoretical
attributes.description: The theoretical food cost as a percentage of food sales revenue for the period. Represents the ideal COGS% if all operational standards were met. Calculated as (Theoretical Food Cost / Food Sales Revenue) * 100.

attributes.name: count_method
attributes.description: The method used to determine closing inventory value: physical (manual count), perpetual (system-tracked), or hybrid (system with periodic physical verification).

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this food cost period record was first created in the system. Marks the initiation of the period tracking.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary values in this record (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency operations for international restaurant chains.

attributes.name: food_sales_revenue
attributes.description: The total food sales revenue (excluding beverage) for the period. Used as the denominator in COGS% calculations. Sourced from Point of Sale (POS) system.

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this food cost period record was last updated. Tracks the most recent change to any field in the record.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-text notes or comments about this food cost period, such as unusual events, operational issues, inventory discrepancies, or explanations for variance. Used for management review and audit purposes.

attributes.name: opening_inventory_value
attributes.description: The total dollar value of food inventory on hand at the beginning of the period. Represents the starting stock value for Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) calculation.

attributes.name: period_end_date
attributes.description: The last date of the accounting period for which food costs are being calculated. Marks the close of the cost accumulation window.

attributes.name: period_number
attributes.description: The accounting period identifier in format YYYY-PNN (e.g., 2024-P01 for period 1 of 2024). Used for financial reporting alignment.

attributes.name: period_start_date
attributes.description: The first date of the accounting period for which food costs are being calculated. Marks the beginning of the cost accumulation window.

attributes.name: period_status
attributes.description: The current status of the food cost period: open (still accepting transactions), closed (period ended, calculations complete), locked (finalized, no further changes allowed), or under_review (being audited or reviewed).

attributes.name: period_type
attributes.description: The frequency type of the accounting period for food cost calculation (weekly, bi-weekly, monthly, or quarterly).

attributes.name: purchases_value
attributes.description: The total dollar value of all food and beverage purchases received during the period. Includes all vendor invoices posted to inventory during the period.

attributes.name: theoretical_food_cost
attributes.description: The calculated ideal food cost based on recipe costing and actual sales mix (PMIX). Represents what food cost should have been if all recipes were followed perfectly with no waste or theft.

attributes.name: total_sales_revenue
attributes.description: The combined food and beverage sales revenue for the period. Represents total top-line revenue for the restaurant unit during the period.

attributes.name: transfers_in_value
attributes.description: The total dollar value of inventory transferred into this restaurant unit from other locations during the period. Increases available inventory.

attributes.name: transfers_out_value
attributes.description: The total dollar value of inventory transferred out of this restaurant unit to other locations during the period. Decreases available inventory.

attributes.name: variance_amount
attributes.description: The dollar difference between actual food cost and theoretical food cost (Actual - Theoretical). Positive variance indicates higher-than-expected costs due to waste, theft, portioning errors, or recipe non-compliance.

attributes.name: variance_percent
attributes.description: The variance expressed as a percentage of theoretical food cost. Calculated as (Variance Amount / Theoretical Food Cost) * 100. Used to assess the magnitude of cost control issues.

attributes.name: waste_percent
attributes.description: The waste value as a percentage of total food usage (Waste Value / (Actual Food Cost + Waste Value)) * 100. Key metric for food quality control and operational efficiency.

attributes.name: waste_value
attributes.description: The total dollar value of food waste recorded during the period, including spoilage, expiration, preparation errors, and quality control discards. Tracked for Waste% calculation and food cost control.

products.name: uom
products.description: Reference master for all units of measure used in inventory transactions — ordering UOM, storage UOM, and recipe UOM — plus their conversion factors. Captures UOM code, UOM name, UOM type (weight/volume/count/length), base UOM flag, and applicable item categories. Conversion detail: from-UOM, to-UOM, stock-item-specific overrides (item-specific conversions override global defaults), conversion factor, effective date, and source (vendor spec/lab measurement/standard). Supports accurate translation between ordering (case of 6), storage (each), and recipe (ounce/gram) contexts — critical for inventory valuation and COGS% calculation across all restaurant units.

attributes.name: uom_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the unit of measure. Primary key.

attributes.name: base_uom_id
attributes.description: Reference to the base UOM for this measurement type. Used to establish conversion chains. Null if this UOM is itself the base unit.

attributes.name: abbreviation
attributes.description: Short form or symbol for the UOM (e.g., lb, oz, gal, kg, g, ml). Used in compact displays, labels, and Kitchen Display System (KDS) screens.

attributes.name: allows_fractional_quantities
attributes.description: Indicates whether fractional quantities are permitted for this UOM (e.g., 2.5 pounds allowed, but 2.5 cases may not be). Enforces business rules in inventory transactions and recipe scaling.

attributes.name: allows_temperature_tracking
attributes.description: Indicates whether items measured in this UOM require Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points (HACCP) temperature monitoring during storage and transfer. Supports food safety compliance per Food and Drug Administration (FDA) regulations.

attributes.name: applicable_item_categories
attributes.description: Comma-separated list of Stock Keeping Unit (SKU) categories or commodity types where this UOM is applicable (e.g., dry goods, produce, dairy, beverages, proteins). Supports context-aware UOM selection in ordering and recipe management.

attributes.name: conversion_factor_to_base
attributes.business_glossary_term: Conversion Factor to Base Unit of Measure (UOM)
attributes.description: Multiplier to convert this UOM to the base UOM (e.g., 1 pound = 453.592 grams, so conversion_factor_to_base = 453.592). Critical for accurate Cost of Goods Sold (COGS%) calculation and yield management across ordering, storage, and recipe contexts.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when this UOM record was first created in the system. Supports audit trails and data lineage tracking per Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP) requirements.

attributes.name: default_shelf_life_days
attributes.description: Default shelf life in days for items measured in this UOM. Used as a fallback when Stock Keeping Unit (SKU)-specific shelf life is not defined. Supports waste tracking (Waste%) and First In First Out (FIFO) rotation.

attributes.name: display_sequence
attributes.description: Sort order for displaying UOMs in user interfaces and dropdown lists. Lower numbers appear first. Supports user experience optimization in Point of Sale (POS) and inventory management systems.

attributes.name: effective_end_date
attributes.description: Date when this UOM was retired or deprecated. Null for currently active UOMs. Supports historical reporting and audit trails for Cost of Goods Sold (COGS%) analysis.

attributes.name: effective_start_date
attributes.description: Date when this UOM became available for use in inventory transactions. Supports temporal validity and historical analysis of UOM changes.

attributes.name: is_base_uom
attributes.business_glossary_term: Is Base Unit of Measure (UOM) Flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this UOM is the base unit for its type (e.g., gram for weight, milliliter for volume). Base UOMs have a conversion factor of 1.0 and serve as the reference for all conversions within their type.

attributes.name: is_system_standard
attributes.description: Indicates whether this UOM is a system-defined standard (e.g., gram, liter, each) that cannot be modified or deleted by users. Protects core reference data integrity.

attributes.name: iso_code
attributes.description: ISO 80000 standard code for the unit of measure. Supports international operations and regulatory compliance across multiple jurisdictions.

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when this UOM record was last updated. Supports change tracking and data governance for Cost of Goods Sold (COGS%) accuracy and regulatory compliance.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-text field for additional context, usage guidelines, or special handling instructions for this UOM. Supports operational documentation and training materials.

attributes.name: ordering_uom_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this UOM is used in purchase orders and vendor ordering (e.g., case, pallet). Supports procurement workflows and vendor catalog integration.

attributes.name: plural_name
attributes.description: Plural form of the UOM name (e.g., Pounds, Ounces, Cases). Supports grammatically correct reporting and user interface text generation.

attributes.name: precision_decimal_places
attributes.description: Number of decimal places to use when displaying or calculating quantities in this UOM. Ensures consistent rounding and precision across inventory transactions.

attributes.name: recipe_uom_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this UOM is used in recipe Bill of Materials (BOM) and menu item costing (e.g., ounce, gram, tablespoon). Critical for accurate recipe yield management and menu engineering.

attributes.name: requires_lot_tracking
attributes.description: Indicates whether items measured in this UOM require lot number and expiration date tracking for traceability. Supports food safety recalls and Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) compliance.

attributes.name: storage_uom_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this UOM is used for Back of House (BOH) inventory storage and stock tracking (e.g., each, bag, box). Supports Periodic Automatic Replenishment (PAR) level management and cycle counts.

attributes.name: symbol
attributes.description: Standard international symbol for the UOM (e.g., °F, °C, %, #). Used in scientific and regulatory contexts, particularly for Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points (HACCP) temperature monitoring.

attributes.name: un_cefact_code
attributes.business_glossary_term: United Nations Centre for Trade Facilitation and Electronic Business (UN/CEFACT) Code
attributes.description: Standardized UN/CEFACT Recommendation 20 code for the unit of measure. Supports Electronic Data Interchange (EDI) integration with suppliers and third-party logistics providers.

attributes.name: uom_category
attributes.description: Measurement system category (metric, imperial, count-based, or custom). Supports multi-region operations with different measurement standards.

attributes.name: uom_code
attributes.description: Short alphanumeric code representing the unit of measure (e.g., EA, CS, LB, OZ, GAL, KG, G, ML, L). Used as the business identifier in inventory transactions and recipe management.

attributes.name: uom_name
attributes.description: Full descriptive name of the unit of measure (e.g., Each, Case, Pound, Ounce, Gallon, Kilogram, Gram, Milliliter, Liter). Human-readable label for reporting and user interfaces.

attributes.name: uom_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the UOM. Active UOMs are available for use in transactions; deprecated UOMs are retained for historical data but not available for new transactions.

attributes.name: uom_type
attributes.description: Classification of the unit of measure by dimension type. Determines conversion logic and applicability to different inventory contexts (receiving, storage, recipe). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: weight|volume|count|length|area|temperature|time — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

products.name: vendor_item
products.description: Maps each stock item to the supplier(s) that provide it, capturing the supplier-specific item details used in ordering and receiving. Captures supplier reference, stock item reference, supplier item code (vendor SKU), supplier item description, order UOM, pack size, pack quantity, current unit cost, contract price flag, lead time days, minimum order quantity, preferred vendor flag, and active status. Enables multi-vendor sourcing for critical items and supports cost comparison and replenishment order generation. Distinct from the procurement domain's supplier master — this is the inventory-domain view of what each vendor supplies.
products.reference: MarketMan Vendor Item / Supplier Item Catalog

attributes.name: vendor_item_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the vendor item mapping record. Primary key.

attributes.name: expense_gl_account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Maps vendor items to expense GL accounts for automated AP posting and cost tracking.

attributes.name: managed_by_employee_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Vendor item master management is a named procurement process: a purchasing/category manager employee owns each vendor item record, negotiating pricing and preferred vendor status. Foodservice procurem

attributes.name: order_uom_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to inventory.uom. Business justification: vendor_item.order_uom is a STRING column denoting the unit of measure used when ordering from this vendor. The uom table is the authoritative UOM reference master. Adding order_uom_id FK (prefixed 'or

attributes.name: stock_item_id
attributes.description: Reference to the stock item (SKU) in the inventory master that this vendor supplies.

attributes.name: supplier_contract_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supply.supplier_contract. Business justification: REQUIRED: Procurement audit needs to know which supplier contract governs each vendor‑supplied item for pricing and compliance.

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supply.supply_supplier. Business justification: Vendor item master management requires a direct supplier reference independent of contract status. Prospective or contract-free suppliers must be linkable to their catalog items for vendor qualificati

attributes.name: activation_date
attributes.description: The date when this vendor item mapping became active and available for ordering.

attributes.name: contract_effective_date
attributes.description: The date when the contract pricing becomes effective for this vendor item.

attributes.name: contract_expiration_date
attributes.description: The date when the contract pricing expires for this vendor item.

attributes.name: contract_number
attributes.description: Reference number of the procurement contract governing this vendor item pricing, if applicable.

attributes.name: contract_price_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the unit cost is based on a negotiated contract (True) or spot pricing (False).

attributes.name: cost_currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the unit cost. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USD|CAD|EUR|GBP|MXN|AUD|JPY|CNY — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: country_of_origin
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO country code indicating where this item is produced or sourced by the vendor. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USA|CAN|MEX|CHN|BRA|ARG|AUS|NZL|GBR|FRA|DEU|ITA|ESP|JPN|KOR|THA|VNM|IND — 18 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this vendor item mapping record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: deactivation_date
attributes.description: The date when this vendor item mapping was deactivated or discontinued, if applicable.

attributes.name: deactivation_reason
attributes.description: Business reason for deactivating this vendor item mapping (e.g., vendor discontinued, better pricing found, quality issues).

attributes.name: gtin
attributes.description: The vendor's GTIN/UPC/EAN barcode for this item, used for receiving and inventory tracking.

attributes.name: last_cost_update_date
attributes.description: The date when the unit cost for this vendor item was last updated.

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this vendor item mapping record was most recently updated.

attributes.name: last_order_date
attributes.description: The most recent date when this item was ordered from this vendor.

attributes.name: last_received_date
attributes.description: The most recent date when this item was received from this vendor.

attributes.name: lead_time_days
attributes.description: The number of days between order placement and expected delivery from this vendor for this item.

attributes.name: manufacturer_name
attributes.description: The name of the actual manufacturer of this item, which may differ from the vendor/distributor.

attributes.name: manufacturer_part_number
attributes.description: The manufacturer's unique part or item number for this product.

attributes.name: minimum_order_quantity
attributes.description: The minimum quantity (in order UOM) that must be ordered from this vendor for this item.

attributes.name: next_cost_review_date
attributes.description: The scheduled date for the next cost review or renegotiation with this vendor for this item.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-form text field for additional information, special handling instructions, or vendor-specific requirements for this item.

attributes.name: on_time_delivery_percent
attributes.description: Percentage of orders for this vendor item that were delivered within the promised lead time window.

attributes.name: order_increment
attributes.description: The quantity increment (in order UOM) in which this item must be ordered (e.g., must order in multiples of 5).

attributes.name: pack_quantity
attributes.description: The number of individual packs or units contained in one order UOM (e.g., 12 cans per case, 4 bags per box).

attributes.name: pack_size
attributes.description: The size or weight of each individual pack or unit within the order UOM (e.g., '5 LB', '12 OZ', '1 GAL').

attributes.name: preferred_vendor_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this vendor is the preferred supplier for this stock item (True) or an alternate source (False).

attributes.name: quality_rating
attributes.description: Quality score for this vendor's supply of this item, typically on a scale of 0.00 to 5.00, based on receiving inspections and quality audits.

attributes.name: unit_cost
attributes.description: The current cost per order UOM from this vendor. Used for purchase order generation and COGS calculation.

attributes.name: vendor_brand_name
attributes.description: The brand name under which the vendor supplies this item, if applicable.

attributes.name: vendor_item_description
attributes.description: Detailed description of the item from the vendor's catalog, including specifications, grade, or quality attributes.

attributes.name: vendor_item_name
attributes.description: The supplier's product name or description for this item as it appears in their catalog.

attributes.name: vendor_item_status
attributes.description: Current status of this vendor item mapping in the system lifecycle.

attributes.name: vendor_priority_rank
