attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: unit_ownership records the operating legal entity for each restaurant (operating_entity_legal_name, tax_identification_number, financial_reporting_entity_code). Linking to legal_entity is required for

attributes.name: primary_restaurant_unit_id
attributes.description: Reference to the restaurant unit for which this ownership record applies. Links to the restaurant master record.

attributes.name: primary_unit_employee_id
attributes.description: The identifier of the user who created this ownership record. Used for audit trail and accountability.

attributes.name: primary_unit_franchise_partner_franchisee_id
attributes.description: Reference to the franchise partner master record when ownership_type is franchised, licensed, joint_venture, master_franchise, or area_development. Null for company-owned units.

attributes.name: tertiary_unit_last_modified_by_user_employee_id
attributes.description: The identifier of the user who last modified this ownership record. Used for audit trail and accountability.

attributes.name: area_development_agreement_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this ownership is part of a multi-unit area development agreement. Used for franchise development tracking and compliance reporting.
attributes.references: IFA (International Franchise Association) - Franchise Best Practices

attributes.name: compliance_status
attributes.description: Current compliance status of this ownership arrangement with franchise agreement terms and brand standards. Used for franchise compliance reporting and risk management.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: The timestamp when this ownership record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.

attributes.name: franchise_agreement_expiration_date
attributes.description: The expiration date of the franchise agreement. Used for renewal planning and compliance tracking.
attributes.references: IFA (International Franchise Association) - Franchise Agreement Standards

attributes.name: franchise_agreement_start_date
attributes.description: The start date of the franchise agreement governing this ownership. May differ from ownership_effective_start_date if ownership transferred mid-agreement.
attributes.references: IFA (International Franchise Association) - Franchise Agreement Standards

attributes.name: initial_franchise_fee_usd
attributes.description: The one-time initial franchise fee paid at the start of this ownership arrangement. Null for company-owned units. Used for revenue recognition and franchise economics analysis.
attributes.references: FTC (Federal Trade Commission) - Franchise Disclosure Requirements

attributes.name: joint_venture_partner_name
attributes.description: The name of the joint venture partner if ownership_type is joint_venture. Used for partnership management and financial reporting.

attributes.name: last_compliance_audit_date
attributes.description: The date of the most recent compliance audit for this ownership arrangement. Used for audit scheduling and compliance tracking.

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: The timestamp when this ownership record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.

attributes.name: marketing_fee_percent
attributes.description: The percentage of gross sales contributed to the marketing fund under this ownership arrangement. Used for marketing fund accounting and campaign budget allocation.
attributes.references: FTC (Federal Trade Commission) - Franchise Disclosure Requirements

attributes.name: master_franchise_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this ownership operates under a master franchise arrangement with sub-franchising rights. Used for franchise hierarchy management and royalty flow tracking.
attributes.references: IFA (International Franchise Association) - Franchise Best Practices

attributes.name: next_compliance_audit_due_date
attributes.description: The scheduled date for the next compliance audit. Used for audit planning and compliance management.

attributes.name: operating_entity_legal_name
attributes.description: The full legal name of the entity that owns and operates this restaurant unit. Used for legal compliance, tax reporting, and franchise agreement documentation.
attributes.references: FTC (Federal Trade Commission) - Franchise Disclosure Requirements

attributes.name: operating_license_expiration_date
attributes.description: The expiration date of the operating license. Used for renewal tracking and compliance management.

attributes.name: operating_license_number
attributes.description: The business operating license number issued by local authorities for this ownership entity. Used for regulatory compliance and legal documentation.

attributes.name: ownership_effective_end_date
attributes.description: The date on which this ownership arrangement ended or will end. Null for current active ownership. Used to close out royalty periods and finalize P&L attribution.
attributes.references: IFA (International Franchise Association) - Franchise Agreement Standards

attributes.name: ownership_effective_start_date
attributes.description: The date on which this ownership arrangement became effective. Used to determine royalty calculation periods and P&L attribution windows.
attributes.references: IFA (International Franchise Association) - Franchise Agreement Standards

attributes.name: ownership_notes
attributes.description: Free-text notes capturing additional context about this ownership arrangement, including special terms, conditions, or historical information.

attributes.name: ownership_percentage
attributes.description: The percentage of ownership stake held by this entity in joint venture arrangements. 100% for sole ownership. Used for P&L attribution and profit distribution.

attributes.name: ownership_record_number
attributes.description: Business-facing unique identifier for this ownership record, used in franchise agreements and legal documentation.

attributes.name: ownership_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of this ownership record. Active indicates current operational ownership; pending indicates ownership transfer in progress; transferred indicates completed ownership change; terminated indicates closure or reversion; suspended indicates temporary operational hold.

attributes.name: ownership_transfer_date
attributes.description: The date on which ownership was transferred from a previous owner to the current owner. Used for historical tracking and transfer analysis.

attributes.name: ownership_type
attributes.description: Classification of the ownership model for this restaurant unit. Determines royalty calculation, P&L attribution, and compliance reporting requirements.
attributes.references: IFA (International Franchise Association) - Franchise Best Practices

attributes.name: previous_owner_name
attributes.description: The name of the previous owner if this ownership record represents a transfer or resale. Used for historical tracking and franchise relationship management.

attributes.name: royalty_rate_percent
attributes.description: The percentage of gross sales paid as royalty fees under this ownership arrangement. Null for company-owned units. Used for royalty calculation and financial forecasting.
attributes.references: FTC (Federal Trade Commission) - Franchise Disclosure Requirements

attributes.name: territory_code
attributes.description: The geographic territory code assigned to this franchise ownership. Used for territory management, market planning, and franchise compliance.

attributes.name: transfer_approval_date
attributes.description: The date on which the franchisor approved the ownership transfer. Used for compliance tracking and transfer timeline analysis.
attributes.references: FTC (Federal Trade Commission) - Franchise Disclosure Requirements

attributes.name: transfer_approved_by
attributes.description: The name or identifier of the franchisor representative who approved the ownership transfer. Used for audit trail and compliance documentation.

attributes.name: transfer_reason
attributes.description: The business reason for this ownership record being created or terminated. Used for franchise compliance reporting, trend analysis, and strategic planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: new_franchise_award|resale|corporate_acquisition|refranchising|closure|default|voluntary_termination|expiration — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
attributes.references: FTC (Federal Trade Commission) - Franchise Disclosure Requirements

domains.name: workforce
domains.description: Manages employee lifecycle including recruiting, onboarding, scheduling, time and attendance, labor forecasting, Labor% optimization, FTE tracking, certifications (ServSafe), performance management, and payroll integration via Workday HCM and Planday. Optimizes labor deployment across dayparts, BOH/FOH staffing ratios, and restaurant locations.
domains.references: OSHA 29 CFR, ServSafe Certification, Workday HCM, Planday Workforce Scheduling

products.name: employee
products.description: Master record for every restaurant employee across company-owned and franchised locations. Captures full employee lifecycle data including personal details, employment type (FTE/PTE), BOH/FOH role classification, hire date, termination date, employment status, pay grade, home restaurant assignment, Workday HCM employee ID, declared availability windows (preferred dayparts, max weekly hours, blackout dates, cross-location availability), and current benefit enrollment summary. Single source of truth for workforce identity and worker profile across the enterprise.

attributes.name: department_id

products.name: shift
products.description: Represents a scheduled work shift for an employee at a specific restaurant location, daypart (breakfast, lunch, dinner, late-night), and station assignment (grill, fry, drive-thru, expo, host, bar, dish). Captures planned start/end times, actual clock-in/clock-out times, assigned BOH/FOH station, shift type (regular, overtime, on-call, training), break duration, scheduling source (Planday), and swap/coverage details (original assignee, covering employee, swap request reason, swap approval status, approval timestamp) when shift reassignment occurs. Core operational record for labor deployment, station coverage planning, Speed of Service (SOS) staffing optimization, and Planday shift coverage workflows.

products.name: schedule
products.description: Weekly or period-level labor schedule published for a restaurant location, representing the planned staffing plan across all dayparts. Captures schedule period (start/end dates), restaurant unit, total scheduled hours, scheduled Labor%, FTE count by daypart, publication status (draft/published/locked), and the manager who approved the schedule. Links to individual shifts for granular staffing detail.

products.name: labor_forecast
products.description: Projected labor demand by restaurant, daypart, and week based on historical transaction volume (ADT/ATC), seasonal patterns, and planned promotions or LTOs. Captures forecasted cover count, expected throughput, recommended FTE count by BOH/FOH, projected Labor%, and the forecasting model version used. Drives Planday schedule generation and labor budget alignment.

products.name: certification
products.description: Single source of truth for all employee learning credentials, training completions, and regulatory certifications required for foodservice operations. Covers external certifications (ServSafe Food Handler, ServSafe Manager, allergen awareness, HACCP, alcohol service permits, OSHA safety) and internal training completions (new hire orientation, food safety modules, POS/KDS operation, BOH/FOH SOPs, LTO product training, management development). Captures credential type, issuing body (NRA ServSafe, local health department, internal L&D), delivery method (in-person, e-learning, OJT), issue/completion date, expiration date, assessment score, trainer/facilitator, and compliance status. Critical for food safety regulatory compliance, health department inspections, scheduling eligibility validation, and employee development tracking.

products.name: labor_budget
products.description: Period-level labor budget targets by restaurant location, capturing budgeted Labor%, budgeted total labor dollars, budgeted FTE count by BOH/FOH, budgeted hours by daypart, budgeted SPLH (Sales Per Labor Hour), and the fiscal period. Serves as the financial target against which actual labor spend (from payroll_record and time_entry) and forecasted demand (from labor_forecast) are measured. Supports P&L management, AUV optimization, and manager accountability for labor cost control.

domains.name: franchise

products.name: franchisee
products.description: Master record for each franchise partner entity — the legal business entity (individual, LLC, corporation) that holds one or more franchise agreements with the brand. Captures franchisee identity, legal structure, ownership details, contact information, financial standing, FDD disclosure status, IFA membership, and FranConnect system ID. SSOT for franchise partner identity across the enterprise.

products.name: agreement
products.description: Authoritative record of each franchise agreement between the franchisor and a franchisee, encompassing the full agreement lifecycle from execution through renewal, transfer, or termination. Captures agreement type (initial, renewal, transfer, successor), effective and expiration dates, territory grant, initial franchise fee, royalty rate and basis, marketing fund contribution rate, renewal terms, and agreement status. Owns all lifecycle event records including: transfers (transferor/transferee, ROFR exercise, approval, transfer conditions, effective date), renewals (updated terms, FDD re-disclosure, renewal fee, execution date), and terminations (type, default notice, cure period, de-identification, post-termination obligations, non-compete). SSOT for all contractual franchise obligations and their complete lifecycle history. Supports FTC Franchise Rule Items 5, 6, 17 disclosure requirements.

products.name: billing
products.description: Transactional record of all periodic franchise fees billed to a franchisee for a specific reporting period, including royalties, marketing fund contributions, technology fees, and other recurring charges. Captures billing period, gross sales reported, rates applied, line-item amounts (royalty, marketing fund, technology, other), total amount billed, payment due date, payment status, and variance from prior period. Source of truth for franchise revenue recognition, AR aging, and marketing fund governance. Supports FDD Item 6 and Item 11 disclosure compliance.

products.name: sales_report
products.description: Franchisee-submitted periodic gross sales report used as the basis for royalty and marketing fund calculation, SSS (Same-Store Sales) tracking, and operational benchmarking. Captures reporting period (weekly, monthly), reported gross sales by daypart, net sales, transaction count (ATC), average check value (ACV), submission date, submission method (POS auto-pull, manual entry, EDI), validation status, and variance flags. Supports royalty billing, comp sales analysis, franchisee performance benchmarking, and FDD Item 19 financial performance representation validation.

products.name: nro_pipeline
products.description: Tracks all franchise capital construction and development projects from initiation through completion, including New Restaurant Openings (NROs), remodels, reimaging, refreshes, and conversions. Captures project type (new build, conversion, full remodel, refresh, reimaging, ADA compliance), project stage (site identified, LOI signed, lease executed, permits obtained, construction started, construction complete, training complete, opened/completed), target and actual completion dates, development type, assigned development consultant, capital investment estimate, approved contractor, permit and inspection status, brand approval status, contractual obligation source, and milestone completion dates. SSOT for the franchise development and facility lifecycle in FranConnect.

domains.name: foodsafety
domains.description: Governs HACCP plan management, food safety audit results, health inspection records, corrective action tracking, temperature monitoring logs, sanitation schedules, allergen management, and SOP compliance via Zenput. Ensures adherence to FDA FSMA, local health department requirements, ISO 22000, and ServSafe standards across all restaurant units.

products.name: haccp_plan
products.description: Master record for each restaurant units Hazard Analysis and Critical Control Points (HACCP) plan, including plan version, scope, approval status, regulatory framework alignment (FDA FSMA, ISO 22000, Codex Alimentarius), effective and expiration dates, responsible food safety manager, team members, and prerequisite program references. Serves as the authoritative SSOT for HACCP program governance across all company-owned and franchised units. Each plan undergoes annual review and revalidation.
products.reference: HACCP Plan — FDA FSMA / ISO 22000 / Codex Alimentarius

Each CCP is tied to a specific process step (e.g., cooking, cooling, receiving) and HACCP plan version.

attributes.name: validity_period_days
attributes.description: Number of days between issue_date and expiration_date.
attributes.references: Length of validity expressed in days

products.name: illness_report
products.description: Transactional record of a reported employee foodborne illness or suspected foodborne illness event at a restaurant unit, capturing report date, employee role, symptoms reported, onset date, suspected pathogen or food item, exclusion-from-work decision, return-to-work clearance date, and health department notification status. Supports FDA Food Code employee health policy compliance.
products.reference: Employee Illness Report — FDA Food Code 2-201 / OSHA 300 Log

attributes.name: illness_report_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the illness report record.

attributes.name: campaign_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Illness outbreak traceability: Foodservice regulators and operators must trace illness clusters to specific LTO or promotional items. Linking illness_report to campaign enables the named 'illness-to-L

attributes.name: employee_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the employee who reported or is associated with the illness.

attributes.name: guest_profile_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to guest.profile. Business justification: Illness reports in foodservice must identify the affected guest for follow-up, legal liability management, and health department notification. Currently illness_report only has employee_id; guest-repo

attributes.name: primary_illness_restaurant_unit_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the restaurant location where the incident occurred.

attributes.name: shift_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the work shift during which the employee fell ill.

attributes.name: site_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to realestate.site. Business justification: Foodborne illness reports must be tied to a specific location for health department notification and outbreak investigation. Regulatory agencies track illness incidents by site address. This link enab

attributes.name: stock_item_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_item. Business justification: illness_report.suspected_food_item is a denormalized text field. Normalizing to stock_item_id enables foodborne illness traceability, lot-number-based recalls, and regulatory reporting. Health departm

attributes.name: suspected_menu_item_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to menu.menu_item. Business justification: FDA/regulatory foodborne illness investigations require tracing the specific menu item suspected in an illness event. The existing 'suspected_food_item' plain-text column is a denormalization of menu_

attributes.name: visit_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to guest.guest_visit. Business justification: Health departments require identifying the specific visit/meal event associated with a reported foodborne illness to trace the exposure source. Linking illness_report to guest_visit enables investigat

attributes.name: action_plan
attributes.description: Planned actions to prevent recurrence.

attributes.name: action_plan_completed_date
attributes.description: Date when the action plan was completed.

attributes.name: action_plan_due_date
attributes.description: Target date for completing the action plan.

attributes.name: compliance_reference
attributes.description: Reference to the specific regulatory requirement or guidance applicable to the report.

attributes.name: corrective_action_taken
attributes.description: Description of any corrective action performed in response to the incident.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the report record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: exclusion_decision
attributes.description: Indicates whether the employee was excluded from work due to the illness.

attributes.name: exclusion_start_date
attributes.description: Date when the employee's work exclusion began.

attributes.name: health_department_notification_date
attributes.description: Date when the health department was notified about the incident.

attributes.name: health_department_notified
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether the local health department was notified.

attributes.name: illness_report_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the illness report.

attributes.name: investigation_end_date
attributes.description: Date when the health investigation concluded.

attributes.name: investigation_start_date
attributes.description: Date when the health investigation began.

attributes.name: investigation_status
attributes.description: Current status of the health investigation.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free‑form notes captured by investigators or managers.

attributes.name: onset_date
attributes.description: Date when the employee first experienced symptoms.

attributes.name: report_method
attributes.description: Method by which the illness was reported.

attributes.name: report_number
attributes.description: Business identifier assigned to the illness report.

attributes.name: report_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the illness was reported.

attributes.name: return_to_work_date
attributes.description: Date when the employee was cleared to return to work.

attributes.name: root_cause
attributes.description: Identified root cause of the illness, if determined.

attributes.name: severity_level
attributes.description: Categorical severity level derived from the severity score.

attributes.name: severity_score
attributes.description: Numeric score representing the severity of the reported illness.

attributes.name: source_system
attributes.description: System of record that originated the illness report (e.g., Zenput, HR portal).

attributes.name: suspected_pathogen
attributes.description: Pathogen suspected to have caused the illness, if known.

attributes.name: symptoms
attributes.description: Symptoms reported by the employee, captured for health analysis.

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp of the most recent update to the report record.

domains.name: loyalty
domains.description: Manages guest loyalty program enrollment, membership tiers, points accrual and redemption, rewards catalog, promotional offers, personalized campaigns, member engagement, and loyalty analytics. Drives repeat visits, ACV lift, and customer lifetime value through targeted incentives and gamification across OLO and POS channels.
domains.references: PCI DSS, CRM Best Practices, Olo Digital Ordering Platform

products.name: member
products.description: Master record for every enrolled loyalty program member. Captures member identity linkage (guest_id FK to guest domain), enrollment channel (OLO, POS, in-app, kiosk, staff-assisted), enrollment date, current tier assignment, lifetime points earned/redeemed, opt-in preferences, program assignment, auto-accrue payment linkage flag, and program status (active, suspended, churned). This is the SSOT for loyalty membership identity — distinct from the guest profile master in the guest domain.

attributes.name: member_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the loyalty program member. Primary key for the member entity.

attributes.name: account_manager_employee_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Needed for assigning a dedicated employee to manage high‑value members, enabling personalized service and performance reporting.

attributes.name: acquisition_campaign_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Acquisition Campaign Attribution report requires linking each new member to the marketing campaign that drove the sign‑up, enabling ROI calculation of acquisition spend.

attributes.name: current_tier_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to loyalty.tier. Business justification: member.current_tier is a denormalized STRING storing the tier name/code. Replacing it with a FK to the tier reference catalog (current_tier_id -> loyalty.tier.tier_id) normalizes this relationship and

attributes.name: franchisee_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to franchise.franchisee. Business justification: Supports franchisee‑level loyalty analytics by linking each member to the owning franchisee, used in franchise performance dashboards.

attributes.name: member_guest_profile_id
attributes.description: Foreign key reference to the guest domain master record. Links loyalty membership to the guest profile system of record.

attributes.name: member_preferred_location_unit_id
attributes.description: Restaurant location that the member visits most frequently or has designated as their preferred location. Used for personalized offers and location-based campaigns.

attributes.name: member_profile_id
attributes.description: Foreign key reference to the guest domain master record. Links loyalty membership to the guest profile system of record.

attributes.name: member_unit_id
attributes.description: Restaurant location where the member enrolled, if enrollment occurred at a physical location. Null for digital enrollments.

attributes.name: program_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to loyalty.program. Business justification: Every loyalty member is enrolled in a specific loyalty program. The program governs the member's points rules, tier structure, earning rates, and redemption thresholds. Adding program_id to member est

attributes.name: referred_by_member_id
attributes.description: Member ID of the existing member who referred this member to the loyalty program. Null if member enrolled without a referral.

attributes.name: account_closure_date
attributes.description: Date when the loyalty membership was closed or terminated. Null for active memberships. Used for retention analysis and right-to-be-forgotten compliance.
attributes.references: Loyalty Program Business Rules; GDPR Article 17

attributes.name: account_closure_reason
attributes.description: Business reason for account closure. Examples: member request, fraud, inactivity, duplicate account, policy violation, data privacy request.

attributes.name: account_created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the loyalty member record was first created in the system. Audit field for data lineage and compliance.

attributes.name: account_updated_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the loyalty member record was last modified. Audit field for data lineage and change tracking.

attributes.name: badges_earned
attributes.description: Total number of achievement badges earned by the member through gamification activities, challenges, and milestones.

attributes.name: birthday_month
attributes.description: Month of the member's birthday (1-12). Stored without year or day for privacy. Used for birthday reward campaigns and personalized offers.

attributes.name: current_points_balance
attributes.description: Current available points balance that the member can redeem. Calculated as lifetime earned minus lifetime redeemed minus expired points.

attributes.name: data_privacy_consent_date
attributes.description: Date when the member provided explicit consent for data collection, processing, and storage under applicable privacy regulations.

attributes.name: direct_mail_opt_in
attributes.description: Indicates whether the member has consented to receive physical direct mail offers and communications. True if opted in, False if opted out.

attributes.name: email_opt_in
attributes.description: Indicates whether the member has consented to receive promotional and transactional emails. True if opted in, False if opted out.

attributes.name: enrollment_channel
attributes.description: Channel through which the guest enrolled in the loyalty program. OLO (Online Ordering), POS (Point of Sale), mobile app, website, in-restaurant kiosk, or call center.
attributes.references: Olo Digital Ordering Platform; Oracle MICROS POS

attributes.name: enrollment_date
attributes.description: Date when the guest enrolled in the loyalty program. Marks the start of the member lifecycle.

attributes.name: gamification_level
attributes.description: Current level in the loyalty program gamification system. Members progress through levels by completing challenges, earning badges, and accumulating points.

attributes.name: last_activity_date
attributes.description: Date of the most recent loyalty program activity by the member, including points earned, points redeemed, or profile updates. Used for churn prediction and reactivation campaigns.

attributes.name: last_transaction_date
attributes.description: Date of the most recent purchase transaction where the member earned or redeemed points. Subset of last_activity_date focused on revenue-generating activity.
attributes.references: Oracle MICROS POS; Olo Digital Ordering Platform

attributes.name: lifetime_points_earned
attributes.description: Total cumulative loyalty points earned by the member since enrollment. Never decreases; used for tier qualification and lifetime value analysis.

attributes.name: lifetime_points_redeemed
attributes.description: Total cumulative loyalty points redeemed by the member for rewards, discounts, or offers since enrollment.

attributes.name: membership_number
attributes.description: Externally-facing unique membership identifier displayed on loyalty cards, mobile app, and customer communications. Human-readable alphanumeric code.

attributes.name: next_expiration_date
attributes.description: Date when the next batch of points will expire. Null if no points are scheduled to expire.

attributes.name: nps_score
attributes.description: Most recent Net Promoter Score provided by the member, ranging from 0 (detractor) to 10 (promoter). Measures member satisfaction and likelihood to recommend.

attributes.name: nps_survey_date
attributes.description: Date when the most recent NPS survey was completed by the member.

attributes.name: points_expiring_soon
attributes.description: Number of points scheduled to expire within the next 30 days. Used for proactive member engagement and retention campaigns.

attributes.name: preferred_language
attributes.description: Member's preferred language for communications and app interface. Three-letter ISO 639-2 language code.

attributes.name: program_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the loyalty membership. Active: member in good standing. Suspended: temporarily blocked due to policy violation. Churned: member opted out or account closed. Inactive: no activity for extended period. Pending: enrollment initiated but not completed.

attributes.name: push_notification_opt_in
attributes.description: Indicates whether the member has consented to receive push notifications through the mobile app. True if opted in, False if opted out.

attributes.name: referral_code
attributes.description: Unique referral code assigned to this member for member-get-member campaigns. When shared and used by new enrollees, both parties receive bonus points.

attributes.name: sms_opt_in
attributes.description: Indicates whether the member has consented to receive promotional and transactional SMS text messages. True if opted in, False if opted out.

attributes.name: status_reason
attributes.description: Business reason or explanation for the current program status. Examples: fraud detection, member request, inactivity, policy violation, system migration.

attributes.name: terms_accepted_date
attributes.description: Date when the member accepted the loyalty program terms and conditions. Required for legal compliance and dispute resolution.

attributes.name: terms_version
attributes.description: Version number of the loyalty program terms and conditions that the member accepted. Format: v1.0, v2.1, etc.

attributes.name: third_party_sharing_opt_in
attributes.description: Indicates whether the member has consented to sharing their data with third-party partners for marketing purposes. True if opted in, False if opted out.

attributes.name: tier_effective_date
attributes.description: Date when the member achieved or was assigned their current tier level. Used for tier anniversary calculations and renewal cycles.

attributes.name: tier_expiration_date
attributes.description: Date when the current tier status expires and member will be re-evaluated for tier qualification. Null for lifetime tiers.

attributes.name: total_visits
attributes.description: Total number of restaurant visits or orders placed by the member since enrollment. Includes dine-in, drive-thru, OLO, and 3PD orders where loyalty was applied.

products.name: tier
products.description: Reference catalog of all loyalty membership tiers (e.g., Bronze, Silver, Gold, Platinum) defined in the Restaurants loyalty program. Stores tier name, tier code, qualification threshold (points or visit count), tier benefits summary, tier multiplier for points accrual, tier validity period, and sort order. Drives tier assignment logic and benefit entitlement across the program.

attributes.name: tier_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the loyalty membership tier. Primary key.

attributes.name: brand_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to restaurant.brand. Business justification: Tier structures (Gold/Silver/Bronze) are brand-specific in multi-brand operators. Brand managers configure and audit tier ladders per brand. Tier eligibility reporting and brand-level loyalty analytic

attributes.name: modified_by_employee_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Tier configuration change management requires tracking which employee modified tier thresholds, multipliers, or qualification criteria — essential for loyalty program governance and compliance audits.

attributes.name: program_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to loyalty.program. Business justification: Loyalty tiers (Bronze, Silver, Gold, Platinum) are defined within the context of a specific loyalty program. A tier belongs to one program. Adding program_id to tier establishes this parent-child rela

attributes.name: annual_fee_amount
attributes.description: Annual membership fee charged to maintain this tier status, if applicable. Null or zero indicates no fee. Used for premium tier monetization.

attributes.name: benefits_summary
attributes.description: Comma-separated or structured text summary of key benefits and perks associated with this tier (e.g., free delivery, birthday rewards, exclusive offers, priority support). Used for member communications and benefit entitlement logic.

attributes.name: birthday_reward_eligible
attributes.description: Indicates whether members in this tier receive a special birthday reward (e.g., free item, bonus points, discount). True if eligible, false otherwise.

attributes.name: color_code
attributes.description: Hexadecimal color code used for tier branding in digital channels (e.g., #CD7F32 for bronze, #C0C0C0 for silver, #FFD700 for gold). Used in mobile app, website, and marketing materials.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this tier record was first created in the loyalty system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.

attributes.name: downgrade_threshold
attributes.description: Minimum threshold value required to maintain this tier status during re-evaluation period. Falling below this value triggers downgrade to lower tier. Null indicates no downgrade policy.

attributes.name: early_access_lto
attributes.description: Indicates whether members in this tier receive early access to new Limited Time Offers (LTO) before general availability. True if eligible, false otherwise.

attributes.name: effective_end_date
attributes.description: Date when this tier configuration was retired or replaced. Null indicates the tier is currently active. Used for tier lifecycle management and historical analysis.

attributes.name: effective_start_date
attributes.description: Date when this tier configuration became active and available for member assignment. Used for tier lifecycle management and historical analysis.

attributes.name: exclusive_offers_eligible
attributes.description: Indicates whether members in this tier receive access to exclusive promotional offers and Limited Time Offers (LTO) not available to lower tiers. True if eligible, false otherwise.

attributes.name: free_delivery_eligible
attributes.description: Indicates whether members in this tier are eligible for free delivery on Online Ordering (OLO) and Third-Party Delivery (3PD) orders. True if eligible, false otherwise.

attributes.name: grace_period_days
attributes.description: Number of days after tier expiration during which members retain tier benefits before downgrade. Used to provide buffer period for re-qualification.

attributes.name: icon_url
attributes.description: URL path to the tier badge or icon image asset used in member portal, mobile app, and digital communications. Supports tier visual identity and gamification.

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this tier record was last updated. Used for audit trail, change tracking, and data synchronization.

attributes.name: launch_date
attributes.description: Date when this tier was officially launched to the market and communicated to guests. May differ from effective_start_date for soft launches or pilot programs.

attributes.name: max_redemption_discount_pct
attributes.description: Maximum percentage discount that can be applied when redeeming points for rewards in this tier (e.g., 20.00 for 20% max discount). Used to control reward economics and prevent abuse.

attributes.name: points_multiplier
attributes.description: Multiplier applied to base points accrual for members in this tier (e.g., 1.0 for base, 1.5 for 50% bonus, 2.0 for double points). Used to calculate accelerated points earning.

attributes.name: priority_support_eligible
attributes.description: Indicates whether members in this tier receive priority customer support (e.g., dedicated phone line, faster response times). True if eligible, false otherwise.

attributes.name: qualification_metric
attributes.description: The metric used to determine tier qualification (points accrued, visit count, total spend amount, or transaction count). Defines how qualification_threshold is interpreted.

attributes.name: qualification_period_days
attributes.description: Number of days over which the qualification threshold must be met (e.g., 365 for annual qualification, 90 for quarterly). Null indicates lifetime accumulation.

attributes.name: qualification_threshold
attributes.description: Numeric threshold value required to qualify for this tier (e.g., 1000 points, 25 visits, $500 spend). Interpretation depends on qualification_metric.

attributes.name: referral_bonus_points
attributes.description: Bonus points awarded to members in this tier for each successful referral of a new loyalty program member. Used to incentivize member acquisition through word-of-mouth.

attributes.name: rollover_points_allowed
attributes.description: Indicates whether unused points can roll over to the next qualification period for members in this tier. True if rollover is allowed, false if points expire at period end.

attributes.name: sort_order
attributes.description: Integer defining the hierarchical display order of tiers (e.g., 1 for Bronze, 2 for Silver, 3 for Gold, 4 for Platinum). Lower values represent entry-level tiers; higher values represent premium tiers.

attributes.name: target_member_segment
attributes.description: Description of the target guest segment this tier is designed to attract and retain (e.g., frequent visitors, high spenders, digital-first guests, family diners). Used for marketing strategy and program design.

attributes.name: tier_code
attributes.description: Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the tier (e.g., BRONZE, SILVER, GOLD, PLATINUM). Used as business identifier in operational systems and reporting.

attributes.name: tier_description
attributes.description: Detailed description of the tier, including value proposition, benefits summary, and marketing messaging. Used in loyalty program communications and member portal.

attributes.name: tier_name
attributes.description: Human-readable display name of the loyalty tier (e.g., Bronze Member, Silver Member, Gold Member, Platinum Elite). Used in guest-facing communications and marketing materials.

attributes.name: tier_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the tier. Active tiers are available for member assignment; inactive tiers are temporarily disabled; retired tiers are no longer offered but may have legacy members; pending tiers are configured but not yet launched.

attributes.name: upgrade_notification
attributes.description: Indicates whether members should receive automated notification when they qualify for upgrade to this tier. True if notification is enabled, false otherwise.

attributes.name: validity_days
attributes.description: Number of days a member retains this tier status after qualification before re-evaluation (e.g., 365 for annual renewal). Null indicates permanent tier assignment.

attributes.name: welcome_bonus_points
attributes.description: One-time bonus points awarded to a member upon first achieving this tier. Used to incentivize tier progression and enhance member engagement.

products.name: tier_history
products.description: Transactional audit of every tier change event for a loyalty member — captures previous tier, new tier, effective date, expiry date, reason code (qualification, demotion, manual override, promotional upgrade), and the triggering transaction reference. Enables tier progression analytics, member lifecycle tracking, and regulatory audit trails for program fairness.
products.reference: Tier Change Event / Tier Upgrade/Downgrade Log

attributes.name: tier_history_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for each tier change event in the loyalty program. Primary key for the tier history transactional audit log.

attributes.name: guest_order_id
attributes.description: Reference to the specific order, purchase, or points transaction that triggered this tier change event. Null for manual adjustments or scheduled reviews.

attributes.name: loyalty_member_id
attributes.description: Reference to the loyalty program member who experienced this tier change event.

attributes.name: new_tier_tier_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to loyalty.tier. Business justification: tier_history records every tier change event and stores new_tier_code as a STRING. Replacing this with a FK (new_tier_tier_id -> loyalty.tier.tier_id) normalizes the reference to the tier catalog for

attributes.name: override_authorized_by_employee_id
attributes.business_glossary_term: Override Authorized By Employee Id (Foreign Key)
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Manual tier override authorization requires tracking which manager authorized the override — critical for fraud prevention and compliance in loyalty operations. override_authorized_by is a denormalize

attributes.name: primary_tier_restaurant_unit_id
attributes.description: Reference to the restaurant location where the triggering transaction occurred. Null for tier changes not associated with a specific location (e.g., scheduled reviews, manual adjustments).

attributes.name: promotional_campaign_id
attributes.description: Reference to the marketing campaign that granted this promotional tier upgrade. Null for non-promotional tier changes.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this tier history record was first created in the data platform. Used for data lineage and audit tracking.

attributes.name: effective_date
attributes.description: The date when the new tier status became active and the member began receiving benefits associated with the new tier level.

attributes.name: expiry_date
attributes.description: The date when this tier status is scheduled to expire, requiring re-qualification or resulting in tier review. Null indicates indefinite or lifetime tier status.

attributes.name: is_manual_override
attributes.description: Indicates whether this tier change was manually processed by an administrator or customer service representative rather than automatically triggered by system rules. True for manual adjustments, false for automated changes.

attributes.name: is_promotional_tier
attributes.description: Indicates whether this tier change was the result of a promotional campaign or marketing offer rather than organic qualification. True for promotional upgrades, false for earned tier changes.

attributes.name: last_updated_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this tier history record was most recently modified in the data platform. Used for change tracking and data quality monitoring.

attributes.name: new_tier_benefits_activated_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether benefits associated with the new tier were immediately activated and available for member use. Used for benefit provisioning tracking.

attributes.name: notification_channel
attributes.description: The communication channel used to notify the member of this tier change: email, SMS, push notification, in-app message, physical mail, or none if no notification was sent.

attributes.name: notification_sent_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether a tier change notification was successfully sent to the member via email, mobile app push, or other communication channel. Used for member engagement tracking.

attributes.name: notification_sent_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when the tier change notification was sent to the member. Used for communication audit and member experience analytics.

attributes.name: override_justification
attributes.description: Business justification and approval notes for manual tier overrides, required for regulatory audit trails and program fairness documentation.

attributes.name: previous_tier_benefits_revoked_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether benefits associated with the previous tier were immediately revoked upon tier change. Used for benefit lifecycle management and member experience tracking.

attributes.name: previous_tier_code
attributes.description: The loyalty tier code the member held immediately before this change event (e.g., BRONZE, SILVER, GOLD, PLATINUM).

attributes.name: processing_channel
attributes.description: The channel through which this tier change was processed: POS (Point of Sale), OLO (Online Ordering), mobile app, web portal, call center, or admin portal. Used for channel analytics.

attributes.name: qualification_period_end_date
attributes.description: The ending date of the measurement period used to evaluate tier qualification criteria.

attributes.name: qualification_period_start_date
attributes.description: The beginning date of the measurement period used to evaluate tier qualification criteria (e.g., rolling 12 months, calendar year).

attributes.name: qualifying_points_balance
attributes.description: The total loyalty points balance at the time of tier change that contributed to qualification or demotion decision.

attributes.name: qualifying_spend_amount
attributes.description: The cumulative spend amount that qualified the member for this tier change, measured in the program's base currency. Used for tier progression analytics.

attributes.name: qualifying_visit_count
attributes.description: The number of restaurant visits or transactions during the qualification period that contributed to this tier change.

attributes.name: source_system
attributes.description: The operational system that originated this tier change event (e.g., Olo Digital Ordering Platform, Oracle MICROS POS, Salesforce CRM). Used for data lineage and system integration tracking.

attributes.name: source_system_record_code
attributes.description: The unique identifier of this tier change record in the source operational system, used for data reconciliation and troubleshooting.

attributes.name: tier_change_notes
attributes.description: Additional free-text notes or comments about this tier change event, used for customer service context and audit documentation.

attributes.name: tier_change_number
attributes.description: Business-readable identifier for this tier change event, used for customer service reference and audit tracking.

attributes.name: tier_change_reason_code
attributes.description: Standardized code indicating the business reason for the tier change: qualification (earned through spend/visits), demotion (failed to maintain requirements), manual_override (customer service adjustment), promotional_upgrade (marketing campaign), anniversary_renewal (annual tier review), points_threshold (points-based qualification), spend_threshold (spend-based qualification), visit_threshold (frequency-based qualification).

attributes.name: tier_change_reason_description
attributes.description: Detailed human-readable explanation of why this tier change occurred, providing context for customer service and audit purposes.

attributes.name: tier_change_timestamp
attributes.description: The precise date and time when the tier change event was processed and recorded in the loyalty system.

attributes.name: tier_change_type
attributes.description: Classification of the tier movement direction and nature: upgrade (promotion to higher tier), downgrade (demotion to lower tier), lateral (same tier level renewal), initial_enrollment (first tier assignment), reinstatement (reactivation after lapse), manual_adjustment (administrative override).

attributes.name: tier_duration_days
attributes.description: The number of days this tier status is valid, calculated as the difference between effective date and expiry date. Used for tier lifecycle analytics.

attributes.name: triggering_transaction_reference
attributes.description: Business-readable reference number of the transaction that triggered this tier change, used for customer service inquiries and audit trails.

products.name: points_ledger
products.description: Immutable financial-grade ledger recording every points movement for a loyalty member — accruals, redemptions, expirations, adjustments, bonus awards, goodwill credits, promotional credits, dispute resolutions, system error corrections, and fraud reversals. Each row captures transaction type (earn, redeem, expire, adjust, bonus, goodwill, reversal, correction), points delta, running balance, source channel (POS, OLO, 3PD), source order reference, campaign reference, expiry date of earned points, adjustment reason code, adjustment category (goodwill, dispute, error_correction, promotional, fraud), authorizing agent (for manual adjustments), approval status (auto_approved, pending_review, approved, rejected), processing timestamp, and related original transaction reference (for reversals/corrections). This is the single SSOT for ALL member point balance movements — no other product in this domain records points changes of any kind.

attributes.name: points_ledger_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for each points transaction record in the loyalty ledger. Serves as the immutable primary key for this financial-grade system of record.

attributes.name: accrual_rule_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to loyalty.accrual_rule. Business justification: Each points accrual entry in the ledger is generated by a specific accrual rule (e.g., 2x points on weekends, bonus points for specific menu categories). Adding accrual_rule_id to points_ledger links

attributes.name: campaign_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the marketing campaign or promotional offer that triggered this points transaction. Populated for bonus awards, multiplier events, or targeted incentives. Null for standard earn/redeem transactions not tied to a specific campaign.

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Points ledger entries represent financial transactions (earn/burn) that must be allocated to a cost center for restaurant-level P&L and loyalty program cost reporting. Foodservice finance teams requir

attributes.name: enrollment_event_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to loyalty.enrollment_event. Business justification: Enrollment events award initial_points_awarded to new members, which generates a points_ledger entry. Adding enrollment_event_id to points_ledger links the welcome/enrollment bonus ledger entry to the

attributes.name: financial_period_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.financial_period. Business justification: Points ledger entries drive deferred revenue balances that must be reported by financial period for period-end close. Foodservice finance teams require 'loyalty points deferred revenue period-end clos

attributes.name: gl_account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Required for accounting of each points accrual/redemption to the appropriate GL account per financial reporting standards.

attributes.name: loyalty_member_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier of the loyalty program member whose points balance is affected by this transaction. Links to the member master record.

attributes.name: offer_redemption_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to loyalty.offer_redemption. Business justification: Offer redemptions can trigger points accrual events (e.g., bonus points for redeeming an offer). Adding offer_redemption_id to points_ledger links the ledger entry to the specific offer redemption tha

attributes.name: points_restaurant_unit_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the restaurant location where this points transaction originated. Populated for POS and kiosk transactions. Null for OLO, mobile app, or admin transactions not tied to a specific physical location.

attributes.name: primary_points_adjusted_by_user_employee_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the system user or customer service agent who authorized and executed the manual adjustment or reversal. Populated for adjust and reversal transactions. Critical for SOX compliance and fraud prevention.

attributes.name: primary_points_franchise_franchisee_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the franchise entity that owns or operates the restaurant where this transaction occurred. Used for royalty calculations and franchise performance analytics. Null for company-owned locations or non-location transactions.

attributes.name: primary_points_guest_order_id
attributes.description: Reference to the originating order transaction that triggered this points movement. Populated for earn and redeem transactions tied to guest purchases. Null for non-order events such as bonus awards, expirations, or manual adjustments.

attributes.name: redemption_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to loyalty.redemption. Business justification: When a member redeems a reward (burn event), a points_ledger entry is created recording the points deduction. Adding redemption_id to points_ledger links the ledger debit entry to the specific redempt

attributes.name: reversal_of_transaction_points_ledger_id
attributes.description: Reference to the original points_ledger_id that this reversal transaction is canceling. Populated only for reversal transaction types. Creates an audit chain linking reversals to their source transactions.

attributes.name: reward_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the specific reward item redeemed by the member. Populated only for redeem transaction types. Links to the rewards catalog to identify what was claimed (free item, discount, experience).

attributes.name: source_channel_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to order.channel. Business justification: Points accrual channel normalization: points_ledger.source_channel is a plain-text denormalized attribute. FK to order.channel normalizes channel taxonomy for channel-level points accrual reporting an

attributes.name: source_transaction_guest_order_id
attributes.description: The unique transaction identifier from the originating source system. Enables end-to-end traceability and reconciliation between the loyalty ledger and upstream systems. Format varies by source system.

attributes.name: tier_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the loyalty membership tier the member held at the time of this transaction. Used to determine earn rates, bonus multipliers, and redemption privileges. Links to tier master data.

attributes.name: adjustment_reason_code
attributes.description: Standardized code explaining why a manual adjustment or reversal was made. Populated only for adjust and reversal transaction types. Supports audit compliance and customer service quality tracking.

attributes.name: adjustment_reason_notes
attributes.description: Free-text explanation provided by the customer service representative or system administrator who initiated the adjustment. Populated for adjust and reversal transactions. Supports audit trail and dispute resolution.

attributes.name: batch_reference
attributes.description: Identifier of the processing batch or job that created this ledger entry. Used for bulk operations such as nightly expiration runs, mass bonus awards, or system migrations. Null for real-time individual transactions.

attributes.name: fiscal_period
attributes.description: The fiscal period (month or accounting period number) within the fiscal year when this transaction occurred. Supports monthly financial close, P&L reporting, and loyalty liability accrual tracking.

attributes.name: fiscal_year
attributes.description: The fiscal year in which this points transaction occurred, derived from transaction_timestamp. Supports financial reporting, GAAP revenue recognition for breakage, and year-over-year loyalty program analytics.

attributes.name: is_voided
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether this transaction has been voided or reversed by a subsequent transaction. True if a reversal exists, false otherwise. Immutable ledger entries are never deleted, only marked as voided.

attributes.name: order_currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the order total amount. Supports multi-currency loyalty programs for international operations. Null for non-order transactions.

attributes.name: order_total_amount
attributes.description: The total monetary value of the order that triggered this points transaction, in the local currency. Used to calculate points earn rate and for financial reconciliation. Null for non-order transactions.

attributes.name: points_balance_after
attributes.description: The cumulative running balance of points for this member immediately after this transaction was applied. Provides audit trail and reconciliation capability. Must equal prior balance plus points_delta.

attributes.name: points_delta
attributes.description: The signed integer change in points balance for this transaction. Positive values represent accruals (earn, bonus), negative values represent deductions (redeem, expire, adjust downward). Zero-delta entries are not permitted.

attributes.name: points_earn_rate
attributes.description: The rate at which points were earned per currency unit spent. Example: 1.0000 means 1 point per dollar, 2.0000 means 2 points per dollar (double points promotion). Populated for earn transactions, null otherwise.

attributes.name: points_expiry_date
attributes.description: The date on which the points earned in this transaction will expire if not redeemed. Populated only for earn and bonus transaction types. Null for redeem, expire, and adjust transactions. Supports FIFO expiration logic and member communication.

attributes.name: processed_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this ledger entry was committed to the loyalty system of record. May differ from transaction_timestamp for batch processing or delayed reconciliation. Used for ETL audit and SLA monitoring.

attributes.name: restaurant_number
attributes.description: Human-readable store or unit number for the restaurant location. Denormalized for operational reporting and guest service. Null for non-location-specific transactions.

attributes.name: source_order_number
attributes.description: Human-readable order number or receipt identifier from the POS or OLO system. Provides guest-facing reference for customer service inquiries. Null for non-order transactions.

attributes.name: source_system_code
attributes.description: Code identifying the operational system that originated this points transaction. Supports multi-system integration and data lineage tracking. Examples: micros_pos (Oracle MICROS POS), olo_platform (Olo Digital Ordering), salesforce_crm (Salesforce Marketing Cloud), loyalty_engine (core loyalty platform), admin_portal (back-office admin tool).

attributes.name: transaction_timestamp
attributes.description: The precise date and time when this points transaction occurred in the source system. Represents the business event time, not the ETL load time. Critical for sequencing and audit.

attributes.name: transaction_type
attributes.description: Classification of the points movement: earn (points accrued from purchase or activity), redeem (points spent on rewards), expire (points lapsed due to inactivity or time limit), adjust (manual correction by support or system), bonus (promotional award), reversal (cancellation of prior transaction).

attributes.name: voided_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this transaction was voided by a reversal. Null if the transaction has not been voided. Supports audit trail and reconciliation.

products.name: reward
products.description: Master catalog of all redeemable rewards available in the Restaurants loyalty rewards catalog — free menu items, discounts, merchandise, experiences, and partner offers. Captures reward name, reward type (food, discount, merchandise, experience), points cost, monetary value equivalent, redemption channel eligibility (POS, OLO, app), availability window (start/end date), quantity limit, restaurant applicability scope (all units, specific markets, specific formats), and active status. Distinct from promotional offers which are push-based incentives.

attributes.name: reward_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the loyalty reward. Primary key.

attributes.name: brand_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to restaurant.brand. Business justification: Rewards are brand-scoped in multi-brand restaurant groups. Brand-level reward catalog management, franchise royalty cost allocation for reward redemptions, and brand marketing reporting all require li

attributes.name: franchisee_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to franchise.franchisee. Business justification: Allows franchisees to manage their own reward catalog, needed for localized promotions and compliance reporting.

attributes.name: free_item_stock_item_id
attributes.business_glossary_term: Free Item Stock Item Id (Foreign Key)
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_item. Business justification: Reward definitions for free-item rewards must reference the inventory stock_item to calculate COGS and theoretical food cost impact. Foodservice operators track reward fulfillment cost per stock item

attributes.name: gl_account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Rewards carry cost_of_goods_sold and monetary_value attributes requiring a GL account mapping for reward cost accrual and promotional expense posting. Foodservice finance teams map each reward type to

attributes.name: menu_item_id
attributes.description: Reference to the specific menu item associated with this reward (for food and beverage rewards). Links to the menu item master catalog. Null for non-menu rewards.

attributes.name: modified_by_employee_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Reward catalog change control requires linking reward modifications to the responsible employee for accountability and audit. Foodservice loyalty teams track who changed reward point costs or availabi

attributes.name: origin_campaign_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Campaign‑Reward mapping needed for tracking which marketing campaign created a specific reward, supporting performance analysis of campaign‑driven incentives.

attributes.name: program_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to loyalty.program. Business justification: Rewards in the loyalty catalog belong to a specific loyalty program. A reward's availability, point cost, and redemption rules are governed by the program it belongs to. Adding program_id to reward es

attributes.name: promotion_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to marketing.promotion. Business justification: Loyalty rewards (e.g., free item) are often the fulfillment mechanism for a specific marketing promotion. Linking reward to promotion enables promotion cost reporting that includes loyalty reward COGS

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supply.supply_supplier. Business justification: Needed for Reward‑Supplier cost tracking and compliance; loyalty rewards are sourced from suppliers, and reporting requires linking each reward to its supplying supplier.

attributes.name: availability_end_date
attributes.description: Date when this reward is no longer available for redemption. Null for evergreen rewards with no expiration. Used for Limited Time Offer (LTO) rewards and seasonal promotions.

attributes.name: availability_start_date
attributes.description: Date when this reward becomes available for redemption by loyalty members. Used for Limited Time Offer (LTO) rewards and seasonal promotions.

attributes.name: combinable_with_other_offers
attributes.description: Indicates whether this reward can be combined with other promotional offers, coupons, or discounts in a single transaction. True if combinable, False if exclusive.

attributes.name: cost_of_goods_sold
attributes.description: Direct cost to the business to fulfill this reward, including food cost, packaging, and direct labor. Used for profitability analysis, loyalty program Return on Investment (ROI) calculation, and financial accrual. Expressed in USD.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this reward record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.

attributes.name: daypart_restriction
attributes.description: Specifies which daypart(s) this reward can be redeemed during: breakfast, lunch, dinner, late night, or all day. Used to manage product mix (PMIX) and drive traffic during specific service periods.

attributes.name: discount_type
attributes.description: Type of discount mechanism applied: percentage (e.g., 10% off), fixed amount (e.g., $5 off), free item (no charge), buy-one-get-one (BOGO), or none (for non-discount rewards like merchandise or experiences).

attributes.name: discount_value
attributes.description: Numeric value of the discount. For percentage discounts, this is the percentage (e.g., 15.00 for 15%). For fixed amount discounts, this is the dollar amount (e.g., 5.00 for $5 off). Null for free items and non-discount rewards.

attributes.name: featured_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this reward is featured prominently in the loyalty app and marketing campaigns. True if featured, False if standard catalog placement. Used to drive awareness and redemption of strategic rewards.

attributes.name: format_restriction_list
attributes.description: Comma-separated list of restaurant format types where this reward is valid (e.g., QSR, casual, fine-dining, food court). Null if applicable to all formats. Used when restaurant_applicability_scope is 'specific_formats'.

attributes.name: image_url
attributes.description: URL path to the reward image asset displayed in the loyalty app, Online Ordering (OLO) platform, and marketing materials. Used for visual merchandising and guest engagement.

attributes.name: market_restriction_list
attributes.description: Comma-separated list of market codes or geographic regions where this reward is valid. Null if applicable to all markets. Used when restaurant_applicability_scope is 'specific_markets'.

attributes.name: minimum_purchase_amount
attributes.description: Minimum transaction amount required to redeem this reward. Null if no minimum purchase is required. Used to protect Average Check Value (ACV) and prevent low-value transactions.

attributes.name: modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this reward record was last modified. Used for audit trail, change tracking, and data synchronization across systems.

attributes.name: monetary_value
attributes.description: Estimated dollar value equivalent of the reward for financial reporting, liability accrual, and Average Check Value (ACV) impact analysis. Expressed in USD.

attributes.name: partner_name
attributes.description: Name of the third-party partner providing this reward (for partner offers). Null for internally-fulfilled rewards. Used for co-branded rewards and strategic partnerships.

attributes.name: partner_offer_code
attributes.description: External offer code or voucher code provided by the partner for redemption tracking. Null for internally-fulfilled rewards.

attributes.name: points_cost
attributes.description: Number of loyalty points required to redeem this reward. Used to calculate point liability and drive guest engagement.

attributes.name: quantity_limit_per_member
attributes.description: Maximum number of times a single loyalty member can redeem this reward during the availability window. Null indicates no limit.

attributes.name: redemption_channel_app
attributes.description: Indicates whether this reward can be redeemed exclusively through the branded mobile app (distinct from general OLO). True if eligible, False if not.

attributes.name: redemption_channel_drive_thru
attributes.description: Indicates whether this reward can be redeemed at Drive-Thru (DT) service points. True if eligible, False if not.

attributes.name: redemption_channel_olo
attributes.description: Indicates whether this reward can be redeemed through Online Ordering (OLO) platforms including web and mobile app. True if eligible, False if not.

attributes.name: redemption_channel_pos
attributes.business_glossary_term: Point of Sale (POS) Redemption Channel Eligibility
attributes.description: Indicates whether this reward can be redeemed at Point of Sale (POS) terminals in restaurant locations. True if eligible, False if not.

attributes.name: redemption_channel_third_party_delivery
attributes.description: Indicates whether this reward can be redeemed through Third-Party Delivery (3PD) platforms such as DoorDash, Uber Eats, or Grubhub. True if eligible, False if not.

attributes.name: redemption_count
attributes.description: Total number of times this reward has been redeemed by loyalty members since inception. Used for popularity analysis, inventory planning, and loyalty program performance reporting.

attributes.name: restaurant_applicability_scope
attributes.description: Defines which restaurant units can honor this reward: all units (system-wide), specific markets (geographic regions), specific formats (Quick-Service Restaurant (QSR), casual, fine-dining), franchise only, or company-owned only.

attributes.name: reward_code
attributes.description: Externally-known unique alphanumeric code for the reward, used in Point of Sale (POS) and Online Ordering (OLO) systems for redemption lookup.

attributes.name: reward_description
attributes.description: Detailed description of the reward, including terms, conditions, and guest-facing messaging used in marketing materials and the loyalty app.

attributes.name: reward_name
attributes.description: Human-readable name of the reward displayed to guests in the loyalty app, Online Ordering (OLO) platform, and Point of Sale (POS) system.

attributes.name: reward_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the reward: active (available for redemption), inactive (temporarily unavailable), pending (scheduled for future activation), expired (past availability end date), or discontinued (permanently removed from catalog).

attributes.name: reward_type
attributes.description: Classification of the reward by category: food item (free menu item), beverage (free drink), discount (percentage or dollar off), merchandise (branded goods), experience (event access, VIP treatment), or partner offer (third-party rewards).

attributes.name: tax_treatment
attributes.description: Indicates how sales tax is applied to this reward: taxable (tax calculated on reward value), non-taxable (no tax applied), or tax included (tax already embedded in points cost). Required for accurate Point of Sale (POS) processing and financial reporting.

attributes.name: terms_and_conditions
attributes.description: Legal terms and conditions governing the redemption and use of this reward. Includes restrictions, exclusions, and compliance language required by Federal Trade Commission (FTC) regulations.

attributes.name: tier_eligibility
attributes.description: Specifies which loyalty membership tier(s) are eligible to redeem this reward. Used to create tier-exclusive rewards that drive tier progression and engagement.

attributes.name: total_quantity_limit
attributes.description: Maximum total number of redemptions allowed across all loyalty members for this reward. Used for limited-supply rewards and exclusive experiences. Null indicates no limit.

attributes.name: created_by
attributes.description: User identifier or system account that created this reward record. Used for audit trail and accountability.

products.name: redemption
products.description: Transactional record of every burn event by a loyalty member — both reward catalog redemptions (member-initiated pull from rewards catalog) and offer redemptions (program-initiated push incentives). Captures member reference, redemption type (reward, offer), reward or offer reference, redemption channel (POS, OLO, drive-thru, kiosk), restaurant unit, order reference, redemption timestamp, points deducted, monetary discount applied, offer assignment reference (for offer-type), delivery confirmation details, redemption status (pending, fulfilled, voided, expired, duplicate_attempt), and fulfillment confirmation. Unified SSOT for ALL burn events — no other product in this domain records redemption transactions. Enables holistic redemption analytics, PMIX impact analysis, offer ROI measurement, and reward popularity tracking.

attributes.name: redemption_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the loyalty reward redemption transaction. Primary key.

attributes.name: campaign_id
attributes.description: Reference to the marketing campaign that drove this redemption, if the reward was part of a targeted promotional campaign.

attributes.name: channel_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to order.channel. Business justification: Redemption channel normalization: redemption.channel is a plain-text denormalized attribute. Replacing with FK to order.channel normalizes the channel taxonomy, enabling channel-level redemption rate

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Redemption discount amounts must be allocated to a cost center for restaurant-level P&L and promotional discount variance reporting. Foodservice finance teams track 'redemption discount cost center al

attributes.name: employee_id
attributes.description: Reference to the employee who processed the redemption transaction at the POS, if applicable to in-store redemptions.

attributes.name: financial_period_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.financial_period. Business justification: Redemption discount expenses must be assigned to a financial period for revenue recognition and discount expense period-end reporting. Foodservice finance teams require 'redemption discount period-end

attributes.name: gl_account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Redemption creates expense/revenue entries; linking to GL account enables accurate journal posting and audit trails.

attributes.name: guest_order_id
attributes.description: Reference to the order transaction where the reward was applied and redeemed.

attributes.name: member_id
attributes.description: Reference to the loyalty program member who redeemed the reward.

attributes.name: menu_item_id
attributes.description: Reference to the specific menu item that was redeemed or discounted through this reward, if applicable to item-specific rewards.

attributes.name: pos_terminal_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the POS terminal or kiosk device where the redemption was processed, sourced from Oracle MICROS POS system.

attributes.name: promotion_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to marketing.promotion. Business justification: Loyalty reward redemptions co-occur with active marketing promotions (e.g., BOGO + points). Linking redemption to promotion enables transaction-level reconciliation of loyalty redemptions against prom

attributes.name: redeemed_stock_item_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_item. Business justification: When a free-item loyalty reward is redeemed, the specific stock_item consumed must be recorded for inventory depletion, COGS tracking, and food cost variance reporting. Foodservice operators require t

attributes.name: redemption_restaurant_unit_id
attributes.description: Reference to the restaurant unit where the redemption occurred.

attributes.name: redemption_unit_id
attributes.description: Reference to the restaurant unit where the redemption occurred.
