attributes.description: Phone number of the primary bank contact. Classified as confidential organizational contact data.

attributes.name: reconciliation_frequency
attributes.description: How often this bank account is reconciled against the general ledger: daily (high-volume operating accounts), weekly (moderate activity), monthly (low activity or reserve accounts).
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-TR Bank Reconciliation, Internal Controls Policy

attributes.name: signatory_list
attributes.description: Comma-separated list of employee IDs or names authorized to sign checks or approve wire transfers on this account. Confidential for fraud prevention.
attributes.references: Internal Controls and Segregation of Duties Policy

attributes.name: swift_code
attributes.description: Bank Identifier Code (BIC) or SWIFT code for international wire transfers. 8 or 11 character alphanumeric code identifying the bank and branch.

products.name: financial_period
products.description: Master reference table for financial_period.

attributes.name: financial_period_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the financial period record. Primary key.

attributes.name: comparative_prior_period_id
attributes.description: Foreign key reference to the immediately preceding period (e.g., prior month). Used for sequential period-over-period analysis.

attributes.name: comparative_prior_year_period_id
attributes.description: Foreign key reference to the equivalent period in the prior fiscal year. Used for year-over-year comparative analysis and SSS reporting.

attributes.name: prior_financial_period_id
attributes.description: Self-referencing FK on financial_period (prior_financial_period_id)

attributes.name: accounting_principle
attributes.description: Accounting standard applied in this ledger: US_GAAP (United States Generally Accepted Accounting Principles), IFRS (International Financial Reporting Standards), local_GAAP (country-specific standards), management (internal reporting).

attributes.name: adjustment_posting_allowed_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether adjustment postings (corrections, reclassifications) are allowed in this period after initial close. Used for controlled reopening during audit or correction cycles.

attributes.name: adjustment_reason
attributes.description: Reason code or description for why the period is an adjustment period.

attributes.name: audit_required_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this period is subject to external audit review. Typically true for year-end periods and quarterly periods for publicly traded entities.

attributes.name: audit_trail_flag
attributes.description: True if changes to this period are tracked for audit purposes.

attributes.name: calendar_days_count
attributes.description: Total number of calendar days in the period. Used for daily average calculations and period normalization in financial analysis.

attributes.name: calendar_month
attributes.description: Month number (1‑12) in the calendar year.

attributes.name: calendar_quarter
attributes.description: Quarter number (1‑4) in the calendar year.

attributes.name: calendar_year
attributes.description: Gregorian calendar year of the period.

attributes.name: close_actual_date
attributes.description: Actual date when the period-end close process was completed and the period status changed to closed. Null if period is still open.

attributes.name: close_scheduled_date
attributes.description: Target date for completing the period-end close process. Used for close calendar planning and tracking adherence to financial reporting deadlines.

attributes.name: close_variance_days
attributes.description: Number of days between scheduled and actual close date (positive if late, negative if early). Used to track period-end close efficiency and identify process improvement opportunities.

attributes.name: company_code
attributes.description: Four-character alphanumeric code identifying the legal entity in SAP S/4HANA. Links this period to a specific company within the multi-entity structure.

attributes.name: consolidation_group_code
attributes.description: Code identifying the consolidation group for multi‑entity reporting.

attributes.name: consolidation_required_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this period requires multi-entity consolidation processing. True for periods where intercompany eliminations and group reporting are performed.

attributes.name: created_by_user
attributes.description: User ID or username of the person who created this financial period record. Used for audit trail and accountability.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this financial period record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.

attributes.name: day_count
attributes.description: Total count of calendar days covered by the period.

attributes.name: effective_end_date
attributes.description: Date when the period ceases to be effective (nullable).

attributes.name: effective_start_date
attributes.description: Date when the period becomes effective for reporting purposes.

attributes.name: end_date
attributes.description: Last calendar day of the financial period.

attributes.name: exchange_rate_to_reporting
attributes.description: Conversion rate from functional currency to reporting currency for the period.

attributes.name: exchange_rate_type
attributes.description: Type of exchange rate applied for currency translation in this period: average (period average rate for P&L), closing (period-end rate for balance sheet), budget (planning rate), historical (acquisition date rate for fixed assets).

attributes.name: financial_period_description
attributes.description: Optional free‑text description of the period.

attributes.name: financial_statement_type
attributes.description: Type of financial statement the period primarily supports.

attributes.name: fiscal_month
attributes.description: Month number (1‑12) within the fiscal year.

attributes.name: fiscal_period_code
attributes.description: Standard code used by SAP for the fiscal period (e.g., "2023P01").

attributes.name: fiscal_quarter
attributes.description: Quarter number (1‑4) within the fiscal year.

attributes.name: fiscal_year
attributes.description: Four-digit fiscal year to which this period belongs. May differ from calendar year depending on fiscal year variant configuration.

attributes.name: fiscal_year_variant
attributes.description: Two-character code defining the fiscal calendar structure (number of periods, special periods, year-end rules) used by the company code. Determines how the fiscal year is divided into posting periods.

attributes.name: holiday_indicator
attributes.description: Degree to which holidays affect the period.

attributes.name: is_adjustment_period
attributes.description: True if the period is used for post‑close adjustments.

attributes.name: is_current
attributes.description: True if this period is the active reporting period.

attributes.name: is_estimated
attributes.description: True if the period values are based on estimates rather than actuals.

attributes.name: is_holiday_period
attributes.description: True if the period includes a recognized holiday schedule.

attributes.name: is_interim
attributes.description: True if the period is an interim (non‑full) reporting period.

attributes.name: ledger_type
attributes.description: Classification of the ledger: leading (primary GAAP ledger for statutory reporting), non_leading (parallel IFRS or local GAAP ledger), special_purpose (management or segment reporting ledger).

attributes.name: lock_flag
attributes.description: True when the period is locked for posting to prevent changes.

attributes.name: modified_by_user
attributes.description: User ID or username of the person who last modified this financial period record. Used for audit trail and change tracking.

attributes.name: modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this financial period record was last modified. Updated whenever period status, close dates, or other attributes change.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-text field for recording period-specific notes, exceptions, or special circumstances (e.g., 'Includes acquisition of XYZ brand', 'Impacted by COVID-19 closures', 'Leap year adjustment').

attributes.name: period_category
attributes.description: Business classification of the period data.

attributes.name: period_end_date
attributes.description: Last calendar date of the financial period. Defines the end of the posting window for transactions assigned to this period.

attributes.name: period_key
attributes.description: Compact code representing the period (e.g., FY2023Q1, FY2023M04).

attributes.name: period_name
attributes.description: Human-readable name or label for the period (e.g., 'January 2024', 'Q1 FY2024', 'Period 13 - Year-End Adjustments'). Used for reporting and user interface display.

attributes.name: period_number
attributes.description: Sequential period number within the fiscal year (typically 1-12 for monthly periods, or 1-13 for 4-4-5 retail calendars). Special periods (13, 14, 15, 16) used for year-end adjustments.

attributes.name: period_sequence_number
attributes.description: Sequential order of the period within its fiscal year.

attributes.name: period_start_date
attributes.description: First calendar date of the financial period. Defines the beginning of the posting window for transactions assigned to this period.

attributes.name: period_status
attributes.description: Current posting status of the period: not_opened (future period, no posting allowed), open (active posting allowed), closed (period-end close completed, limited posting), locked (archived, no posting allowed).

attributes.name: period_type
attributes.description: Classification of the period: regular (standard posting period), special (year-end adjustment period 13-16), or adjustment (mid-year correction period).

attributes.name: posting_allowed_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether new transactions can be posted to this period. True if period is open for posting, false if closed or locked.

attributes.name: quarter_number
attributes.description: Fiscal quarter number (1-4) to which this period belongs. Supports quarterly financial reporting and Same-Store Sales (SSS) analysis.
attributes.references: GAAP Financial Reporting, SEC Regulation S-X

attributes.name: reporting_currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for consolidated financial reporting in this period (e.g., USD for US Dollar, EUR for Euro). Used for multi-currency consolidation.

attributes.name: segment_reporting_flag
attributes.description: True if the period is used for segment‑level reporting.

attributes.name: source_system
attributes.description: Originating system that defines the period (e.g., SAP S/4HANA).

attributes.name: sss_eligible_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this period is eligible for Same-Store Sales (SSS) or Comparable Store Sales (Comp Sales) analysis. Typically true for periods where stores have been open for at least 12-13 months.

attributes.name: start_date
attributes.description: First calendar day of the financial period.

attributes.name: tax_reporting_flag
attributes.description: True if the period is used for tax reporting calculations.

attributes.name: tax_reporting_period_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this period is a tax reporting period requiring submission of tax returns (e.g., quarterly estimated tax, annual tax filing). Used for tax compliance tracking.
attributes.references: IRS Tax Code, Local Tax Regulations

attributes.name: working_days_count
attributes.description: Number of business working days in the period, excluding weekends and holidays. Used for normalizing operational metrics and labor cost analysis.

attributes.name: year_period_key
attributes.description: Concatenated key combining fiscal year and period number (format: YYYYPPP, e.g., 2024001 for FY2024 Period 1). Used for sorting and comparative period analysis.

products.name: payment_run
products.description: Master reference table for payment_run. Referenced by payment_run_id.

attributes.name: payment_run_id
attributes.description: Primary key for payment_run

attributes.name: employee_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the employee who triggered the payment run.

attributes.name: financial_period_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.financial_period. Business justification: Payment runs in SAP FI-AP are executed within a specific financial period. The fiscal_period (STRING) and fiscal_year (INT) columns are denormalized period identifiers replaced by a FK to the financia

attributes.name: reversed_payment_run_id
attributes.description: Self-referencing FK on payment_run (reversed_payment_run_id)

attributes.name: actual_completion_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the payment run finished processing.

attributes.name: approval_status
attributes.description: Current approval state of the payment run.

attributes.name: approved_by
attributes.description: Identifier of the employee who approved the payment run.

attributes.name: approved_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the payment run was approved.

attributes.name: batch_reference
attributes.description: External reference or identifier for the batch used by downstream systems.

attributes.name: cost_center_code
attributes.description: Internal cost center associated with the payment run for accounting allocation.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the payment run record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three‑letter ISO currency code for the payment run amounts.

attributes.name: error_count
attributes.description: Number of errors encountered during processing of the run.

attributes.name: external_reference_code
attributes.description: Identifier used by external systems (e.g., banking partner) to reference the run.

attributes.name: is_automated
attributes.description: Indicates whether the payment run was generated automatically by the system.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Additional free‑form notes captured for the payment run.

attributes.name: payment_channel
attributes.description: Technical channel through which the payment run was processed.

attributes.name: payment_method
attributes.description: Instrument used to execute the payments.

attributes.name: payment_run_description
attributes.description: Free‑form text describing the purpose or context of the payment run.

attributes.name: payment_run_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the payment run.

attributes.name: payment_run_type
attributes.description: Classification of the run based on its business purpose.

attributes.name: processing_time_seconds
attributes.description: Total elapsed time in seconds for the run to complete.

attributes.name: region_code
attributes.description: Geographic region identifier for the payment run (e.g., NA, EU, APAC).

attributes.name: retry_flag
attributes.description: True if the run is a retry of a previously failed run.

attributes.name: run_number
attributes.description: Business-visible sequential number assigned to the payment run.

attributes.name: run_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the payment run was initiated in the business process.

attributes.name: scheduled_date
attributes.description: Planned calendar date for the payment run execution.

attributes.name: settlement_date
attributes.description: Date on which the payments are settled with banks or vendors.

attributes.name: total_discount_amount
attributes.description: Total discounts deducted from the gross amount.

attributes.name: total_gross_amount
attributes.description: Sum of all payment amounts before taxes, discounts, and fees.

attributes.name: total_net_amount
attributes.description: Net amount payable after taxes and discounts.

attributes.name: total_tax_amount
attributes.description: Aggregate tax amount applied to the payment run.

attributes.name: transaction_count
attributes.description: Count of individual payment transactions included in the run.

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp of the most recent update to the payment run record.

domains.name: realestate
domains.description: Manages site selection, lease negotiations, property acquisition, facility design and construction, CAM (Common Area Maintenance) charges, landlord relationships, lease administration, NRO development pipeline, facility R&M (Repairs and Maintenance), and CapEx planning for new builds and remodels. Tracks lease obligations for IFRS 16 / ASC 842 compliance.
domains.references: IFRS 16 / ASC 842 Lease Accounting, Local Zoning Regulations, FranConnect Site Selection

products.name: site
products.description: Master record for every physical site under evaluation, active operation, or disposition in the restaurant real estate portfolio. Captures site identification, geographic coordinates, full address, market classification, trade area demographics, traffic counts (ADT), visibility scores, zoning classification, site type (pad, inline, endcap, freestanding, drive-thru capable), ownership status (owned/leased/ground-lease), parcel size, and current lifecycle stage (prospecting, under negotiation, LOI executed, active, temporarily closed, permanently closed, disposed). Serves as the SSOT for all site-level real estate data across the NRO pipeline and existing portfolio. Links to restaurant unit in the restaurant domain.
products.reference: Site Master / Real Estate Portfolio Record

attributes.name: site_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the physical site record. Primary key for the site entity.

attributes.name: agreement_id
attributes.description: Reference to the franchise agreement governing this site, if operated under franchise model. Links site to franchisee obligations and royalty calculations.

attributes.name: franchisee_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to franchise.franchisee. Business justification: Site Assignment Report requires each property to be linked to the operating franchisee for lease compliance and royalty calculations.

attributes.name: landlord_id
attributes.description: FK to realestate.landlord

attributes.name: legal_entity_id
attributes.description: Reference to the legal entity or franchisee responsible for operating this site. Used for financial consolidation and P&L (Profit and Loss) reporting.

attributes.name: profit_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Each restaurant site corresponds to a profit center for unit-level P&L reporting. Foodservice operators run profit center accounting at the site level for occupancy cost analysis. profit_center links

attributes.name: territory_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to franchise.territory. Business justification: Every restaurant site belongs to a franchise territory governing exclusivity, royalty rates, and development obligations. Linking site to territory enables territory compliance enforcement, competitiv

attributes.name: accessibility_score
attributes.description: Quantitative assessment of ease of ingress and egress, typically scored 0-100. Considers turn lanes, traffic signals, median cuts, and parking availability.

attributes.name: acquisition_date
attributes.description: Date on which the site was acquired or lease was executed. Used for portfolio tenure analysis and depreciation start date for owned properties.

attributes.name: address_line_1
attributes.description: Primary street address line for the site location. Business-confidential organizational contact data.

attributes.name: address_line_2
attributes.description: Secondary address line for suite, unit, or building identifiers. Business-confidential organizational contact data.

attributes.name: building_square_footage
attributes.description: Total interior square footage of the restaurant building. Used for lease valuation, R&M (Repairs and Maintenance) budgeting, and utility forecasting.
attributes.references: BOMA (Building Owners and Managers Association) Measurement Standards

attributes.name: city
attributes.description: City or municipality name where the site is located.

attributes.name: closure_date
attributes.description: Date on which the restaurant ceased operations at this site. Used for portfolio reporting, lease termination tracking, and asset disposition.

attributes.name: country_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO country code for the site location. Determines regulatory jurisdiction and reporting requirements.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this site record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage and audit trail.

attributes.name: daily_traffic_count
attributes.description: Average daily vehicle or pedestrian traffic count passing the site. Critical visibility and accessibility metric for site selection scoring.

attributes.name: drive_thru_capable
attributes.description: Indicates whether the site configuration and zoning permit a drive-thru lane. Critical for QSR (Quick-Service Restaurant) operational model and throughput forecasting.

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this site record was last updated. Used for change tracking and data quality monitoring.

attributes.name: latitude
attributes.description: Geographic latitude coordinate of the site in decimal degrees. Used for GIS mapping, trade area analysis, and proximity calculations.

attributes.name: lease_commencement_date
attributes.description: Date on which the lease agreement becomes effective and rent obligations begin. Critical for IFRS 16 / ASC 842 right-of-use asset recognition.

attributes.name: lease_expiration_date
attributes.description: Date on which the current lease term expires. Used for lease renewal planning, portfolio optimization, and financial forecasting.

attributes.name: lifecycle_stage
attributes.description: Current stage of the site within the NRO (New Restaurant Opening) pipeline or existing portfolio lifecycle. Drives workflow, reporting, and financial treatment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: prospecting|under_negotiation|approved|under_construction|active|temporarily_closed|closed|disposed — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: longitude
attributes.description: Geographic longitude coordinate of the site in decimal degrees. Used for GIS mapping, trade area analysis, and proximity calculations.

attributes.name: lot_square_footage
attributes.description: Total land area of the site parcel in square feet. Used for property valuation, CAM (Common Area Maintenance) calculations, and site development planning.
attributes.references: BOMA (Building Owners and Managers Association) Measurement Standards

attributes.name: market_classification
attributes.description: Demographic and geographic market segment classification. Drives menu mix, pricing strategy, and operational model selection.

attributes.name: monthly_base_rent
attributes.description: Fixed monthly rent payment obligation under the lease agreement, excluding variable charges. Used for P&L (Profit and Loss) forecasting and lease liability calculation.

attributes.name: monthly_cam_charges
attributes.description: Monthly charges for common area maintenance, property taxes, and insurance allocated to the tenant. Variable component of total occupancy cost.
attributes.references: BOMA (Building Owners and Managers Association) CAM Standards

attributes.name: opening_date
attributes.description: Date on which the restaurant began serving customers at this site. Used for SSS (Same-Store Sales) eligibility and maturity curve analysis.

attributes.name: ownership_status
attributes.description: Legal ownership structure of the site. Determines accounting treatment under IFRS 16 / ASC 842 lease standards and CapEx (Capital Expenditure) responsibility.

attributes.name: parking_spaces
attributes.description: Number of dedicated parking spaces available at the site. Must meet local zoning minimums and impacts customer accessibility scoring.

attributes.name: percentage_rent_rate
attributes.description: Percentage of sales above the threshold that is paid as additional rent. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0600 for 6%).

attributes.name: percentage_rent_threshold
attributes.description: Annual sales breakpoint above which percentage rent becomes due to the landlord. Common in retail lease structures for high-traffic locations.

attributes.name: postal_code
attributes.description: Postal or ZIP code for the site address. Business-confidential organizational contact data. Used for trade area analysis and demographic mapping.

attributes.name: projected_auv
attributes.description: Forecasted annual sales volume for the site based on site selection model. Used for NRO (New Restaurant Opening) approval and ROI (Return on Investment) analysis.

attributes.name: seating_capacity
attributes.description: Total number of customer seats within the dining area. Used for capacity planning, labor scheduling, and health department permitting.

attributes.name: site_name
attributes.description: Human-readable name or designation for the site, often incorporating location landmarks or address elements for easy identification.

attributes.name: site_number
attributes.description: Externally-known unique business identifier for the site, used across real estate, operations, and finance systems. Typically assigned during site prospecting phase.

attributes.name: site_type
attributes.description: Physical configuration and placement classification of the site within its development or property. Critical for site selection scoring and operational model determination. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: freestanding|inline|endcap|pad|food_court|airport|travel_center — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
attributes.references: NRA (National Restaurant Association) Site Classification Standards

attributes.name: state_province
attributes.description: Two-letter state or province code for the site location. Used for tax jurisdiction, regulatory compliance, and market segmentation.

attributes.name: total_capex_investment
attributes.description: Total capital investment for site development, including land acquisition, construction, equipment, and pre-opening costs. Used for ROI calculation and capital planning.

attributes.name: visibility_score
attributes.description: Quantitative assessment of site visibility from primary traffic routes, typically scored 0-100. Incorporates signage sightlines, obstructions, and approach angles.

attributes.name: zoning_classification
attributes.description: Local government zoning designation for the site parcel. Determines permitted uses, building restrictions, and signage allowances.

products.name: lease
products.description: Authoritative master record for every lease agreement governing a restaurant location, including ground leases, building leases, and sublease arrangements. Captures lease type, commencement and expiration dates, base rent, rent escalation schedule, renewal option terms, termination clauses, co-tenancy provisions, exclusive use rights, permitted use language, landlord entity, guarantor, and IFRS 16 / ASC 842 classification (operating vs. finance lease). Tracks right-of-use (ROU) asset value, lease liability, discount rate, and remaining lease term for accounting compliance. Includes structured clause abstractions: clause type (rent escalation, renewal option, termination right, co-tenancy, exclusivity, assignment, subletting, HVAC responsibility, CAM cap, audit right), clause text, effective date range, financial impact flag, and compliance obligation indicator. Links to site and landlord records. Serves as the SSOT for all lease agreement data including clause-level detail.
products.reference: Lease Agreement / IFRS 16 / ASC 842 Lease Record

attributes.name: lease_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the lease agreement. Primary key for the lease entity.

attributes.name: agreement_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to franchise.agreement. Business justification: In franchise foodservice, the franchise agreement governs the lease relationship — percentage rent thresholds, permitted use, and term alignment must match agreement terms. Linking lease to franchise

attributes.name: franchisee_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to franchise.franchisee. Business justification: Lease Management process tracks which franchisee is the tenant; needed for rent payment scheduling, compliance, and financial reporting.

attributes.name: gl_account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Under IFRS 16/ASC 842, leases map to ROU asset and lease liability GL accounts. Linking lease to its primary GL account supports lease accounting schedules and balance sheet classification — a standar

attributes.name: landlord_id
attributes.description: Reference to the landlord entity that owns the leased property.

attributes.name: legal_entity_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Leases are executed by a specific legal entity; under IFRS 16/ASC 842, ROU assets and lease liabilities must be recorded under the correct legal entity. Foodservice multi-entity operators require this

attributes.name: site_id
attributes.description: Reference to the restaurant site or location governed by this lease agreement.

attributes.name: accounting_classification
attributes.description: Lease classification for financial reporting purposes under IFRS 16 / ASC 842: operating lease (most common for real estate) or finance lease (if lease transfers substantially all risks and rewards of ownership).

attributes.name: base_rent_amount
attributes.description: Fixed monthly or annual base rent payment amount specified in the lease agreement, excluding variable charges such as CAM, taxes, and percentage rent.

attributes.name: base_rent_frequency
attributes.description: Frequency at which base rent payments are due: monthly, quarterly, or annually.

attributes.name: cam_charges_annual
attributes.description: Estimated annual Common Area Maintenance charges for shared property expenses such as landscaping, parking lot maintenance, snow removal, and common utilities.

attributes.name: co_tenancy_clause_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the lease includes a co-tenancy provision allowing rent reduction or termination if anchor tenants or a minimum occupancy threshold is not maintained.

attributes.name: commencement_date
attributes.description: Date when the lease agreement becomes effective and rent obligations begin. Critical for IFRS 16 / ASC 842 right-of-use asset recognition.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this lease record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this lease (e.g., USD, CAD, EUR). Critical for multi-currency lease portfolios.

attributes.name: discount_rate
attributes.description: Interest rate used to discount future lease payments to present value for IFRS 16 / ASC 842 calculations. Represents the rate the lessee would pay to borrow funds to purchase a similar asset.

attributes.name: document_url
attributes.description: Reference URL or file path to the digitally stored lease agreement document for audit and compliance purposes.

attributes.name: exclusive_use_rights
attributes.description: Description of exclusive use provisions that restrict the landlord from leasing to competing restaurant concepts within the same property or trade area.

attributes.name: execution_date
attributes.description: Date when the lease agreement was signed by all parties, establishing the legal contract.

attributes.name: expiration_date
attributes.description: Date when the initial lease term ends, excluding any renewal options. Used to calculate remaining lease term for accounting and planning purposes.

attributes.name: guarantor_entity
attributes.description: Name of the corporate entity providing a lease guarantee, typically the parent company for franchise or subsidiary-operated locations.

attributes.name: insurance_annual
attributes.description: Estimated annual property insurance premium obligation passed through to the tenant under a triple-net (NNN) lease structure.

attributes.name: lease_number
attributes.description: Externally-known unique business identifier for the lease agreement, used in legal documents and correspondence.

attributes.name: lease_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the lease agreement: draft (under negotiation), active (in force), expired (term ended), terminated (ended early), renewed (extended), or pending renewal (approaching expiration with renewal option).

attributes.name: lease_type
attributes.description: Classification of the lease arrangement: ground lease (land only), building lease (structure and land), sublease (tenant subleases from another tenant), or license agreement.

attributes.name: liability_value
attributes.description: Initial measurement of the lease liability recognized on the balance sheet under IFRS 16 / ASC 842, representing the present value of future lease payments discounted at the incremental borrowing rate.

attributes.name: modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this lease record was last modified in the system.

attributes.name: percentage_rent_rate
attributes.description: Percentage of gross sales above the threshold that is paid as additional rent to the landlord. Typically ranges from 3% to 8% in QSR leases.

attributes.name: percentage_rent_threshold
attributes.description: Annual sales breakpoint above which percentage rent becomes due. Common in retail leases where landlord receives a percentage of sales exceeding this threshold.

attributes.name: permitted_use
attributes.description: Legal description of the permitted business activities and operations allowed under the lease, typically specifying restaurant or foodservice use.

attributes.name: property_tax_annual
attributes.description: Estimated annual property tax obligation passed through to the tenant under a triple-net (NNN) lease structure.

attributes.name: remaining_term_months
attributes.description: Number of months remaining from the current date until lease expiration, used for lease liability calculations under IFRS 16 / ASC 842.

attributes.name: renewal_notice_days
attributes.description: Number of days prior to lease expiration by which the tenant must notify the landlord of intent to exercise a renewal option. Typically 180-365 days.

attributes.name: renewal_option_count
attributes.description: Number of renewal option periods available to the tenant after the initial lease term expires. Common in restaurant leases to provide long-term site control.

attributes.name: renewal_option_term_months
attributes.description: Duration in months of each renewal option period. Typically 5 years (60 months) per option in QSR leases.

attributes.name: rent_escalation_rate
attributes.description: Annual percentage rate of rent increase if escalation type is fixed percentage. Null if escalation is CPI-indexed or fair market value.

attributes.name: rent_escalation_type
attributes.description: Method by which base rent increases over the lease term: fixed percentage (e.g., 3% annually), CPI-indexed (tied to Consumer Price Index), fair market value (periodic reappraisal), or none (flat rent).

attributes.name: rou_asset_value
attributes.description: Initial measurement of the right-of-use asset recognized on the balance sheet under IFRS 16 / ASC 842, representing the present value of future lease payments plus initial direct costs.

attributes.name: security_deposit_amount
attributes.description: Refundable deposit held by the landlord to cover potential damages or unpaid rent. Typically equivalent to 1-3 months of base rent.

attributes.name: term_months
attributes.description: Total duration of the initial lease term expressed in months, excluding renewal options.

attributes.name: termination_clause_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the lease includes an early termination clause allowing the tenant to exit the lease before expiration under specified conditions.

attributes.name: termination_notice_days
attributes.description: Number of days advance notice required to exercise the early termination clause, if applicable.

attributes.name: termination_penalty_amount
attributes.description: Financial penalty or buyout amount required to exercise the early termination clause, if applicable.

products.name: landlord
products.description: Master record for every landlord, property owner, or lessor entity with whom the company maintains a real estate relationship. Captures landlord legal name, entity type (REIT, private owner, developer, municipality), primary contact details, billing address, tax identification, preferred communication channel, relationship tier, and portfolio overlap count. Tracks relationship health score, escalation history, and negotiation posture. Serves as the SSOT for landlord identity within the real estate domain.

attributes.name: landlord_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the landlord entity. Primary key.

attributes.name: billing_address_line1
attributes.description: First line of the landlord's billing address for rent payments and invoicing.

attributes.name: billing_address_line2
attributes.description: Second line of the billing address (suite, unit, building number).

attributes.name: billing_city
attributes.description: City for the landlord's billing address.

attributes.name: billing_country_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO country code for the landlord's billing address.

attributes.name: billing_postal_code
attributes.description: Postal or ZIP code for the landlord's billing address.

attributes.name: billing_state_province
attributes.description: State or province for the landlord's billing address.

attributes.name: cam_billing_method
attributes.description: Method by which CAM charges are calculated and billed by this landlord.
attributes.references: BOMA Standards, Internal Lease Administration Guidelines

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this landlord record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: dba_name
attributes.description: Trade name or DBA name under which the landlord operates, if different from legal name.

attributes.name: effective_date
attributes.description: Date when the landlord relationship became effective or when the landlord record was first established.
attributes.references: Internal Real Estate Master Data Policy

attributes.name: entity_type
attributes.description: Classification of the landlord's organizational structure. REIT = Real Estate Investment Trust.

attributes.name: escalation_history_count
attributes.description: Total number of formal escalations or disputes raised with this landlord over the relationship history.

attributes.name: insurance_certificate_frequency
attributes.description: Frequency at which insurance certificates must be submitted to this landlord.

attributes.name: insurance_certificate_required_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this landlord requires periodic submission of insurance certificates.

attributes.name: landlord_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the landlord relationship.
attributes.references: Internal Real Estate Master Data Policy

attributes.name: last_escalation_date
attributes.description: Date of the most recent formal escalation or dispute with this landlord.

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this landlord record was last updated.

attributes.name: legal_name
attributes.description: The full legal name of the landlord entity as registered with governing authorities.
attributes.references: IRS Publication 1179, State Business Registration Standards

attributes.name: negotiation_posture
attributes.description: Assessment of the landlord's typical approach to lease negotiations and dispute resolution.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-text field for capturing additional context, special requirements, or relationship notes about the landlord.

attributes.name: payment_terms_days
attributes.description: Standard number of days for rent payment terms agreed with this landlord (e.g., Net 30).

attributes.name: portfolio_property_count
attributes.description: Number of properties currently leased from this landlord across the restaurant portfolio.

attributes.name: preferred_communication_channel
attributes.description: The landlord's preferred method for receiving communications and notices.

attributes.name: primary_contact_email
attributes.description: Email address of the primary contact for landlord communications.

attributes.name: primary_contact_name
attributes.description: Full name of the primary contact person for landlord communications and relationship management.

attributes.name: primary_contact_phone
attributes.description: Primary telephone number for the landlord contact.

attributes.name: primary_contact_title
attributes.description: Job title or role of the primary contact person (e.g., Property Manager, Leasing Director, Asset Manager).

attributes.name: relationship_health_score
attributes.description: Quantitative score (0-100) representing the overall health of the landlord relationship based on payment history, dispute frequency, and collaboration quality.

attributes.name: relationship_tier
attributes.description: Classification of the landlord relationship based on portfolio size, negotiation history, and strategic importance.

attributes.name: status_reason
attributes.description: Explanation or reason code for the current status, particularly for inactive or suspended landlords.
attributes.references: Internal Real Estate Master Data Policy

attributes.name: tax_number
attributes.description: Federal Employer Identification Number (EIN) or Tax ID for the landlord entity.

attributes.name: termination_date
attributes.description: Date when the landlord relationship was terminated or when the last lease with this landlord expired.
attributes.references: Internal Real Estate Master Data Policy

attributes.name: w9_last_updated_date
attributes.description: Date when the W-9 form was last received or updated for this landlord.

attributes.name: w9_on_file_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether a current IRS Form W-9 is on file for this landlord for tax reporting purposes.

attributes.name: website_url
attributes.description: Official website URL for the landlord entity.

products.name: rent_schedule
products.description: Periodic rent obligation schedule derived from executed lease agreements, capturing each scheduled rent payment period, base rent amount, percentage rent threshold and rate, CAM (Common Area Maintenance) estimate, real estate tax contribution, insurance contribution, total occupancy cost, and payment due date. Supports IFRS 16 / ASC 842 lease liability amortization schedules, cash flow forecasting, and occupancy cost benchmarking against AUV (Average Unit Volume). One record per lease per billing period.

attributes.name: rent_schedule_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the rent schedule record. Primary key. Represents one billing period for one lease agreement.

attributes.name: agreement_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to franchise.agreement. Business justification: Percentage rent calculations on rent schedules are governed by franchise agreement sales thresholds and royalty terms. Linking rent_schedule to franchise agreement enables automated percentage rent va

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: FK to finance.cost_center

attributes.name: financial_period_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.financial_period. Business justification: Rent schedules must align to financial periods for period-close accruals, IFRS 16 amortization schedules, and occupancy cost reporting by fiscal period. Foodservice lease accountants require this for

attributes.name: gl_account_id
attributes.description: FK to finance.gl_account

attributes.name: landlord_id
attributes.description: FK to realestate.landlord

attributes.name: lease_id
attributes.description: Reference to the executed lease agreement that governs this rent schedule period. Links to the master lease agreement entity.

attributes.name: primary_rent_restaurant_unit_id
attributes.description: Reference to the restaurant location occupying the leased premises for this rent schedule period.

attributes.name: profit_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Rent schedules represent occupancy costs attributed to a profit center for restaurant-level P&L reporting. Foodservice operators track occupancy cost as a percentage of sales by profit center — a key

attributes.name: base_rent_amount
attributes.description: The fixed minimum rent amount due for this billing period as stipulated in the lease agreement, excluding percentage rent, CAM, taxes, and insurance.

attributes.name: billing_period_end_date
attributes.description: The last day of the rent billing period covered by this schedule record. Defines the period for which rent and occupancy costs are calculated.

attributes.name: billing_period_start_date
attributes.description: The first day of the rent billing period covered by this schedule record. Typically the first day of a calendar month or lease anniversary month.

attributes.name: cam_amount
attributes.description: The tenant's share of common area maintenance charges for this billing period, including property management, landscaping, parking lot maintenance, and shared facility upkeep. May be estimated or reconciled.

attributes.name: cam_reconciliation_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this billing period includes a CAM reconciliation adjustment, where estimated CAM charges are trued up to actual landlord expenses.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this rent schedule record was first created in the system. Audit trail for data lineage.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this rent schedule record (e.g., USD, CAD, EUR, GBP, MXN).

attributes.name: escalation_rate
attributes.description: The annual escalation rate applied to base rent for this period, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0300 for 3% annual increase). Null if escalation type is none or stepped.

attributes.name: escalation_type
attributes.description: The method by which base rent increases over the lease term. Fixed = predetermined amount, CPI = Consumer Price Index linked, Percentage = annual percentage increase, Stepped = scheduled step increases, None = flat rent.

attributes.name: insurance_amount
attributes.description: The tenant's share of property insurance premiums for this billing period, covering landlord's building and liability insurance. May be estimated and reconciled.

attributes.name: interest_expense
attributes.description: The interest expense component of the lease payment for this period under IFRS 16 / ASC 842 finance lease accounting. Calculated using the incremental borrowing rate.

attributes.name: lease_accounting_classification
attributes.description: The accounting treatment classification for this lease under IFRS 16 / ASC 842. Operating leases recognize rent expense; finance leases recognize depreciation and interest expense.

attributes.name: lease_liability_reduction
attributes.description: The portion of the total occupancy cost that reduces the lease liability on the balance sheet for this period under IFRS 16 / ASC 842. Represents principal payment component.

attributes.name: modified_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this rent schedule record was last updated. Audit trail for change tracking.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-text field for additional context, explanations, or special circumstances related to this rent schedule period (e.g., rent abatement, dispute details, payment plan arrangements).

attributes.name: occupancy_cost_percentage
attributes.description: The total occupancy cost as a percentage of restaurant sales for this period, calculated as (total_occupancy_cost / reported_sales_amount). Key performance metric for site profitability. Null if sales not reported.

attributes.name: payment_date
attributes.description: The actual date the rent payment was remitted to the landlord. Null if payment has not yet been made.

attributes.name: payment_due_date
attributes.description: The date by which the total rent and occupancy cost payment must be remitted to the landlord to avoid late fees or default.

attributes.name: payment_reference_number
attributes.description: The check number, wire transfer reference, or electronic payment confirmation number used to remit this rent payment. Null if payment has not yet been made.

attributes.name: payment_status
attributes.description: Current payment status for this rent schedule period. Tracks whether the scheduled payment has been made, is outstanding, or is under dispute.

attributes.name: percentage_rent_amount
attributes.description: The calculated percentage rent due for this billing period based on actual sales performance exceeding the threshold. Zero if sales did not exceed threshold or lease has no percentage rent clause.

attributes.name: percentage_rent_rate
attributes.description: The percentage of sales revenue above the threshold that is owed as additional rent. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0600 for 6%). Null if lease does not include percentage rent clause.

attributes.name: percentage_rent_threshold
attributes.description: The sales revenue breakpoint above which percentage rent becomes due. Also known as natural breakpoint or sales threshold. Null if lease does not include percentage rent clause.

attributes.name: property_address
attributes.description: The full street address of the leased restaurant property for this rent schedule. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.

attributes.name: real_estate_tax_amount
attributes.description: The tenant's proportionate share of property taxes for this billing period. May be estimated monthly and reconciled annually based on actual tax assessments.

attributes.name: rent_per_square_foot
attributes.description: The base rent amount divided by square footage for this billing period, expressed as cost per square foot. Key benchmarking metric for real estate portfolio management.

attributes.name: reported_sales_amount
attributes.description: The gross sales revenue reported to the landlord for this billing period, used to calculate percentage rent. Confidential business performance data. Null if sales reporting is not required.

attributes.name: right_of_use_asset_depreciation
attributes.description: The depreciation expense for the right-of-use asset for this period under IFRS 16 / ASC 842. Typically straight-line over the lease term.

attributes.name: sales_reporting_required_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the tenant is contractually required to report sales revenue to the landlord for this billing period, typically for percentage rent calculation or lease compliance.

attributes.name: square_footage
attributes.description: The total leasable square footage of the restaurant premises as defined in the lease agreement. Used to calculate per-square-foot rent metrics.

attributes.name: tax_reconciliation_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this billing period includes a real estate tax reconciliation adjustment, where estimated tax charges are trued up to actual property tax assessments.

attributes.name: total_occupancy_cost
attributes.description: The sum of all rent and occupancy charges for this billing period: base rent + percentage rent + CAM + real estate tax + insurance + other charges. Represents total cash outflow for occupancy.

products.name: rent_payment
products.description: Transactional record of each rent and occupancy cost payment made to a landlord, capturing payment date, payment method, amount paid (base rent, CAM, taxes, insurance), payment reference number, lease period covered, variance from scheduled amount, late fee applied flag, and reconciliation status. Supports accounts payable integration with SAP S/4HANA, landlord dispute resolution, and occupancy cost audit trails. Distinct from the rent schedule (obligation) — this captures actual cash disbursements.

attributes.name: rent_payment_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the rent payment transaction. Primary key.

attributes.name: bank_account_id
attributes.description: Reference to the company bank account from which this payment was disbursed.

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Reference to the cost center to which this rent payment is allocated for internal cost tracking and P&L reporting.

attributes.name: financial_period_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.financial_period. Business justification: Rent payments must be assigned to a financial period for period-close occupancy cost reporting and accruals. Foodservice operators close books monthly and require period-coded rent payments. The exist

attributes.name: gl_account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Rent payments must be posted to a GL account (occupancy expense) for financial reporting and AP/GL reconciliation. Foodservice finance controllers require GL coding on every payment for period-close p

attributes.name: journal_entry_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Under ASC 842/IFRS 16, each rent payment generates a journal entry for lease liability reduction and cash posting. Linking rent_payment to its journal_entry supports audit trails and lease accounting

attributes.name: landlord_id
attributes.description: Reference to the landlord or property owner receiving the payment.

attributes.name: lease_id
attributes.description: Reference to the lease agreement under which this payment is made.

attributes.name: primary_rent_approved_by_user_employee_id
attributes.description: Reference to the user or employee who approved this payment for disbursement.

attributes.name: primary_rent_restaurant_unit_id
attributes.description: Reference to the restaurant location for which this rent payment applies.

attributes.name: profit_center_id
attributes.description: Reference to the profit center to which this rent payment is allocated for segment reporting and profitability analysis.

attributes.name: rent_schedule_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to realestate.rent_schedule. Business justification: A rent payment fulfills a scheduled rent obligation. rent_payment already has scheduled_payment_amount and payment_variance_amount fields that only make sense in the context of a specific schedule lin

attributes.name: approval_date
attributes.description: The date on which this payment was approved for disbursement by the authorized approver.

attributes.name: base_rent_amount
attributes.description: The base rent amount paid for the lease period, excluding additional charges such as CAM, taxes, and insurance.

attributes.name: cam_amount
attributes.description: The Common Area Maintenance charge paid as part of this rent payment, covering shared facility costs such as landscaping, parking lot maintenance, and common utilities.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: The timestamp when this rent payment record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code indicating the currency in which this payment was made (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).

attributes.name: days_late
attributes.description: The number of days between the payment due date and the actual payment date, indicating payment timeliness.

attributes.name: dispute_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this payment is under dispute with the landlord due to billing discrepancies or lease interpretation issues.

attributes.name: dispute_reason
attributes.description: Description of the reason for dispute if the payment is flagged as disputed (e.g., incorrect CAM charges, billing error, lease term interpretation).

attributes.name: exchange_rate
attributes.description: The exchange rate applied if this payment was made in a foreign currency, used to convert to the company's reporting currency.

attributes.name: fiscal_period
attributes.description: The fiscal period (e.g., 2024-01, 2024-Q1) to which this payment is allocated for financial reporting.

attributes.name: gl_posting_date
attributes.description: The date on which this payment was posted to the general ledger for financial reporting purposes.

attributes.name: insurance_amount
attributes.description: The insurance premium amount paid as part of this rent payment, covering property and liability insurance as required by the lease.

attributes.name: late_fee_amount
attributes.description: The amount of late fee charged for this payment if payment was made after the due date.

attributes.name: late_fee_applied_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether a late fee was applied to this payment due to payment being made after the due date.

attributes.name: lease_period_end_date
attributes.description: The end date of the lease period covered by this rent payment.

attributes.name: lease_period_start_date
attributes.description: The start date of the lease period covered by this rent payment.

attributes.name: modified_timestamp
attributes.description: The timestamp when this rent payment record was last modified or updated.

attributes.name: payment_date
attributes.description: The date on which the rent payment was made or disbursed to the landlord.

attributes.name: payment_due_date
attributes.description: The date by which this rent payment was contractually due per the lease agreement.

attributes.name: payment_method
attributes.description: The payment instrument or method used to disburse the rent payment (e.g., ACH, wire transfer, check).

attributes.name: payment_notes
attributes.description: Free-text notes or comments related to this payment, capturing special circumstances, adjustments, or additional context.

attributes.name: payment_reference_number
attributes.description: External reference number or check number assigned to this payment for tracking and reconciliation purposes.

attributes.name: payment_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the payment transaction indicating whether it has been processed, cleared, or is pending.

attributes.name: payment_variance_amount
attributes.description: The difference between the scheduled payment amount and the actual total payment amount, indicating overpayment or underpayment.

attributes.name: property_tax_amount
attributes.description: The property tax amount paid as part of this rent payment, representing the tenant's share of real estate taxes.

attributes.name: reconciliation_date
attributes.description: The date on which this payment was reconciled and approved in the accounts payable system.

attributes.name: reconciliation_status
attributes.description: Indicates whether this payment has been reconciled against the lease schedule and landlord invoice, and whether any disputes exist.

attributes.name: scheduled_payment_amount
attributes.description: The originally scheduled or expected payment amount per the lease agreement for this period.

attributes.name: total_payment_amount
attributes.description: The total amount paid in this rent payment transaction, including base rent, CAM, taxes, insurance, and any other charges.

products.name: nro_project
products.description: Master record for each New Restaurant Opening (NRO) development project, tracking the full lifecycle from site approval through construction completion and restaurant opening. Captures project name, restaurant unit number, project type (new build, relocation, conversion), target and actual opening dates, project manager, general contractor, architect of record, total CapEx budget reference, and blocking issues. Includes detailed construction milestone tracking: each milestone (site plan approval, permit submission, permit issuance, groundbreaking, foundation complete, steel erection, MEP rough-in, drywall, equipment installation, punch list, final inspection, certificate of occupancy, opening day) with planned date, actual date, variance in days, responsible party, completion status, and blocking issues. Supports critical path management, CapEx draw schedule alignment, and integration with SAP S/4HANA CapEx modules and FranConnect NRO tracking.

attributes.name: nro_project_id
attributes.description: Unique system-generated identifier for the NRO development project.

attributes.name: budget_id

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: NRO projects incur pre-opening costs (training, marketing, setup) allocated to a cost center for capitalization decisions and pre-opening expense reporting. Foodservice operators track NRO pre-opening

attributes.name: facility_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to realestate.facility. Business justification: An NRO project results in the creation of a new facility (building and improvements). Linking nro_project.facility_id → facility.facility_id connects the development project to the physical facility i

attributes.name: lease_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to realestate.lease. Business justification: An NRO project results in the execution of a lease for the new restaurant location. nro_project has inline lease planning fields (lease_type, lease_term_years, ifr16_lease_start_date, lease_obligation

attributes.name: legal_entity_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: NRO projects are executed under a specific legal entity for tax, regulatory, and financial reporting purposes. Multi-entity foodservice operators must attribute project costs and capitalized assets to

attributes.name: nro_pipeline_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to franchise.nro_pipeline. Business justification: The real estate NRO project executes the franchise NRO pipeline opportunity. Linking nro_project to nro_pipeline enables integrated new-opening reporting, aligning construction/permitting milestones w

attributes.description: Identifier of the franchise entity that owns the new restaurant.

attributes.name: project_manager_employee_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: New Restaurant Opening projects are managed by a specific employee whose accountability is tracked for capex reporting, permitting timelines, and opening milestone reviews. The existing project_manage

attributes.name: restaurant_unit_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to restaurant.unit. Business justification: Once a new restaurant opens, the unit record must reference its originating NRO project for post-opening capex reconciliation, warranty tracking, and opening performance analysis (AUV vs. projected).

attributes.name: site_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to realestate.site. Business justification: A NRO project is always associated with a physical site; linking to site eliminates silo and removes duplicated address columns.

attributes.name: actual_opening_date
attributes.description: Actual date the restaurant opened for service.

attributes.name: capex_budget_amount

attributes.name: capex_committed_amount

attributes.name: certificate_of_occupancy_date
attributes.description: Date the Certificate of Occupancy was issued, allowing the restaurant to operate.

attributes.name: compliance_status
attributes.description: Current compliance status with local health, safety, and regulatory requirements.

attributes.name: ifr16_lease_asset_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the lease is accounted for as a right‑of‑use asset under IFRS 16.

attributes.name: ifr16_lease_end_date

attributes.name: ifr16_lease_start_date

attributes.name: lease_obligation_end_date

attributes.name: lease_obligation_start_date
attributes.description: Start date of the lease obligation for the site (IFRS 16 / ASC 842).

attributes.name: lease_term_years

attributes.name: lease_type
attributes.description: Classification of the lease (e.g., gross, net, percentage rent).

attributes.name: nro_project_description
attributes.description: Free‑form description of the project's scope and objectives.

attributes.name: nro_project_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the NRO project.

attributes.name: permit_expiry_date
attributes.description: Expiration date of the permit, if applicable.

attributes.name: permit_issue_date
attributes.description: Date the permit was issued.

attributes.name: permit_number

attributes.name: permitting_approval_date
attributes.description: Date when all required permits were approved.

attributes.name: permitting_status

attributes.name: project_code
attributes.description: External business code or identifier used for the project in franchise and CapEx systems.

attributes.description: Human‑readable name of the development project.

products.name: capex_budget
products.description: CapEx (Capital Expenditure) planning, budgeting, and spend tracking record for each real estate project (NRO, remodel, relocation, or major facility upgrade). Budget envelope: total approved budget, budget by cost category (land, building shell, leasehold improvements, FF&E, signage, technology, soft costs), budget version, approval date, approving authority, funding source. Actual spend detail: each invoice-level expenditure event with vendor, cost category, amount, payment date, PO reference, SAP cost element, budget line item, cumulative spend to date, and variance to budget. Enables real-time CapEx tracking, budget burn rate monitoring, cost-to-complete forecasting, and draw schedule management. Integrates with SAP S/4HANA CO (Controlling) and Coupa Procurement for PO-to-invoice matching. Serves as the single source of truth for both authorized budgets and actual capital expenditures.

attributes.name: capex_budget_id
attributes.description: Unique surrogate key for each CapEx budget record.

attributes.name: approving_employee_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Capex budgets for restaurant construction and remodels require formal employee approval (VP Development, CFO) for audit trails, SOX compliance, and capital authorization workflows. The existing approv

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: FK to finance.cost_center

attributes.name: financial_period_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.financial_period. Business justification: Capex budgets are approved and tracked within fiscal periods for period-close reporting and capital expenditure planning cycles. Foodservice operators budget capex by fiscal year/period for board appr

attributes.name: gl_account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Capital budgets are tracked against specific GL accounts (capex GL codes) for financial reporting and budget vs. actual analysis. Foodservice finance teams require GL account coding on capex budgets f

attributes.description: Foreign key linking to realestate.nro_project. Business justification: A CapEx budget is created to fund a specific NRO (New Restaurant Opening) development project. nro_project has inline capex_budget_amount and capex_actual_amount fields, but the authoritative budget p

attributes.name: profit_center_id

attributes.name: site_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to realestate.site. Business justification: CapEx budgets are created for specific physical sites (new builds, remodels, or facility upgrades). Linking capex_budget.site_id → site.site_id anchors each budget to its location, enabling site-level

attributes.name: amendment_date
attributes.description: Date the latest amendment was recorded.

attributes.name: amendment_number

attributes.name: amendment_reason
attributes.description: Reason or justification for the most recent budget amendment.

attributes.name: approval_date

attributes.name: budget_code

attributes.name: budget_name

attributes.description: Planned end date of the budget period.

attributes.name: ffe_cost
attributes.description: Budget for furniture, fixtures, and equipment.

attributes.name: funding_source
attributes.description: Source of capital funding (e.g., corporate cash, debt, lease financing).

attributes.name: land_cost
attributes.description: Approved budget amount allocated for land acquisition.

attributes.name: leasehold_improvements_cost
attributes.description: Budget for interior build‑out and leasehold improvements.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Additional remarks, comments, or special conditions.

attributes.name: signage_cost
attributes.description: Budget for exterior and interior signage.

attributes.name: soft_costs
attributes.description: Budget for professional services, permits, design, and other indirect costs.

attributes.name: start_date
attributes.description: Planned start date of the budget period.

attributes.name: technology_cost
attributes.description: Budget for POS, kitchen display, networking, and other technology.

attributes.name: total_budget_amount
attributes.description: Aggregate approved budget amount across all cost categories.

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to the budget record.

products.name: facility
products.description: Master record for the physical facility (building and improvements) associated with each restaurant location, capturing building square footage, seating capacity (FOH covers), drive-thru lane configuration (DT lanes, stacking capacity), kitchen layout type (BOH configuration), building ownership (owned/leased shell), year built, last remodel date, remodel type, ADA compliance status, energy rating, utility account references, and facility condition score. Distinct from the site (land/location) — this represents the built structure. Links to the restaurant unit in the restaurant domain.

attributes.name: facility_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the facility record.

attributes.name: brand_standard_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to restaurant.brand_standard. Business justification: Each facility must comply with a specific brand standard version governing signage, layout, equipment specs, and ADA requirements. Foodservice brand compliance audits require knowing which brand stand

attributes.name: lease_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to realestate.lease. Business justification: A facility is governed by a lease agreement. facility currently stores lease_start_date, lease_end_date, and lease_rate inline — these are denormalized copies of data that lives authoritatively in the

attributes.name: site_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to realestate.site. Business justification: Facility belongs to a Site; linking provides parent relationship and removes duplicated address fields.

attributes.name: ada_compliance_status
attributes.description: Indicates compliance with the Americans with Disabilities Act.

attributes.name: building_material
attributes.description: Primary material used in the construction of the facility (e.g., brick, steel).

attributes.name: cam_charges
attributes.description: Common Area Maintenance charges associated with the facility.

attributes.name: capex_spent
attributes.description: Capital expenditures incurred to date for the facility.

attributes.name: condition_score
attributes.description: Overall condition rating of the facility (e.g., 0-100 scale).

attributes.name: construction_status
attributes.description: Status of any ongoing construction projects.

attributes.name: energy_rating
attributes.description: Energy efficiency rating of the facility.

attributes.name: facility_code
attributes.description: External business identifier or code for the facility used in operational systems.

attributes.name: facility_name
attributes.description: Descriptive name of the facility (e.g., restaurant location name).

attributes.name: facility_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the facility.

attributes.name: facility_type
attributes.description: Category of the facility based on its operational model.

attributes.name: fire_safety_compliance_status
attributes.description: Indicates compliance with fire safety regulations.

attributes.name: health_inspection_score
attributes.description: Score from the latest health inspection.

attributes.name: hvac_type
attributes.description: Heating, ventilation, and air conditioning system type.

attributes.name: insurance_expiry_date
attributes.description: Expiration date of the facility's insurance policy.

attributes.name: insurance_policy_number
attributes.description: Policy number for the facility's insurance coverage.

attributes.name: last_inspection_date
attributes.description: Date of the most recent health or safety inspection.

attributes.name: maintenance_last_date
attributes.description: Date when the most recent maintenance activity was performed.

attributes.name: maintenance_next_due
attributes.description: Scheduled date for the next maintenance activity.

attributes.name: ownership_type
attributes.description: Indicates whether the facility is owned by the company or leased.

attributes.name: property_tax_rate
attributes.description: Applicable property tax rate (percentage).

attributes.name: r_and_m_status
attributes.description: Current status of repairs and maintenance activities.

attributes.name: remodel_date
attributes.description: Date of the most recent remodel or renovation.

attributes.name: remodel_type
attributes.description: Scope of the most recent remodel.

attributes.name: roof_type
attributes.description: Type of roof installed on the facility (e.g., flat, pitched).

attributes.name: seating_capacity
attributes.description: Number of guest seats (covers) available in the front of house.

attributes.name: square_footage
attributes.description: Total building area in square feet.

attributes.name: tax_assessment_value
attributes.description: Assessed property tax value of the facility.

attributes.name: waste_percentage
attributes.description: Percentage of food waste relative to total food usage.

attributes.name: year_built
attributes.description: Calendar year the facility was originally constructed.

attributes.name: yield_percentage
attributes.description: Percentage of usable product yield from raw inputs.

attributes.name: zoning_type
attributes.description: Zoning classification of the property (e.g., commercial, mixed-use).

products.name: maintenance_work_order
products.description: Transactional record for each R&M (Repairs and Maintenance) work order issued for a restaurant facility, capturing work order number, facility, issue category (HVAC, plumbing, electrical, equipment, structural, exterior, signage), issue description, priority level, reported date, assigned vendor/technician, scheduled date, completion date, labor hours, parts cost, total cost, warranty claim flag, and resolution notes. Supports OpEx R&M spend tracking, vendor performance management, and preventive maintenance compliance. Integrates with facility management workflows.
products.reference: Work Order / R&M Work Order / Facility Maintenance Request

attributes.name: maintenance_work_order_id
attributes.description: System-generated unique identifier for the maintenance work order record.

attributes.name: complaint_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to guest.complaint. Business justification: Guest facility complaints directly trigger maintenance work orders in real restaurant operations. Linking the work order back to the originating guest complaint enables complaint-to-resolution trackin

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Maintenance costs must be allocated to a cost center for R&M expense reporting and P&L management. Foodservice facilities managers require direct cost center coding on work orders for expense allocati

attributes.name: facility_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to realestate.facility. Business justification: Maintenance work orders are performed at specific facilities. While maintenance_work_order already links to maintenance_contract (which links to facility), a direct facility_id FK is essential for wor

attributes.name: franchisee_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to franchise.franchisee. Business justification: Maintenance Approval workflow requires the franchisee to approve work orders for their locations, linking orders to the responsible franchisee.

attributes.name: gl_account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Maintenance expenses must be coded to a GL account (R&M expense accounts) for financial posting and AP invoice matching. Foodservice operators require GL coding on work orders for automated expense po

attributes.name: maintenance_contract_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to realestate.maintenance_contract. Business justification: Work orders are issued under a Maintenance Contract; linking captures this relationship.

attributes.name: primary_maintenance_employee_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the internal or contracted technician who performed the work.

attributes.name: primary_maintenance_restaurant_unit_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the restaurant facility where the work order originated.

attributes.name: completion_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the work order was completed.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the work order record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for cost amounts.

attributes.name: issue_category
attributes.description: Category of the maintenance issue (e.g., HVAC, plumbing, electrical, equipment, structural, exterior, signage, other). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hvac|plumbing|electrical|equipment|structural|exterior|signage|other — promote to reference product]

attributes.name: issue_description
attributes.description: Detailed textual description of the maintenance issue reported.

attributes.name: labor_cost
attributes.description: Monetary cost of labor associated with the work order.

attributes.name: labor_hours
attributes.description: Total labor hours expended to resolve the work order.

attributes.name: maintenance_work_order_status
