  comment: "Offer distribution and engagement funnel metrics. Tracks offer delivery, open, click, and redemption rates from assignment through conversion — KPIs for measuring offer campaign effectiveness and personalization ROI."
  source: "`restaurants_ecm`.`loyalty`.`offer_assignment`"
  dimensions:
    - name: "assignment_channel"
      expr: assignment_channel
      comment: "Channel through which the offer was assigned to the member (e.g. App, Email, Push, SMS). Used to attribute offer engagement metrics to distribution channels."
    - name: "assignment_type"
      expr: assignment_type
      comment: "Type of offer assignment (e.g. Targeted, Broadcast, Triggered). Used to compare engagement rates across assignment strategies."
    - name: "delivery_status"
      expr: delivery_status
      comment: "Status of the offer delivery to the member (e.g. Delivered, Failed, Pending). Used to monitor delivery success rates and identify channel delivery failures."
    - name: "redemption_status"
      expr: redemption_status
      comment: "Current redemption status of the assigned offer (e.g. Redeemed, Unredeemed, Expired). Used to measure conversion from assignment to redemption."
    - name: "ab_test_variant"
      expr: ab_test_variant
      comment: "A/B test variant assigned to this offer assignment. Used to compare engagement and redemption rates across test variants to optimize offer design."
    - name: "assignment_timestamp_month"
      expr: DATE_TRUNC('MONTH', assignment_timestamp)
      comment: "Month of offer assignment, used for trend analysis and monthly campaign performance reporting."
    - name: "is_wallet_visible"
      expr: is_wallet_visible
      comment: "Whether the offer is visible in the member's digital wallet. Used to analyze the impact of wallet visibility on redemption rates."
  measures:
    - name: "total_offer_assignments"
      expr: COUNT(DISTINCT offer_assignment_id)
      comment: "Total number of offer assignments issued to members. The primary distribution volume KPI — used to track offer campaign reach against targeting goals."
    - name: "delivered_assignment_count"
      expr: COUNT(CASE WHEN delivery_status = 'Delivered' THEN offer_assignment_id END)
      comment: "Count of offer assignments successfully delivered to members. Measures the effective reach of the offer campaign after accounting for delivery failures."
    - name: "delivery_success_rate"
      expr: ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(CASE WHEN delivery_status = 'Delivered' THEN offer_assignment_id END) / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT offer_assignment_id), 0), 2)
      comment: "Percentage of offer assignments that were successfully delivered. A low rate signals channel delivery failures (e.g. push notification opt-out, email bounces) requiring channel health investigation."
    - name: "offer_open_count"
      expr: COUNT(CASE WHEN opened_timestamp IS NOT NULL THEN offer_assignment_id END)
      comment: "Count of offer assignments that were opened by the member. Measures top-of-funnel engagement with distributed offers."
    - name: "offer_open_rate"
      expr: ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(CASE WHEN opened_timestamp IS NOT NULL THEN offer_assignment_id END) / NULLIF(COUNT(CASE WHEN delivery_status = 'Delivered' THEN offer_assignment_id END), 0), 2)
      comment: "Percentage of delivered offers that were opened by the member. A key engagement funnel metric — low open rates signal poor offer relevance or notification fatigue."
    - name: "offer_click_count"
      expr: COUNT(CASE WHEN clicked_timestamp IS NOT NULL THEN offer_assignment_id END)
      comment: "Count of offer assignments where the member clicked through to the offer detail. Measures mid-funnel engagement and intent to redeem."
    - name: "offer_click_through_rate"
      expr: ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(CASE WHEN clicked_timestamp IS NOT NULL THEN offer_assignment_id END) / NULLIF(COUNT(CASE WHEN opened_timestamp IS NOT NULL THEN offer_assignment_id END), 0), 2)
      comment: "Percentage of opened offers that were clicked through. Measures the quality of offer creative and relevance in driving member intent — a key personalization effectiveness KPI."
    - name: "offer_redemption_conversion_rate"
      expr: ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(CASE WHEN redemption_status = 'Redeemed' THEN offer_assignment_id END) / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT offer_assignment_id), 0), 2)
      comment: "Percentage of assigned offers that were ultimately redeemed. The end-to-end offer conversion rate — the primary ROI KPI for offer campaigns, directly informing future campaign investment decisions."
    - name: "avg_personalization_score"
      expr: AVG(CAST(personalization_score AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Average personalization relevance score assigned to offer-member pairings. Tracks the quality of the personalization engine — higher scores should correlate with higher redemption rates."
    - name: "unique_members_assigned"
      expr: COUNT(DISTINCT loyalty_member_id)
      comment: "Count of distinct members who received at least one offer assignment in the period. Measures the breadth of offer campaign reach across the member base."
$$
metric_views.description: Offer distribution and engagement funnel metrics. Tracks offer delivery, open, click, and redemption rates from assignment through conversion — KPIs for measuring offer campaign effectiveness and personalization ROI.

metric_views.sql:
CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW `restaurants_ecm`.`_metrics`.`loyalty_points_ledger`
WITH METRICS
LANGUAGE YAML
AS $$
  version: 1.1
  comment: "Points economics and ledger activity metrics. Tracks points earn rates, transaction volumes, voided activity, and order value associated with points transactions — the financial engine of the loyalty program."
  source: "`restaurants_ecm`.`loyalty`.`points_ledger`"
  dimensions:
    - name: "transaction_type"
      expr: transaction_type
      comment: "Type of points ledger transaction (e.g. Earn, Redeem, Adjust, Expire, Reverse). Used to decompose points movement by type for financial reconciliation."
    - name: "transaction_timestamp_month"
      expr: DATE_TRUNC('MONTH', transaction_timestamp)
      comment: "Month of the points transaction, used for trend analysis and monthly points economics reporting."
    - name: "fiscal_year"
      expr: fiscal_year
      comment: "Fiscal year of the points transaction. Used for annual financial reporting and loyalty program P&L attribution."
    - name: "fiscal_period"
      expr: fiscal_period
      comment: "Fiscal period of the points transaction. Used for period-over-period financial reporting of points liability and earn activity."
    - name: "source_system_code"
      expr: source_system_code
      comment: "Source system that originated the points transaction (e.g. POS, App, OLO). Used to audit data quality and reconcile points across systems."
    - name: "order_currency_code"
      expr: order_currency_code
      comment: "Currency of the originating order. Used for multi-currency points economics analysis in international markets."
    - name: "adjustment_reason_code"
      expr: adjustment_reason_code
      comment: "Reason code for manual points adjustments. Used to categorize and monitor the volume and value of manual interventions in the points ledger."
    - name: "is_voided"
      expr: is_voided
      comment: "Whether the points transaction has been voided. Used to filter clean vs. voided transactions and monitor void rates."
    - name: "points_expiry_date_month"
      expr: DATE_TRUNC('MONTH', points_expiry_date)
      comment: "Month in which the points in this ledger entry are scheduled to expire. Used for forward-looking liability expiration forecasting."
  measures:
    - name: "total_points_transactions"
      expr: COUNT(DISTINCT points_ledger_id)
      comment: "Total number of points ledger transactions. Measures the overall activity volume of the loyalty program and is used to track engagement throughput."
    - name: "total_order_amount"
      expr: SUM(CAST(order_total_amount AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Total order value associated with points-earning transactions. Measures the revenue base that the loyalty program is driving — a direct linkage between loyalty engagement and top-line sales."
    - name: "avg_order_amount_per_transaction"
      expr: AVG(CAST(order_total_amount AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Average order value per points-earning transaction. Tracks whether loyalty members are spending more per visit over time — a key measure of program-driven basket size lift."
    - name: "avg_points_earn_rate"
      expr: AVG(CAST(points_earn_rate AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Average points earn rate applied across transactions. Used to monitor the effective generosity of the program and detect anomalies from rule misconfiguration."
    - name: "voided_transaction_count"
      expr: COUNT(CASE WHEN is_voided = TRUE THEN points_ledger_id END)
      comment: "Count of voided points ledger transactions. A high void rate signals operational issues at POS, fraud, or system integration failures requiring investigation."
    - name: "void_rate"
      expr: ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(CASE WHEN is_voided = TRUE THEN points_ledger_id END) / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT points_ledger_id), 0), 2)
      comment: "Percentage of points transactions that were voided. A rising void rate is an operational and fraud risk signal that triggers investigation and system audits."
    - name: "total_order_amount_on_earn_transactions"
      expr: SUM(CASE WHEN transaction_type = 'Earn' THEN CAST(order_total_amount AS DOUBLE) ELSE 0 END)
      comment: "Total order value on earn-type transactions only. Isolates the revenue base directly attributable to points-earning behavior, used for loyalty-driven revenue attribution."
    - name: "unique_members_with_transactions"
      expr: COUNT(DISTINCT loyalty_member_id)
      comment: "Count of distinct members with at least one points ledger entry in the period. Measures the active transacting member base — a key denominator for per-member economics."
$$
metric_views.description: Points economics and ledger activity metrics. Tracks points earn rates, transaction volumes, voided activity, and order value associated with points transactions — the financial engine of the loyalty program.

metric_views.sql:
CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW `restaurants_ecm`.`_metrics`.`loyalty_offer_redemption`
WITH METRICS
LANGUAGE YAML
AS $$
  version: 1.1
  comment: "Offer redemption performance and discount economics metrics. Tracks redemption volume, discount value delivered, order lift, and fraud — KPIs that directly inform offer ROI, promotional spend efficiency, and member engagement quality."
  source: "`restaurants_ecm`.`loyalty`.`redemption`"
  dimensions:
    - name: "redemption_status"
      expr: redemption_status
      comment: "Status of the offer redemption (e.g. Completed, Voided, Pending). Used to filter valid redemptions and monitor void/failure rates."
    - name: "member_tier"
      expr: member_tier
      comment: "Loyalty tier of the member at the time of redemption. Used to analyze offer redemption behavior and discount economics by tier segment."
    - name: "daypart"
      expr: daypart
      comment: "Daypart during which the redemption occurred (e.g. Breakfast, Lunch, Dinner). Used to identify peak redemption windows and optimize offer timing."
    - name: "redemption_timestamp_month"
      expr: DATE_TRUNC('MONTH', redemption_timestamp)
      comment: "Month of the offer redemption, used for trend analysis and monthly promotional spend reporting."
    - name: "currency_code"
      expr: currency_code
      comment: "Currency of the redemption transaction. Used for multi-currency promotional spend analysis in international markets."
  measures:
    - name: "total_discount_amount"
      expr: SUM(CAST(discount_amount AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Total monetary discount value delivered through offer redemptions. Represents the direct promotional spend — a critical P&L line item for marketing budget management."
    - name: "avg_discount_amount_per_redemption"
      expr: AVG(CAST(discount_amount AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Average discount value per redemption event. Used to benchmark offer generosity and detect outlier offers that are over-discounting relative to program targets."
    - name: "unique_members_redeeming"
      expr: COUNT(DISTINCT member_id)
      comment: "Count of distinct members who redeemed at least one offer in the period. Measures the breadth of offer engagement across the member base — a key indicator of program activation."
$$
metric_views.description: Offer redemption performance and discount economics metrics. Tracks redemption volume, discount value delivered, order lift, and fraud — KPIs that directly inform offer ROI, promotional spend efficiency, and member engagement quality.

metric_views.sql:
CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW `restaurants_ecm`.`_metrics`.`loyalty_redemption`
WITH METRICS
LANGUAGE YAML
AS $$
  version: 1.1
  comment: "Points redemption economics and reward utilization metrics. Tracks points depletion, discount value delivered, fraud exposure, and order impact from reward redemptions — KPIs for managing loyalty liability and member value delivery."
  source: "`restaurants_ecm`.`loyalty`.`redemption`"
  dimensions:
    - name: "redemption_status"
      expr: redemption_status
      comment: "Status of the redemption (e.g. Completed, Reversed, Voided). Used to isolate valid redemptions and monitor reversal and void rates."
    - name: "reward_type"
      expr: reward_type
      comment: "Type of reward redeemed (e.g. Free Item, Discount, Experience). Used to analyze redemption mix and reward portfolio performance."
    - name: "member_tier"
      expr: member_tier
      comment: "Loyalty tier of the member at the time of redemption. Used to analyze redemption behavior and discount economics by tier segment."
    - name: "daypart"
      expr: daypart
      comment: "Daypart during which the redemption occurred. Used to identify peak redemption windows and optimize reward availability."
    - name: "redemption_source"
      expr: redemption_source
      comment: "Source system or channel that originated the redemption (e.g. App, POS, OLO). Used to attribute redemption volume to channels and audit cross-channel consistency."
    - name: "redemption_timestamp_month"
      expr: DATE_TRUNC('MONTH', redemption_timestamp)
      comment: "Month of the redemption event, used for trend analysis and monthly liability drawdown reporting."
    - name: "currency_code"
      expr: currency_code
      comment: "Currency of the redemption transaction. Used for multi-currency redemption economics analysis."
    - name: "fraud_flag"
      expr: fraud_flag
      comment: "Whether the redemption was flagged as potentially fraudulent. Used to monitor fraud exposure in the redemption pipeline."
    - name: "third_party_delivery_partner"
      expr: third_party_delivery_partner
      comment: "Third-party delivery partner associated with the redemption (e.g. DoorDash, Uber Eats). Used to analyze redemption behavior and discount economics on third-party channels."
  measures:
    - name: "total_redemptions"
      expr: COUNT(DISTINCT redemption_id)
      comment: "Total number of reward redemptions. The primary volume KPI for reward utilization — used to track redemption against program targets and measure member engagement depth."
    - name: "total_discount_amount"
      expr: SUM(CAST(discount_amount AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Total monetary discount value delivered through reward redemptions. Represents the direct cost of rewards redeemed — a critical loyalty program P&L line item."
    - name: "avg_discount_per_redemption"
      expr: AVG(CAST(discount_amount AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Average discount value per redemption. Used to benchmark reward generosity and detect outlier reward types that are over-delivering discount relative to program economics."
    - name: "total_order_value_before_discount"
      expr: SUM(CAST(order_total_before_discount AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Total pre-discount order value on redemption transactions. Measures the revenue base that reward redemptions are attached to — used for loyalty-driven revenue attribution."
    - name: "total_order_value_after_discount"
      expr: SUM(CAST(order_total_after_discount AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Total post-discount order value on redemption transactions. Measures net revenue realized after reward discounts are applied."
    - name: "discount_to_order_ratio"
      expr: ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CAST(discount_amount AS DOUBLE)) / NULLIF(SUM(CAST(order_total_before_discount AS DOUBLE)), 0), 2)
      comment: "Total discount amount as a percentage of total pre-discount order value. Measures the effective discount depth of the rewards program — a key margin management KPI."
    - name: "avg_fraud_score"
      expr: AVG(CAST(fraud_score AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Average fraud risk score across redemption events. Tracks the overall fraud risk profile of the redemption pipeline — a rising average signals increased fraud pressure requiring security intervention."
    - name: "fraudulent_redemption_count"
      expr: COUNT(CASE WHEN fraud_flag = TRUE THEN redemption_id END)
      comment: "Count of redemptions flagged as fraudulent. Measures the absolute fraud exposure in the redemption pipeline — directly informs fraud prevention investment and policy decisions."
    - name: "fraud_redemption_rate"
      expr: ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(CASE WHEN fraud_flag = TRUE THEN redemption_id END) / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT redemption_id), 0), 2)
      comment: "Percentage of redemptions flagged as fraudulent. A rising rate is a critical risk signal that triggers security audits, rule tightening, and potential program suspension decisions."
    - name: "unique_members_redeeming"
      expr: COUNT(DISTINCT member_id)
      comment: "Count of distinct members who completed at least one reward redemption in the period. Measures the breadth of reward utilization across the member base."
$$
metric_views.description: Points redemption economics and reward utilization metrics. Tracks points depletion, discount value delivered, fraud exposure, and order impact from reward redemptions — KPIs for managing loyalty liability and member value delivery.

metric_views.sql:
CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW `restaurants_ecm`.`_metrics`.`loyalty_tier_history`
WITH METRICS
LANGUAGE YAML
AS $$
  version: 1.1
  comment: "Tier movement and qualification metrics. Tracks upgrade and downgrade velocity, manual override rates, qualification economics, and notification effectiveness — KPIs for managing tier program health and member progression."
  source: "`restaurants_ecm`.`loyalty`.`tier_history`"
  dimensions:
    - name: "tier_change_type"
      expr: tier_change_type
      comment: "Type of tier change event (e.g. Upgrade, Downgrade, Renewal, Expiry). Used to decompose tier movement volume by direction and type."
    - name: "tier_change_reason_code"
      expr: tier_change_reason_code
      comment: "Reason code for the tier change. Used to categorize the drivers of tier movement (e.g. Qualification, Promotion, Manual Override, Expiry)."
    - name: "previous_tier_code"
      expr: previous_tier_code
      comment: "Code of the tier the member was in before the change. Used to analyze tier transition flows and identify which tiers have the highest churn or upgrade rates."
    - name: "processing_channel"
      expr: processing_channel
      comment: "Channel through which the tier change was processed (e.g. Batch, Real-Time, Manual). Used to monitor processing channel mix and identify latency issues."
    - name: "notification_channel"
      expr: notification_channel
      comment: "Channel used to notify the member of their tier change (e.g. Email, Push, SMS). Used to analyze notification delivery effectiveness by channel."
    - name: "tier_change_timestamp_month"
      expr: DATE_TRUNC('MONTH', tier_change_timestamp)
      comment: "Month of the tier change event, used for trend analysis and monthly tier movement reporting."
    - name: "is_manual_override"
      expr: is_manual_override
      comment: "Whether the tier change was a manual override by an employee. Used to monitor the rate of manual interventions in the tier qualification process."
    - name: "is_promotional_tier"
      expr: is_promotional_tier
      comment: "Whether the tier change was driven by a promotional campaign rather than standard qualification. Used to separate organic tier progression from promotional tier grants."
  measures:
    - name: "total_tier_changes"
      expr: COUNT(DISTINCT tier_history_id)
      comment: "Total number of tier change events. Measures the overall velocity of tier movement in the program — high velocity may indicate tier threshold miscalibration or aggressive promotional tier grants."
    - name: "tier_upgrade_count"
      expr: COUNT(CASE WHEN tier_change_type = 'Upgrade' THEN tier_history_id END)
      comment: "Count of tier upgrade events. Measures the rate at which members are progressing to higher tiers — a positive engagement signal and indicator of program aspiration effectiveness."
    - name: "tier_downgrade_count"
      expr: COUNT(CASE WHEN tier_change_type = 'Downgrade' THEN tier_history_id END)
      comment: "Count of tier downgrade events. A rising downgrade count signals member disengagement or overly aggressive qualification thresholds — triggers program design review."
    - name: "upgrade_to_downgrade_ratio"
      expr: ROUND(CAST(COUNT(CASE WHEN tier_change_type = 'Upgrade' THEN tier_history_id END) AS DOUBLE) / NULLIF(COUNT(CASE WHEN tier_change_type = 'Downgrade' THEN tier_history_id END), 0), 2)
      comment: "Ratio of tier upgrades to tier downgrades. A ratio above 1 indicates net positive tier progression across the member base — a key program health indicator for loyalty leadership."
    - name: "manual_override_count"
      expr: COUNT(CASE WHEN is_manual_override = TRUE THEN tier_history_id END)
      comment: "Count of tier changes executed as manual overrides. A high count signals operational inefficiency or policy exceptions that may indicate program rule gaps requiring governance review."
    - name: "manual_override_rate"
      expr: ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(CASE WHEN is_manual_override = TRUE THEN tier_history_id END) / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT tier_history_id), 0), 2)
      comment: "Percentage of tier changes that were manual overrides. A rising rate is a governance and operational risk signal — manual overrides bypass automated qualification rules and create inconsistent member experiences."
    - name: "notification_sent_rate"
      expr: ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(CASE WHEN notification_sent_flag = TRUE THEN tier_history_id END) / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT tier_history_id), 0), 2)
      comment: "Percentage of tier changes where a notification was successfully sent to the member. A low rate means members are not being informed of their tier changes — a key driver of tier benefit awareness and engagement."
    - name: "avg_qualifying_points_balance"
      expr: AVG(CAST(qualifying_points_balance AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Average qualifying points balance at the time of tier change. Used to benchmark the actual qualification level of members changing tiers against the program's stated thresholds."
    - name: "avg_qualifying_spend_amount"
      expr: AVG(CAST(qualifying_spend_amount AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Average qualifying spend amount at the time of tier change. Measures the average revenue contribution of members at the point of tier qualification — used to calibrate spend-based tier thresholds."
    - name: "total_qualifying_spend_amount"
      expr: SUM(CAST(qualifying_spend_amount AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Total qualifying spend across all tier change events. Measures the aggregate revenue contribution associated with tier qualification activity — links tier program investment to revenue outcomes."
    - name: "unique_members_with_tier_changes"
      expr: COUNT(DISTINCT loyalty_member_id)
      comment: "Count of distinct members who experienced a tier change in the period. Measures the breadth of tier movement activity across the member base."
$$
metric_views.description: Tier movement and qualification metrics. Tracks upgrade and downgrade velocity, manual override rates, qualification economics, and notification effectiveness — KPIs for managing tier program health and member progression.

metric_views.sql:
CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW `restaurants_ecm`.`_metrics`.`marketing_campaign`
WITH METRICS
LANGUAGE YAML
AS $$
  version: 1.1
  comment: "Strategic KPI layer over the campaign master table. Tracks campaign investment, performance lift, and budget efficiency to inform marketing spend allocation and campaign effectiveness decisions."
  source: "`restaurants_ecm`.`marketing`.`campaign`"
  filter: is_test_campaign = False
  dimensions:
    - name: "campaign_type"
      expr: campaign_type
      comment: "Type of campaign (e.g., national, local, digital, LTO) used to segment performance by campaign category."
    - name: "campaign_status"
      expr: campaign_status
      comment: "Current lifecycle status of the campaign (e.g., active, completed, cancelled) for filtering and trend analysis."
    - name: "channel_mix"
      expr: channel_mix
      comment: "Media channel mix used in the campaign (e.g., TV+Digital, Radio+OOH) to evaluate channel strategy effectiveness."
    - name: "objective"
      expr: objective
      comment: "Primary business objective of the campaign (e.g., traffic, awareness, trial) for goal-based performance grouping."
    - name: "target_daypart"
      expr: target_daypart
      comment: "Daypart targeted by the campaign (e.g., breakfast, lunch, dinner) to assess daypart-level marketing ROI."
    - name: "target_market"
      expr: target_market
      comment: "Geographic market targeted by the campaign for regional performance benchmarking."
    - name: "is_lto"
      expr: is_lto
      comment: "Indicates whether the campaign supports a Limited Time Offer, enabling LTO vs. evergreen campaign comparison."
    - name: "compliance_flag"
      expr: compliance_flag
      comment: "Indicates whether the campaign is compliant with brand and regulatory standards, used for compliance reporting."
    - name: "planned_start_month"
      expr: DATE_TRUNC('MONTH', planned_start_date)
      comment: "Month the campaign was planned to start, used for time-series trending of campaign launches."
    - name: "actual_start_month"
      expr: DATE_TRUNC('MONTH', actual_start_date)
      comment: "Month the campaign actually started, used to measure launch timing accuracy vs. plan."
  measures:
    - name: "total_actual_spend"
      expr: SUM(CAST(actual_spend AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Total actual marketing spend across campaigns. Core investment metric for budget stewardship and ROI analysis."
    - name: "total_budget_amount"
      expr: SUM(CAST(budget_amount AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Total planned budget allocated across campaigns. Used to assess budget utilization and over/under-spend."
    - name: "avg_actual_spend_per_campaign"
      expr: AVG(CAST(actual_spend AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Average actual spend per campaign. Benchmarks campaign investment levels and identifies outliers."
    - name: "avg_actual_adt_lift_pct"
      expr: AVG(CAST(actual_adt_lift_pct AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Average actual Average Daily Transaction (ADT) lift percentage across campaigns. Key traffic effectiveness KPI for marketing leadership."
    - name: "avg_actual_comp_sales_lift_pct"
      expr: AVG(CAST(actual_comp_sales_lift_pct AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Average actual comparable sales lift percentage across campaigns. Primary revenue impact KPI used in QBRs and board decks."
    - name: "avg_expected_comp_sales_lift_pct"
      expr: AVG(CAST(expected_comp_sales_lift_pct AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Average expected comparable sales lift percentage at campaign planning. Used as the baseline for lift variance analysis."
    - name: "avg_expected_adt_lift_pct"
      expr: AVG(CAST(expected_adt_lift_pct AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Average expected ADT lift percentage at campaign planning. Paired with actual ADT lift to evaluate forecast accuracy."
    - name: "total_spend_over_budget"
      expr: SUM(CAST(actual_spend AS DOUBLE) - CAST(budget_amount AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Total spend variance (actual minus budget) across campaigns. Positive values indicate overspend; drives budget governance actions."
    - name: "count_active_campaigns"
      expr: COUNT(CASE WHEN campaign_status = 'Active' THEN campaign_id END)
      comment: "Number of currently active campaigns. Operational capacity metric used to manage marketing pipeline and resource load."
    - name: "count_lto_campaigns"
      expr: COUNT(CASE WHEN is_lto = True THEN campaign_id END)
      comment: "Number of Limited Time Offer campaigns. Tracks LTO pipeline depth, a key driver of traffic and trial in QSR."
    - name: "count_non_compliant_campaigns"
      expr: COUNT(CASE WHEN compliance_flag = False THEN campaign_id END)
      comment: "Number of campaigns flagged as non-compliant. Risk metric for brand standards and regulatory exposure."
$$
metric_views.description: Strategic KPI layer over the campaign master table. Tracks campaign investment, performance lift, and budget efficiency to inform marketing spend allocation and campaign effectiveness decisions.

metric_views.sql:
CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW `restaurants_ecm`.`_metrics`.`marketing_campaign_execution`
WITH METRICS
LANGUAGE YAML
AS $$
  version: 1.1
  comment: "Operational and performance KPI layer over campaign execution records. Measures channel-level spend efficiency, lift delivery, and execution quality to guide in-flight campaign optimization."
  source: "`restaurants_ecm`.`marketing`.`campaign_execution`"
  dimensions:
    - name: "execution_channel"
      expr: execution_channel
      comment: "Media or marketing channel through which the campaign was executed (e.g., TV, digital, social). Primary dimension for channel ROI analysis."
    - name: "campaign_execution_status"
      expr: campaign_execution_status
      comment: "Current status of the execution record (e.g., live, completed, paused) for operational monitoring."
    - name: "market_dma"
      expr: market_dma
      comment: "Designated Market Area (DMA) for the execution. Enables geographic performance benchmarking across markets."
    - name: "media_vendor"
      expr: media_vendor
      comment: "Media vendor or agency executing the campaign. Used for vendor performance evaluation and contract negotiations."
    - name: "currency_code"
      expr: currency_code
      comment: "Currency in which spend is denominated. Required for multi-currency normalization in global reporting."
    - name: "target_audience"
      expr: target_audience
      comment: "Audience segment targeted by the execution. Enables audience-level effectiveness analysis."
    - name: "launch_month"
      expr: DATE_TRUNC('MONTH', launch_date)
      comment: "Month the execution launched. Used for time-series trending of campaign execution activity."
    - name: "actual_launch_month"
      expr: DATE_TRUNC('MONTH', actual_launch_date)
      comment: "Month the execution actually launched. Compared against planned launch to measure execution timeliness."
    - name: "creative_version"
      expr: creative_version
      comment: "Version of the creative asset used in the execution. Enables A/B creative performance comparison."
  measures:
    - name: "total_channel_spend"
      expr: SUM(CAST(channel_spend_amount AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Total spend across all campaign executions by channel. Core investment metric for channel budget allocation decisions."
    - name: "avg_channel_spend"
      expr: AVG(CAST(channel_spend_amount AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Average spend per campaign execution record. Benchmarks typical execution investment levels by channel or market."
    - name: "avg_roi_percent"
      expr: AVG(CAST(roi_percent AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Average return on investment percentage across executions. Primary efficiency KPI for marketing investment decisions."
    - name: "avg_cost_per_click"
      expr: AVG(CAST(cost_per_click AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Average cost per click across digital executions. Key digital efficiency metric for media buying optimization."
    - name: "avg_cost_per_impression"
      expr: AVG(CAST(cost_per_impression AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Average cost per impression across executions. Used to evaluate media efficiency and negotiate vendor rates."
    - name: "avg_actual_adt_lift_percent"
      expr: AVG(CAST(actual_adt_lift_percent AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Average actual ADT lift percentage delivered by executions. Measures traffic impact of marketing activity at execution level."
    - name: "avg_actual_comp_sales_lift_percent"
      expr: AVG(CAST(actual_comp_sales_lift_percent AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Average actual comparable sales lift percentage at execution level. Revenue impact KPI for in-flight optimization and post-campaign review."
    - name: "avg_comp_sales_lift_variance"
      expr: AVG(CAST(actual_comp_sales_lift_percent AS DOUBLE) - CAST(expected_comp_sales_lift_percent AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Average variance between actual and expected comp sales lift. Measures forecast accuracy and execution quality; negative values trigger corrective action."
    - name: "avg_adt_lift_variance"
      expr: AVG(CAST(actual_adt_lift_percent AS DOUBLE) - CAST(expected_adt_lift_percent AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Average variance between actual and expected ADT lift. Identifies executions underperforming traffic targets, driving optimization decisions."
    - name: "count_executions_by_channel"
      expr: COUNT(campaign_execution_id)
      comment: "Total number of campaign executions. Used to measure marketing activity volume and channel coverage breadth."
$$
metric_views.description: Operational and performance KPI layer over campaign execution records. Measures channel-level spend efficiency, lift delivery, and execution quality to guide in-flight campaign optimization.

metric_views.sql:
CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW `restaurants_ecm`.`_metrics`.`marketing_coupon`
WITH METRICS
LANGUAGE YAML
AS $$
  version: 1.1
  comment: "KPI layer over the coupon master table. Tracks coupon portfolio depth, discount value, and structural risk (stackability, fraud) to inform coupon strategy and margin protection decisions."
  source: "`restaurants_ecm`.`marketing`.`coupon`"
  dimensions:
    - name: "coupon_type"
      expr: coupon_type
      comment: "Type of coupon (e.g., percentage off, flat discount, free item) for segmenting portfolio by promotional mechanic."
    - name: "coupon_status"
      expr: coupon_status
      comment: "Current status of the coupon (e.g., active, expired, redeemed) for pipeline and lifecycle management."
    - name: "discount_type"
      expr: discount_type
      comment: "Discount mechanism of the coupon (e.g., percentage, fixed amount). Used to analyze discount structure and margin impact."
    - name: "is_exclusive"
      expr: is_exclusive
      comment: "Indicates whether the coupon is exclusive to a specific channel or segment. Measures exclusivity strategy in coupon design."
    - name: "is_stackable"
      expr: is_stackable
      comment: "Indicates whether the coupon can be combined with other offers. Key risk dimension for margin erosion analysis."
    - name: "fraud_prevention_flag"
      expr: fraud_prevention_flag
      comment: "Indicates whether fraud prevention controls are active on the coupon. Used for coupon fraud risk monitoring."
    - name: "eligible_item_category"
      expr: eligible_item_category
      comment: "Menu item category eligible for the coupon. Enables category-level promotional effectiveness analysis."
    - name: "issue_month"
      expr: DATE_TRUNC('MONTH', issue_date)
      comment: "Month the coupon was issued. Used for time-series trending of coupon issuance volume and promotional cadence."
    - name: "expiry_month"
      expr: DATE_TRUNC('MONTH', expiry_date)
      comment: "Month the coupon expires. Used to manage coupon expiry cliffs and redemption urgency patterns."
  measures:
    - name: "total_discount_amount_offered"
      expr: SUM(CAST(discount_amount AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Total discount value offered across all coupons in the portfolio. Core margin exposure metric for coupon program governance."
    - name: "avg_discount_amount_per_coupon"
      expr: AVG(CAST(discount_amount AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Average discount amount per coupon. Benchmarks offer depth and informs coupon value calibration decisions."
    - name: "avg_discount_percentage"
      expr: AVG(CAST(discount_percentage AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Average discount percentage across coupons. Tracks discount depth trends; high averages trigger margin review."
    - name: "count_active_coupons"
      expr: COUNT(CASE WHEN coupon_status = 'Active' THEN coupon_id END)
      comment: "Number of currently active coupons. Measures live promotional offer load and risk of offer fatigue."
    - name: "count_stackable_coupons"
      expr: COUNT(CASE WHEN is_stackable = True THEN coupon_id END)
      comment: "Number of stackable coupons in the portfolio. Margin risk metric; high counts indicate elevated combined-discount exposure."
    - name: "count_fraud_protected_coupons"
      expr: COUNT(CASE WHEN fraud_prevention_flag = True THEN coupon_id END)
      comment: "Number of coupons with active fraud prevention controls. Measures fraud risk coverage across the coupon portfolio."
    - name: "fraud_protection_coverage_rate"
      expr: ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(CASE WHEN fraud_prevention_flag = True THEN coupon_id END) / NULLIF(COUNT(coupon_id), 0), 2)
      comment: "Percentage of coupons with fraud prevention controls active. Strategic risk KPI for coupon program integrity management."
$$
metric_views.description: KPI layer over the coupon master table. Tracks coupon portfolio depth, discount value, and structural risk (stackability, fraud) to inform coupon strategy and margin protection decisions.

metric_views.sql:
CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW `restaurants_ecm`.`_metrics`.`marketing_media_plan`
WITH METRICS
LANGUAGE YAML
AS $$
  version: 1.1
  comment: "Strategic KPI layer over media plans. Tracks planned media investment by channel, reach targets, and plan lifecycle to inform media mix optimization and agency performance management."
  source: "`restaurants_ecm`.`marketing`.`media_plan`"
  dimensions:
    - name: "plan_type"
      expr: plan_type
      comment: "Type of media plan (e.g., national, local, co-op) for segmenting media investment by planning tier."
    - name: "approval_status"
      expr: approval_status
      comment: "Approval status of the media plan (e.g., approved, pending, rejected). Used for governance and pipeline management."
    - name: "lifecycle_status"
      expr: lifecycle_status
      comment: "Lifecycle stage of the media plan (e.g., draft, active, completed). Enables pipeline and execution tracking."
    - name: "agency_name"
      expr: agency_name
      comment: "Name of the media agency managing the plan. Used for agency performance benchmarking and contract management."
    - name: "currency_code"
      expr: currency_code
      comment: "Currency of the media plan spend. Required for multi-currency normalization in global media reporting."
    - name: "target_dma"
      expr: target_dma
      comment: "Designated Market Area targeted by the media plan. Enables geographic media investment analysis."
    - name: "daypart_target"
      expr: daypart_target
      comment: "Daypart targeted by the media plan (e.g., breakfast, dinner). Aligns media investment with traffic-driving daypart strategy."
    - name: "is_active"
      expr: is_active
      comment: "Indicates whether the media plan is currently active. Used to filter live plans for operational monitoring."
    - name: "effective_from_month"
      expr: DATE_TRUNC('MONTH', effective_from)
      comment: "Month the media plan became effective. Used for time-series trending of media investment activation."
  measures:
    - name: "total_planned_spend"
      expr: SUM(CAST(total_planned_spend AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Total planned media spend across all media plans. Primary investment metric for media budget governance and allocation decisions."
    - name: "total_tv_spend"
      expr: SUM(CAST(tv_spend AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Total planned TV spend. Tracks the largest traditional media channel investment for mix optimization."
    - name: "total_digital_spend"
      expr: SUM(CAST(digital_spend AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Total planned digital media spend. Tracks digital channel investment growth, a key strategic priority in QSR marketing."
    - name: "total_social_spend"
      expr: SUM(CAST(social_spend AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Total planned social media spend. Measures investment in social channels, critical for brand engagement and younger demographics."
    - name: "total_ooh_spend"
      expr: SUM(CAST(ooh_spend AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Total planned out-of-home (OOH) spend. Tracks investment in proximity-based media, important for drive-to-store campaigns."
    - name: "total_radio_spend"
      expr: SUM(CAST(radio_spend AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Total planned radio spend. Tracks investment in audio channels for daypart and commuter audience targeting."
    - name: "total_print_spend"
      expr: SUM(CAST(print_spend AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Total planned print spend. Monitors declining traditional channel investment as part of media mix shift analysis."
    - name: "total_reach_target"
      expr: SUM(CAST(reach_target AS BIGINT))
      comment: "Total planned audience reach across media plans. Measures the scale of marketing exposure targeted, a key brand awareness input."
    - name: "avg_reach_target_per_plan"
      expr: AVG(CAST(reach_target AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Average planned reach per media plan. Benchmarks the scale of individual media plans for investment efficiency comparison."
    - name: "digital_spend_share"
      expr: ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CAST(digital_spend AS DOUBLE)) / NULLIF(SUM(CAST(total_planned_spend AS DOUBLE)), 0), 2)
      comment: "Digital spend as a percentage of total planned media spend. Strategic KPI for tracking digital transformation of the media mix."
    - name: "count_approved_plans"
      expr: COUNT(CASE WHEN approval_status = 'Approved' THEN media_plan_id END)
      comment: "Number of approved media plans. Governance metric for tracking plan approval pipeline and readiness for execution."
$$
metric_views.description: Strategic KPI layer over media plans. Tracks planned media investment by channel, reach targets, and plan lifecycle to inform media mix optimization and agency performance management.

metric_views.sql:
CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW `restaurants_ecm`.`_metrics`.`marketing_promotion`
WITH METRICS
LANGUAGE YAML
AS $$
  version: 1.1
  comment: "Strategic KPI layer over the promotion master table. Tracks promotion investment, discount depth, and redemption pipeline to inform promotional strategy and margin management."
  source: "`restaurants_ecm`.`marketing`.`promotion`"
  dimensions:
    - name: "promotion_type"
      expr: promotion_type
      comment: "Type of promotion (e.g., BOGO, discount, bundle) for segmenting performance by promotional mechanic."
    - name: "promotion_status"
      expr: promotion_status
      comment: "Current lifecycle status of the promotion (e.g., active, expired, draft) for pipeline and performance monitoring."
    - name: "promo_category"
      expr: promo_category
      comment: "Business category of the promotion (e.g., LTO, value, loyalty) for strategic portfolio analysis."
    - name: "applicable_channels"
      expr: applicable_channels
      comment: "Channels where the promotion is valid (e.g., in-store, digital, drive-thru). Enables channel-level promotional effectiveness analysis."
    - name: "is_exclusive"
      expr: is_exclusive
      comment: "Indicates whether the promotion is exclusive (e.g., loyalty-only). Used to measure exclusivity impact on redemption and margin."
    - name: "is_stackable"
      expr: is_stackable
      comment: "Indicates whether the promotion can be stacked with other offers. Relevant for margin risk assessment."
    - name: "promo_source"
      expr: promo_source
      comment: "Origin of the promotion (e.g., national, local, franchisee). Enables governance and performance comparison by promotion source."
    - name: "start_month"
      expr: DATE_TRUNC('MONTH', start_date)
      comment: "Month the promotion started. Used for time-series trending of promotional activity and seasonality analysis."
    - name: "end_month"
      expr: DATE_TRUNC('MONTH', end_date)
      comment: "Month the promotion ended. Used to analyze promotional duration and expiry patterns."
  measures:
    - name: "total_discount_amount"
      expr: SUM(CAST(discount_amount AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Total discount value offered across promotions. Core margin impact metric for promotional investment governance."
    - name: "avg_discount_amount"
      expr: AVG(CAST(discount_amount AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Average discount amount per promotion. Benchmarks discount depth and informs value proposition calibration."
    - name: "avg_discount_percentage"
      expr: AVG(CAST(discount_percentage AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Average discount percentage across promotions. Key margin management metric; high values trigger pricing strategy review."
    - name: "avg_minimum_purchase_amount"
      expr: AVG(CAST(minimum_purchase_amount AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Average minimum purchase threshold required to redeem a promotion. Measures basket-building effectiveness of promotional design."
    - name: "total_minimum_purchase_amount"
      expr: SUM(CAST(minimum_purchase_amount AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Sum of minimum purchase thresholds across promotions. Proxy for total basket-lift potential embedded in the promotional portfolio."
    - name: "count_active_promotions"
      expr: COUNT(CASE WHEN promotion_status = 'Active' THEN promotion_id END)
      comment: "Number of currently active promotions. Operational metric for managing promotional load and avoiding offer fatigue."
    - name: "count_exclusive_promotions"
      expr: COUNT(CASE WHEN is_exclusive = True THEN promotion_id END)
      comment: "Number of exclusive promotions (e.g., loyalty-only). Tracks exclusivity strategy depth in the promotional portfolio."
    - name: "count_stackable_promotions"
      expr: COUNT(CASE WHEN is_stackable = True THEN promotion_id END)
      comment: "Number of stackable promotions. Risk metric for margin erosion from combined offer redemptions."
$$
metric_views.description: Strategic KPI layer over the promotion master table. Tracks promotion investment, discount depth, and redemption pipeline to inform promotional strategy and margin management.

metric_views.sql:
CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW `restaurants_ecm`.`_metrics`.`marketing_promotion_redemption`
WITH METRICS
LANGUAGE YAML
AS $$
  version: 1.1
  comment: "High-value KPI layer over promotion redemption events. Measures redemption volume, discount delivered, loyalty engagement, and channel mix to drive promotional effectiveness and guest engagement decisions."
  source: "`restaurants_ecm`.`marketing`.`promotion_redemption`"
  filter: is_test_redemption = False
  dimensions:
    - name: "discount_type"
      expr: discount_type
      comment: "Type of discount applied at redemption (e.g., percentage, flat amount, BOGO). Enables mechanic-level performance analysis."
    - name: "daypart"
      expr: daypart
      comment: "Daypart during which the redemption occurred (e.g., breakfast, lunch, dinner). Identifies peak promotional engagement windows."
    - name: "currency_code"
      expr: currency_code
      comment: "Currency of the redemption transaction. Required for multi-currency normalization in global reporting."
    - name: "promotion_redemption_status"
      expr: promotion_redemption_status
      comment: "Status of the redemption event (e.g., successful, voided, failed). Used to filter valid redemptions and track failure rates."
    - name: "loyalty_member_flag"
      expr: loyalty_member_flag
      comment: "Indicates whether the redemption was made by a loyalty program member. Key dimension for loyalty vs. non-loyalty promotional ROI comparison."
    - name: "source_system"
      expr: source_system
      comment: "Source system that recorded the redemption (e.g., POS, mobile app, kiosk). Used for channel attribution and data quality monitoring."
    - name: "redemption_month"
      expr: DATE_TRUNC('MONTH', redemption_timestamp)
      comment: "Month of the redemption event. Primary time dimension for redemption trend analysis and seasonality reporting."
    - name: "device_code"
      expr: device_code
      comment: "Device type used for the redemption (e.g., mobile, kiosk, POS terminal). Informs digital channel investment decisions."
  measures:
    - name: "total_discount_amount_redeemed"
      expr: SUM(CAST(discount_amount AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Total discount value redeemed across all promotion redemption events. Primary margin impact metric for promotional spend governance."
    - name: "avg_discount_amount_per_redemption"
      expr: AVG(CAST(discount_amount AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Average discount amount per redemption event. Measures per-transaction promotional cost; used to calibrate offer depth."
    - name: "avg_discount_percent_redeemed"
      expr: AVG(CAST(discount_percent AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Average discount percentage applied at redemption. Tracks actual discount depth delivered vs. planned, informing margin management."
    - name: "total_redemption_events"
      expr: COUNT(promotion_redemption_id)
      comment: "Total number of valid promotion redemption events. Baseline volume metric for promotional reach and engagement measurement."
    - name: "count_distinct_guests_redeeming"
      expr: COUNT(DISTINCT primary_promotion_guest_profile_id)
      comment: "Number of unique guests who redeemed a promotion. Measures promotional reach breadth and guest engagement depth."
    - name: "count_distinct_loyalty_redemptions"
      expr: COUNT(CASE WHEN loyalty_member_flag = True THEN promotion_redemption_id END)
      comment: "Number of redemptions by loyalty program members. Measures loyalty program engagement with promotional offers; key loyalty ROI input."
    - name: "count_distinct_restaurants_with_redemptions"
      expr: COUNT(DISTINCT primary_promotion_restaurant_unit_id)
      comment: "Number of unique restaurant units with at least one redemption. Measures promotional geographic and unit-level penetration."
    - name: "loyalty_redemption_rate"
      expr: ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(CASE WHEN loyalty_member_flag = True THEN promotion_redemption_id END) / NULLIF(COUNT(promotion_redemption_id), 0), 2)
      comment: "Percentage of redemptions made by loyalty members. Strategic KPI for loyalty program effectiveness and promotional targeting quality."
$$
metric_views.description: High-value KPI layer over promotion redemption events. Measures redemption volume, discount delivered, loyalty engagement, and channel mix to drive promotional effectiveness and guest engagement decisions.

metric_views.sql:
CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW `restaurants_ecm`.`_metrics`.`menu_combo_meal`
WITH METRICS
LANGUAGE YAML
AS $$
  version: 1.1
  comment: "Combo meal portfolio metrics tracking bundle pricing, discount depth, food cost, and availability across channels, dayparts, and restaurant formats. Supports combo strategy, bundle pricing optimization, and promotional planning decisions."
  source: "`restaurants_ecm`.`menu`.`combo_meal`"
  dimensions:
    - name: "launch_date"
      expr: launch_date
      comment: "Combo meal launch date for pipeline and lifecycle analysis."
    - name: "effective_start_date"
      expr: effective_start_date
      comment: "Start date of the combo meal's active pricing window."
    - name: "combo_type"
      expr: combo_type
      comment: "Type of combo (e.g., value, premium, kids) for portfolio segmentation and pricing strategy analysis."
    - name: "combo_status"
      expr: combo_status
      comment: "Lifecycle status of the combo meal (e.g., active, discontinued, test) for portfolio governance."
    - name: "daypart"
      expr: daypart
      comment: "Target daypart for the combo for daypart-level bundle performance analysis."
    - name: "restaurant_format"
      expr: restaurant_format
      comment: "Restaurant format for format-level combo performance benchmarking."
    - name: "menu_engineering_class"
      expr: menu_engineering_class
      comment: "Menu engineering classification for combo profitability tier analysis."
    - name: "is_national_launch"
      expr: is_national_launch
      comment: "Flag for national vs. regional combo launches for scale and investment analysis."
    - name: "is_customizable"
      expr: is_customizable
      comment: "Flag for customizable combos for operational complexity and upsell opportunity analysis."
    - name: "country_code"
      expr: country_code
      comment: "Country for international combo portfolio management."
    - name: "currency_code"
      expr: currency_code
      comment: "Currency of combo pricing amounts for multi-currency normalization."
  measures:
    - name: "total_combo_count"
      expr: COUNT(1)
      comment: "Total number of combo meal records. Tracks combo portfolio size and complexity."
    - name: "avg_bundle_price"
      expr: AVG(CAST(bundle_price AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Average combo bundle price. Core pricing KPI for combo value positioning and competitive benchmarking."
    - name: "avg_individual_items_price_sum"
      expr: AVG(CAST(individual_items_price_sum AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Average sum of individual item prices if purchased separately. Used to compute bundle savings and value proposition."
    - name: "avg_bundle_discount_amount"
      expr: AVG(CAST(bundle_discount_amount AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Average discount provided by purchasing the combo vs. individual items. Measures bundle value proposition and promotional investment."
    - name: "avg_food_cost_pct"
      expr: AVG(CAST(food_cost_pct AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Average food cost percentage for combo meals. Ensures combo pricing maintains acceptable margin thresholds."
    - name: "avg_item_cost"
      expr: AVG(CAST(item_cost AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Average total item cost for combo meals. Used to validate combo pricing against cost structure."
    - name: "avg_pmix_target_pct"
      expr: AVG(CAST(pmix_target_pct AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Average product mix target percentage for combo meals. Informs marketing investment and operational focus for combo promotion."
    - name: "bundle_discount_rate"
      expr: ROUND(100.0 * AVG(CAST(bundle_discount_amount AS DOUBLE)) / NULLIF(AVG(CAST(individual_items_price_sum AS DOUBLE)), 0), 2)
      comment: "Average bundle discount as a percentage of individual item price sum. Measures combo value depth and its impact on revenue per transaction."
    - name: "implied_combo_margin_pct"
      expr: ROUND(100.0 * (AVG(CAST(bundle_price AS DOUBLE)) - AVG(CAST(item_cost AS DOUBLE))) / NULLIF(AVG(CAST(bundle_price AS DOUBLE)), 0), 2)
      comment: "Implied gross margin percentage for combo meals. Composite profitability KPI for combo portfolio management and pricing strategy."
    - name: "digital_channel_availability_rate"
      expr: ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(CASE WHEN is_olo_available = TRUE THEN 1 END) / NULLIF(COUNT(1), 0), 2)
      comment: "Percentage of combo meals available on OLO (online ordering). Digital channel coverage KPI for e-commerce revenue optimization."
    - name: "third_party_delivery_availability_rate"
      expr: ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(CASE WHEN is_3pd_available = TRUE THEN 1 END) / NULLIF(COUNT(1), 0), 2)
      comment: "Percentage of combo meals available on third-party delivery platforms. Delivery channel coverage KPI for off-premise revenue strategy."
$$
metric_views.description: Combo meal portfolio metrics tracking bundle pricing, discount depth, food cost, and availability across channels, dayparts, and restaurant formats. Supports combo strategy, bundle pricing optimization, and promotional planning decisions.

metric_views.sql:
CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW `restaurants_ecm`.`_metrics`.`menu_item_86_event`
WITH METRICS
LANGUAGE YAML
AS $$
  version: 1.1
  comment: "Item 86 (out-of-stock/unavailability) event metrics tracking frequency, duration, food safety linkage, and operational impact of menu item unavailability events. Drives inventory reliability, guest experience, and operational risk management decisions."
  source: "`restaurants_ecm`.`menu`.`item_86_event`"
  dimensions:
    - name: "start_timestamp"
      expr: start_timestamp
      comment: "Timestamp when the 86 event began, used for time-series analysis of unavailability patterns."
    - name: "daypart_affected"
      expr: daypart_affected
      comment: "Daypart during which the item was unavailable, identifying peak-period availability failures."
    - name: "reason_code"
      expr: reason_code
      comment: "Root cause code for the 86 event (e.g., out of stock, equipment failure, recall) for cause-based operational analysis."
    - name: "event_status"
      expr: event_status
      comment: "Current status of the 86 event (e.g., open, resolved, escalated) for active issue management."
    - name: "restaurant_format"
      expr: restaurant_format
      comment: "Restaurant format for format-level availability reliability benchmarking."
    - name: "ownership_model"
      expr: ownership_model
      comment: "Franchise vs. company-owned for ownership-model availability performance comparison."
    - name: "is_food_safety_related"
      expr: is_food_safety_related
      comment: "Flag for food safety-driven 86 events, enabling separation of safety-critical unavailability from operational stockouts."
    - name: "is_recall_related"
      expr: is_recall_related
      comment: "Flag for recall-driven 86 events. Recall-related events require immediate escalation and regulatory reporting."
    - name: "is_lto_item"
      expr: is_lto_item
      comment: "Flag for LTO items being 86'd, which has amplified guest impact due to promotional expectations."
    - name: "olo_suppressed"
      expr: olo_suppressed
      comment: "Flag indicating the item was suppressed on the OLO (online ordering) platform during the event."
    - name: "pos_suppressed"
      expr: pos_suppressed
      comment: "Flag indicating the item was suppressed on the POS during the event."
  measures:
    - name: "total_86_events"
      expr: COUNT(1)
      comment: "Total number of item 86 events. Primary availability reliability KPI; high event counts signal systemic supply or operational failures."
    - name: "distinct_items_86d"
      expr: COUNT(DISTINCT primary_menu_item_id)
      comment: "Count of distinct menu items that experienced at least one 86 event. Measures breadth of menu availability risk across the portfolio."
    - name: "distinct_units_with_86_events"
      expr: COUNT(DISTINCT unit_id)
      comment: "Count of distinct restaurant units with 86 events. Identifies units with systemic availability problems requiring targeted intervention."
    - name: "food_safety_86_event_count"
      expr: COUNT(CASE WHEN is_food_safety_related = TRUE THEN 1 END)
      comment: "Count of 86 events driven by food safety concerns. Critical risk KPI; any food safety-related unavailability requires immediate investigation and reporting."
    - name: "recall_related_86_event_count"
      expr: COUNT(CASE WHEN is_recall_related = TRUE THEN 1 END)
      comment: "Count of 86 events triggered by ingredient or product recalls. Regulatory and brand risk KPI requiring executive visibility."
    - name: "avg_event_duration_minutes"
      expr: AVG(CAST(duration_minutes AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Average duration of 86 events in minutes. Longer durations indicate slower resolution and greater guest impact and lost sales exposure."
    - name: "guest_notification_rate"
      expr: ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(CASE WHEN guest_notification_sent = TRUE THEN 1 END) / NULLIF(COUNT(1), 0), 2)
      comment: "Percentage of 86 events where guests were proactively notified. Guest experience KPI; low notification rates during high-impact events damage brand trust."
    - name: "escalation_rate"
      expr: ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(CASE WHEN escalated_to_team IS NOT NULL THEN 1 END) / NULLIF(COUNT(1), 0), 2)
      comment: "Percentage of 86 events that were escalated to a support team. Measures severity distribution and operational response capability."
    - name: "olo_suppression_rate"
      expr: ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(CASE WHEN olo_suppressed = TRUE THEN 1 END) / NULLIF(COUNT(1), 0), 2)
      comment: "Percentage of 86 events where the item was suppressed on OLO. Tracks digital channel availability management during stockout events."
    - name: "food_safety_86_rate"
      expr: ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(CASE WHEN is_food_safety_related = TRUE THEN 1 END) / NULLIF(COUNT(1), 0), 2)
      comment: "Percentage of all 86 events that are food safety-related. Composite risk KPI; elevated rates signal systemic food safety supply chain vulnerabilities."
$$
metric_views.description: Item 86 (out-of-stock/unavailability) event metrics tracking frequency, duration, food safety linkage, and operational impact of menu item unavailability events. Drives inventory reliability, guest experience, and operational risk management decisions.

metric_views.sql:
CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW `restaurants_ecm`.`_metrics`.`menu_item_cost`
WITH METRICS
LANGUAGE YAML
AS $$
  version: 1.1
  comment: "Menu item cost and margin metrics tracking theoretical vs. actual food cost, waste, yield, and cost variance by item, channel, daypart, and restaurant format. Drives food cost management, recipe optimization, and pricing strategy."
  source: "`restaurants_ecm`.`menu`.`item_cost`"
  dimensions:
    - name: "cost_calculation_date"
      expr: cost_calculation_date
      comment: "Date the cost was calculated, enabling time-series tracking of cost trends and inflation impact."
    - name: "effective_start_date"
      expr: effective_start_date
      comment: "Start date of the cost record's validity window for point-in-time cost analysis."
    - name: "channel"
      expr: channel
      comment: "Sales channel (e.g., dine-in, drive-thru, delivery) for channel-specific cost structure analysis."
    - name: "daypart"
      expr: daypart
      comment: "Meal period for daypart-level cost and margin analysis."
    - name: "restaurant_format"
      expr: restaurant_format
      comment: "Restaurant format for format-level food cost benchmarking."
    - name: "menu_engineering_class"
      expr: menu_engineering_class
      comment: "Menu engineering classification for cost analysis by item profitability tier."
    - name: "cost_calculation_method"
      expr: cost_calculation_method
      comment: "Method used to calculate cost (e.g., standard, actual, theoretical) for methodology-level cost comparison."
    - name: "cost_status"
      expr: cost_status
      comment: "Approval/lifecycle status of the cost record (e.g., approved, pending, superseded)."
    - name: "is_lto"
      expr: is_lto
      comment: "Flag for Limited Time Offer items to compare LTO vs. core menu cost structures."
    - name: "currency_code"
      expr: currency_code
      comment: "Currency of cost amounts for multi-currency normalization."
  measures:
    - name: "avg_theoretical_cogs_pct"
      expr: AVG(CAST(theoretical_cogs_pct AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Average theoretical (recipe-based) cost of goods sold percentage. Baseline food cost target used in menu pricing and engineering."
    - name: "avg_actual_cogs_pct"
      expr: AVG(CAST(actual_cogs_pct AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Average actual realized COGS percentage. Compared against theoretical to identify waste, theft, or portioning issues."
    - name: "avg_target_cogs_pct"
      expr: AVG(CAST(target_cogs_pct AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Average target COGS percentage set by finance/operations. Benchmark for evaluating actual vs. planned food cost performance."
    - name: "avg_cogs_pct_variance"
      expr: AVG(CAST(cogs_pct_variance AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Average variance between actual and target COGS percentage. Negative variance signals over-cost items requiring corrective action."
    - name: "total_theoretical_cost_amount"
      expr: SUM(CAST(theoretical_cost_amount AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Total theoretical cost amount across all item cost records. Used to benchmark actual spend against recipe-based expectations."
    - name: "total_theoretical_cost_variance"
      expr: SUM(CAST(theoretical_cost_variance_amount AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Total variance between theoretical and actual cost amounts. Quantifies the financial impact of food cost inefficiencies."
    - name: "total_packaging_cost"
      expr: SUM(CAST(packaging_cost AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Total packaging cost across menu items. Tracks non-food COGS component for total cost-of-sale analysis."
    - name: "total_primary_protein_cost"
      expr: SUM(CAST(primary_protein_cost AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Total primary protein cost — the largest and most volatile food cost component. Critical for commodity risk and menu pricing decisions."
    - name: "avg_base_selling_price"
      expr: AVG(CAST(base_selling_price AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Average base selling price across item cost records. Used to compute implied margin and validate pricing vs. cost structure."
    - name: "avg_waste_pct"
      expr: AVG(CAST(waste_pct AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Average waste percentage across menu items. Elevated waste drives food cost overruns and sustainability concerns."
    - name: "avg_yield_pct"
      expr: AVG(CAST(yield_pct AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Average yield percentage (usable output per unit of input). Low yield signals prep inefficiency or ingredient quality issues."
    - name: "avg_cost_per_gram"
      expr: AVG(CAST(cost_per_gram AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Average cost per gram of menu item. Enables normalized cost comparison across items of different portion sizes."
    - name: "avg_portion_size_grams"
      expr: AVG(CAST(portion_size_grams AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Average portion size in grams. Tracks portion consistency and its impact on food cost and guest value perception."
    - name: "theoretical_vs_actual_cogs_gap"
      expr: ROUND(AVG(CAST(actual_cogs_pct AS DOUBLE)) - AVG(CAST(theoretical_cogs_pct AS DOUBLE)), 2)
      comment: "Gap between average actual and theoretical COGS percentage. Positive gap indicates systemic over-cost vs. recipe standard — a key operational efficiency KPI."
    - name: "distinct_items_costed"
      expr: COUNT(DISTINCT menu_item_id)
      comment: "Count of distinct menu items with active cost records. Measures cost governance coverage across the menu portfolio."
$$
metric_views.description: Menu item cost and margin metrics tracking theoretical vs. actual food cost, waste, yield, and cost variance by item, channel, daypart, and restaurant format. Drives food cost management, recipe optimization, and pricing strategy.

metric_views.sql:
CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW `restaurants_ecm`.`_metrics`.`menu_item_price`
WITH METRICS
LANGUAGE YAML
AS $$
  version: 1.1
  comment: "Menu item pricing metrics tracking base price, promotional pricing, channel surcharges, franchise price deviation, and price elasticity positioning. Supports pricing strategy, franchise compliance, and revenue optimization decisions."
  source: "`restaurants_ecm`.`menu`.`item_price`"
  dimensions:
    - name: "effective_start_date"
      expr: effective_start_date
      comment: "Price effective start date for point-in-time and trend pricing analysis."
    - name: "effective_end_date"
      expr: effective_end_date
      comment: "Price effective end date for identifying expired or expiring price records."
    - name: "daypart"
      expr: daypart
      comment: "Meal period for daypart-specific pricing analysis (e.g., breakfast value pricing vs. dinner premium)."
    - name: "ordering_channel"
      expr: ordering_channel
      comment: "Channel through which the price applies (e.g., in-store, OLO, 3PD) for channel surcharge and pricing strategy analysis."
    - name: "restaurant_format"
      expr: restaurant_format
      comment: "Restaurant format for format-level price benchmarking and consistency audits."
    - name: "ownership_type"
      expr: ownership_type
      comment: "Franchise vs. company-owned for franchise price compliance monitoring."
    - name: "menu_engineering_category"
      expr: menu_engineering_category
      comment: "Menu engineering tier for price-by-profitability-class analysis."
    - name: "price_elasticity_band"
      expr: price_elasticity_band
      comment: "Price elasticity classification (e.g., elastic, inelastic) for demand-sensitive pricing decisions."
    - name: "is_lto"
      expr: is_lto
      comment: "Flag for Limited Time Offer pricing to compare LTO vs. core menu price structures."
    - name: "is_active"
      expr: is_active
      comment: "Active price record flag for filtering to currently effective prices."
    - name: "country_code"
      expr: country_code
      comment: "Country for international price benchmarking and local market pricing analysis."
    - name: "currency_code"
      expr: currency_code
      comment: "Currency of price amounts for multi-currency normalization."
  measures:
    - name: "avg_base_price"
      expr: AVG(CAST(base_price AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Average base menu price across items and markets. Core pricing KPI for competitive benchmarking and price architecture reviews."
    - name: "avg_suggested_retail_price"
      expr: AVG(CAST(suggested_retail_price AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Average suggested retail price. Compared against base price to measure price realization and franchisee compliance."
    - name: "avg_promotional_price"
      expr: AVG(CAST(promotional_price AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Average promotional price across active promotions. Tracks depth of promotional discounting and its impact on revenue."
    - name: "avg_channel_surcharge"
      expr: AVG(CAST(channel_surcharge AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Average channel surcharge applied (e.g., delivery upcharge). Monitors channel economics and guest price perception."
    - name: "avg_cogs_pct"
      expr: AVG(CAST(cogs_pct AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Average COGS percentage at the price record level. Validates that pricing maintains target food cost margins."
    - name: "avg_cost_of_goods"
      expr: AVG(CAST(cost_of_goods AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Average cost of goods per item price record. Used to compute implied gross margin at the item-price level."
    - name: "avg_franchise_price_deviation_pct"
      expr: AVG(CAST(franchise_price_deviation_pct AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Average percentage deviation of franchise prices from the suggested retail price. Franchise compliance KPI; large deviations trigger brand pricing audits."
    - name: "avg_price_override_limit"
      expr: AVG(CAST(price_override_limit AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Average maximum price override limit granted. Tracks pricing flexibility governance across franchise and company-owned units."
    - name: "distinct_items_priced"
      expr: COUNT(DISTINCT menu_item_id)
      comment: "Count of distinct menu items with active price records. Measures pricing governance coverage across the menu portfolio."
    - name: "distinct_units_with_prices"
      expr: COUNT(DISTINCT unit_id)
      comment: "Count of distinct restaurant units with price records. Used for pricing rollout completeness and compliance audits."
    - name: "promo_vs_base_price_discount_rate"
      expr: ROUND(100.0 * (AVG(CAST(base_price AS DOUBLE)) - AVG(CAST(promotional_price AS DOUBLE))) / NULLIF(AVG(CAST(base_price AS DOUBLE)), 0), 2)
      comment: "Average promotional discount depth as a percentage of base price. Measures promotional intensity and its revenue dilution impact."
    - name: "srp_vs_base_price_gap"
      expr: ROUND(AVG(CAST(suggested_retail_price AS DOUBLE)) - AVG(CAST(base_price AS DOUBLE)), 2)
      comment: "Average gap between suggested retail price and base price. Positive gap indicates upward pricing headroom; negative signals pricing below recommendation."
$$
metric_views.description: Menu item pricing metrics tracking base price, promotional pricing, channel surcharges, franchise price deviation, and price elasticity positioning. Supports pricing strategy, franchise compliance, and revenue optimization decisions.

metric_views.sql:
CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW `restaurants_ecm`.`_metrics`.`menu_lto`
WITH METRICS
LANGUAGE YAML
AS $$
  version: 1.1
  comment: "Limited Time Offer (LTO) lifecycle and performance metrics tracking launch timing, food cost targets, rollout scope, approval status, and market coverage. Supports LTO pipeline management, innovation ROI, and promotional strategy decisions."
  source: "`restaurants_ecm`.`menu`.`menu_lto`"
  dimensions:
    - name: "planned_launch_date"
      expr: planned_launch_date
      comment: "Planned launch date for LTO pipeline scheduling and lead-time analysis."
    - name: "actual_launch_date"
      expr: actual_launch_date
      comment: "Actual launch date for on-time launch performance tracking."
    - name: "lifecycle_status"
      expr: lifecycle_status
      comment: "Current lifecycle stage of the LTO (e.g., concept, approved, active, retired) for pipeline stage-gate reporting."
    - name: "approval_status"
      expr: approval_status
      comment: "Approval status of the LTO for governance and compliance tracking."
    - name: "lto_type"
      expr: lto_type
      comment: "Type of LTO (e.g., seasonal, promotional, test) for portfolio mix analysis."
    - name: "rollout_scope"
      expr: rollout_scope
      comment: "Geographic or unit scope of the LTO rollout (e.g., national, regional, test market)."
    - name: "daypart"
      expr: daypart
      comment: "Target daypart for the LTO for daypart innovation portfolio analysis."
    - name: "restaurant_format"
      expr: restaurant_format
      comment: "Restaurant format applicability for format-level LTO performance comparison."
    - name: "is_national_launch"
      expr: is_national_launch
      comment: "Flag for national vs. regional/test LTO launches for scale and investment analysis."
    - name: "is_returning_item"
      expr: is_returning_item
      comment: "Flag for returning LTO items vs. new innovations for portfolio novelty tracking."
    - name: "season_or_occasion"
      expr: season_or_occasion
      comment: "Season or occasion driving the LTO (e.g., summer, holiday) for seasonal portfolio planning."
    - name: "country_code"
      expr: country_code
      comment: "Country of LTO deployment for international innovation portfolio management."
  measures:
    - name: "total_lto_count"
      expr: COUNT(1)
      comment: "Total number of LTO records. Tracks innovation pipeline volume and cadence of new item introductions."
    - name: "avg_target_food_cost_pct"
      expr: AVG(CAST(target_food_cost_pct AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Average target food cost percentage for LTO items. Ensures new items are designed within acceptable margin thresholds before launch."
    - name: "avg_suggested_retail_price"
      expr: AVG(CAST(suggested_retail_price AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Average suggested retail price for LTO items. Tracks LTO price positioning relative to core menu and competitive set."
    - name: "avg_pmix_target_pct"
      expr: AVG(CAST(pmix_target_pct AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Average product mix target percentage for LTO items. Measures ambition of LTO volume targets and informs marketing investment sizing."
    - name: "avg_launch_lead_time_days"
      expr: AVG(CAST(launch_lead_time_days AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Average lead time in days from concept to launch. Operational efficiency KPI for the LTO development pipeline; long lead times signal process bottlenecks."
    - name: "avg_planned_duration_days"
      expr: AVG(CAST(planned_duration_days AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Average planned duration in days for LTO items. Informs promotional calendar planning and supply chain commitment windows."
    - name: "launch_on_time_rate"
      expr: ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(CASE WHEN actual_launch_date <= planned_launch_date THEN 1 END) / NULLIF(COUNT(CASE WHEN actual_launch_date IS NOT NULL AND planned_launch_date IS NOT NULL THEN 1 END), 0), 2)
      comment: "Percentage of LTOs launched on or before the planned launch date. Operational excellence KPI for the innovation pipeline; low rates indicate systemic execution gaps."
    - name: "food_safety_approval_rate"
      expr: ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(CASE WHEN food_safety_approved = TRUE THEN 1 END) / NULLIF(COUNT(1), 0), 2)
      comment: "Percentage of LTO items with food safety approval. Compliance KPI; any gap below 100% for active LTOs is a critical risk requiring immediate escalation."
    - name: "nutritional_approval_rate"
      expr: ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(CASE WHEN nutritional_approved = TRUE THEN 1 END) / NULLIF(COUNT(1), 0), 2)
      comment: "Percentage of LTO items with nutritional approval. Regulatory compliance KPI for menu labeling requirements."
    - name: "allergen_review_rate"
      expr: ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(CASE WHEN allergen_reviewed = TRUE THEN 1 END) / NULLIF(COUNT(1), 0), 2)
      comment: "Percentage of LTO items with completed allergen review. Guest safety compliance KPI; gaps expose the brand to allergen incident liability."
    - name: "national_launch_pct"
      expr: ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(CASE WHEN is_national_launch = TRUE THEN 1 END) / NULLIF(COUNT(1), 0), 2)
      comment: "Percentage of LTOs designated as national launches. Measures scale of innovation investment and national vs. local promotional strategy balance."
    - name: "returning_item_pct"
      expr: ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(CASE WHEN is_returning_item = TRUE THEN 1 END) / NULLIF(COUNT(1), 0), 2)
      comment: "Percentage of LTOs that are returning (previously launched) items. High rates indicate reliance on proven items vs. true innovation investment."
$$
metric_views.description: Limited Time Offer (LTO) lifecycle and performance metrics tracking launch timing, food cost targets, rollout scope, approval status, and market coverage. Supports LTO pipeline management, innovation ROI, and promotional strategy decisions.

metric_views.sql:
CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW `restaurants_ecm`.`_metrics`.`menu_nutrition_profile`
WITH METRICS
LANGUAGE YAML
AS $$
  version: 1.1
  comment: "Nutritional profile metrics tracking caloric content, macronutrient composition, sodium levels, and regulatory approval status across the menu item portfolio. Supports health and wellness strategy, regulatory compliance, and menu labeling decisions."
  source: "`restaurants_ecm`.`menu`.`nutrition_profile`"
  dimensions:
    - name: "effective_date"
      expr: effective_date
      comment: "Date the nutrition profile became effective for point-in-time nutritional analysis."
    - name: "profile_type"
      expr: profile_type
      comment: "Type of nutrition profile (e.g., standard, as-served, as-prepared) for methodology-level analysis."
    - name: "approval_status"
